subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is the Fundamental Law of any country which sets out the framework and the principal functions of various organs of the government? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Constitution
Set of written rules accepted by all. India has the largest written constitution. It currently contains 12 schedules and 25 parts. Articles related to ordinance are 123 (President) and 213 (Governor).

subject: Polity
question_text: On which of the following dates does India celebrate its Constitution Day? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(C) 26th November
Adopted on 26th November, 1949. Enforced on 26th January, 1950. Certain provisions that were enforced on 26 november, 1949 were- Citizenship, Elections and Provincial Parliament.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who wrote the original handwritten version of the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 26/02/2024]
solution:

It is handwritten by Prem Behari Narain Raizada in English and Vasant Kumar Vaidya in Hindi. Decorated by - Nand Lal Bose and Beohar Ram Manohar Sinha, symbol - Elephant, BN Rau - Constitutional Advisor, BR Ambedkar - Chairman of Drafting Committee

subject: Polity
question_text: When was the first Constituent Assembly election held in India? [SSC CHSL 13/10/2020]
solution:

Elections were held in July-August 1946. Total seats - 389 [292 for provinces election and 93 nominated as they were princely states & 4 for chief commission provinces].

subject: Polity

(1) Rajendra Prasad- Rules of procedure committee, Steering committee (2) Jawaharlal Nehru - Union Power Committee, Union Constitution Committee, States Committee (3) Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel - Provincial Constitution Committee, Advisory Committee on FRs, Minorities & Tribal & Excluded Areas.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following is called 'father of Indian constitution'? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following personalities was the chief draftsman of the constituent assembly that drafted the document of the Constitution? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:

SN Mukherjee was the chief draftsman. KM Munshi was a member of the drafting committee and also headed the order of business committee.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution of free India must be framed, without outside interference by a constituent assembly elected on the basis of adult franchise'. Who made this statement? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023]
solution:

Jawaharlal Nehru headed the Union Constitution Committee, States Committee and Union Powers Committee. M.N. Roy founded the Communist Party of India (CPI) in 1920. He is credited as the first person to propose a Constituent Assembly elected by universal adult franchise in 1934.

subject: Polity

solution:
(C) 1928
Other notable members were Tej Bahadur Sapru, Ali Imam, Subhash Chandra Bose. Some recommendations were: 1) Dominion status, 2) Nineteen fundamental rights, 3) Rejection of separate electorates

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a political philosophy of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]

options.a: Sensitive for the historically disadvantaged groups
options.c: Sensitive to the needs of religious and linguistic minorities
options.d: Non-committal to building a common national identity

solution:
(D) Non-committal to building a common national identity.
The characteristics of Indian Polity are separation of power, checks and balances, parliamentary system and a limited government.

subject: Polity
question_text: GV Mavalankar was the Chairman of the ______ of the Constituent Assembly of India. [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]

options.d: Ad hoc Committee on the National Flag

Constituent Assembly as law making body:- President : GV Mavalankar (First speaker of Lok Sabha), Ad hoc Committee on the National Flag - Rajendra Prasad

question_text: Who among the following was NOT a minister of Cabinet Mission Plan, 1946? [SSC CGL 06/03/2020]

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following words is NOT mentioned in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2023]
solution:

The terms Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic are mentioned in the Preamble. (Socialist , Integrity and Secular added through 42nd CA,1976).

subject: Polity
question_text: All the ______ countries likely to have a Constitution. [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Democratic
Abraham Lincoln famously described democracy with this definition: "Democracy is government of the people, by the people, for the people."

subject: Polity
question_text: With reference to the Constituent Assembly, which of the following statements is Correct? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]

options.a: The Constituent Assembly ratified India's membership of the Commonwealth in May 1947.
options.b: The Constituent Assembly adopted the National Anthem in January 1950.
options.c: The Constituent Assembly adopted the National Song in January 1948.
options.d: The Constituent Assembly adopted the National Flag in July 1949.

solution:
(B) The Constituent Assembly adopted the National Anthem in January 1950.
The constituent assembly adopted the National Anthem (Rabindra Nath Tagore) and the National Song (Bankim Chatterjee) on 24th January, 1950. It ratified commonwealth membership in May, 1949. It adopted the national flag on 22nd July, 1947.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many female members were part of the Constituent Assembly that framed the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 11/04/2022]
solution:
(D) 15
On 24th January, 1950; constitution was signed by the members of CA. Total members - 284 members in which there were 15 females. Important female members of CA: Raj Kumari Amrit Kaur (1st health minister) ; Sucheta Kriplani (1st female CM of UP).

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following wrote in calligraphic style the Hindi version of the original Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Vasant Krishan Vaidya
For explanation, refer question 3

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constituent Assembly was recognised by Section ______ of the Indian Independence Act, 1947. [SSC CGL 19/04/2022]
solution:
(D) 8(1)
Section 8(1) of the Indian Independence Act, 1947 recognized the Constituent Assembly of India as the legislative body for the newly Independent Dominion of India until a new Constitution came into effect.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who was the chairman of the House Committee of the Constituent Assembly of India?
solution:

Pattabhi Sitaramayya was the Chairman of the House Committee of the constituent assembly, responsible for member accommodations and facilities. A.V. Thakkar focused on tribal welfare. JB Kripalani headed the Fundamental rights sub-committee. KM Munshi was the head of the Order of Business Committee.

subject: Polity
question_text: Alladi Krishnaswami Ayyar was the chairman of the ______ of the Constituent Assembly of India. [SSC CGL 19/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Credentials Committee
Union Power committee - Jawaharlal Nehru; Fundamental Rights Sub-Committee - JB Kripalani; Order of Business Committee - KM Munshi

subject: Polity
question_text: When was the Tenth Session of the Constituent Assembly held? [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(A) 6-17 october,1949
Total sessions - 11 sessions (165 days), Important sessions: - 1st session : 9-23 Dec. 1946, 10th session : 6-17th Oct, 1949, 11th session (last) : 14-26th November, 1949.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following became a part of the Constituent Assembly from Madras Constituency in 1946? [SSC CGL 21/04/2022]
solution:

Ammu Swaminathan was elected to the Constituent Assembly from the Madras constituency in 1946. Hansa Jivraj Mehta was elected from Bombay. Kamla Chaudhry represented the United Provinces and Begum Aizaz Rasul was the only Muslim woman member and also represented the United Provinces.

solution:

To whom among the following leaders is this quote attributed? [CAPF 2021]

He was the first law minister of independent India. For details, refer question 6.

He wrote the book Sambad (Socialism). Shyama Prasad Mukherjee - 1st Minister of Industries and Supplies

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following was the last Governor-General of India before the office was permanently abolished? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(B) Chakravarti Rajagopalachari
Chakravarti Rajagopalachari, commonly known as Rajaji, was the last Governor-General of India, serving from 1948 to 1950. He was the only Indian and the last person to hold the office of Governor-General. Also, Lord Mountbatten was the last Viceroy of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: Federalism in the Indian constitutional context means: [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]

options.a: the existence of more than one level of government in the country
options.b: the existence only one level of government in the country
options.c: the existence of states in the country
options.d: the existence of one nation one government in the country

solution:
(A) The existence of more than one level of government in the country.
Federal features - supremacy of the constitution, division of power between the Union and the State, the existence of an independent Judiciary.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Dual Citizenship
Provision of single citizenship adopted from the UK, Part II (Article 5-11) deals with Citizenship. Article 9 - Person voluntarily acquiring citizenship of a foreign country loses Indian citizenship, Article 10 - Continuance of rights of citizenship.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who described the Indian federal system as a bargaining federalism? [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(D) Morris Jones
Comments on Indian Federalism: 1) KC Wheare : referred to it as "Quasi-Federal". 2) Granville Austin : called it "Cooperative federalism". 3) Ivor Jenning : called it "Federalism with Centralizing tendency."

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution of India is a sovereign socialist secular democratic republic with a ______ system of government. [SSC CGL 06/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Parliamentary
India follows the Westminster model of parliamentary government, inspired by the British system. It features a bicameral legislature, with the executive (Prime Minister and Council of Ministers) drawn from and accountable to the lower house (Lok Sabha). The President serves as the nominal head of state, while real executive power lies with the Prime Minister.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following described the Indian Constitution as 'quasi federal'? [SSC CHSL 03/06/2022]
solution:
(D) KC Wheare
Note : AV Dicey gave the concept of rule of law.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which is NOT a unitary feature of the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 01/08/2022]

options.d: Appointment of the Governor by the President

solution:
(A) Bicameral Legislature
Bicameralism is a federal feature. Other important Unitary Features are: single constitution, power of union to override on the state matters, during emergency the system becomes virtually unitary, strong centre, states not indestructible, single citizenship, integrated judiciary, all-india services, appointment of governor.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a federal feature of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 27/05/2022]
solution:
(C) All India Services
It comes under Article 312, PART XIV : Services under the Union and the States (Art 308-314) Public Services Commissions (Art 315-323).

subject: Polity
question_text: The establishment of the Union Public Service Commission, State Public Service Commissions and Joint Public Service Commission falls under which of the following features of the Constitution of India? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Federalism
Article 315-323 of Part XIV deals with the establishment and role of the UPSC, SPSC and JPSC.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct in the context of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
1. There are three major organs of the government, namely Legislature, Executive and Judiciary.
2. A separation of functions rather than powers is followed.
3. All organs of the government are independent.
solution:
(A) 1 and 2 only
The Legislature and Executive are interlinked where the Executive is drawn from the Legislature and must maintain its confidence. The Judiciary acts as a check on both, ensuring they operate within constitutional limits while the Executive appoints judges, and the Legislature can amend laws thus statement 3 is wrong. For example :
Article 74(1) : States that the President shall act on the aid and advice of the Council of Ministers, showing that the Executive is not entirely independent of the Legislature in a parliamentary system.
Article 75(3) : Makes the Council of Ministers collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha, indicating the interdependence of the Executive and Legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: A ______ government governs within the bounds established by constitutional law and citizen rights [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Democratic
Democracy - "of the people, by the people, for the people" : by Abraham Lincoln.

subject: Polity
question_text: Secularism is a feature of the Indian Constitution, which means: [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]

options.a: The State promotes the religion of the majority community.
options.b: The State does not promote any religion.
options.c: The State can promote any religion.
options.d: The State promotes the religion of the minority community

solution:
(B) The State does not promote any religion
The terms 'secular', 'integrity' and 'socialist' were added in the Preamble through 42nd CA,1976.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which country has the lengthiest written constitution in the world? [SSC GD 12/01/2022]
solution:
(B) India
The Indian constitution is the longest written constitution in the world. It consists of 25 Parts and 12 Schedules. It draws inspiration from various countries :
(i) UK contributed the parliamentary system and rule of law.
(ii) USA inspired the structure of fundamental rights, judicial independence, and a written constitution.
(iii) Canada influenced the federal structure with a strong center.
(iv) Ireland provided the Directive Principles of State Policy.
(v) Australia inspired the concurrent list and joint sittings.
(vi) Germany and the USSR contributed emergency provisions and fundamental duties respectively.
(vii) France lent the ideals of liberty, equality, and fraternity.
(viii) South Africa and Japan influenced the amendment process and the concept of "procedure established by law".

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 1 of the Indian Constitution which states India, that is Bharat, shall be a Union of States, mentions which of the following features? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Cooperative Federalism
India, an union of states implies : 1) sharing of power between the centre and state; 2) not a result of agreement between states; 3) States have no right to secede from union; 4) India is an indestructible union of destructible states.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following is/are source/sources of Law in India?
1. Constitution of India
2. Statutes
3. Customary Law
4. Case Law
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(D) 1, 2, 3 and 4
The Constitution which is the supreme law paves way for other laws to be made by the legislature. Customary practices which are recognized by the law and the case decided by courts (Supreme court) acquires the form of law in the country.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a feature of Indian federalism? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: Courts can interpret the Constitution and powers of different levels of the Government.
options.b: Sources of revenue for the Union Government and the State Governments are specified.
options.c: Powers of the Union and the States are specified in the Constitution.
options.d: Indian federalism is based on the principle of Separation of Powers.

solution:
(D) Indian federalism is based on the principle of Separation of Powers.
The Indian Constitution does not follow a rigid separation of powers (unlike the US). Instead, it ensures a balance of power among the three organs of government, with some overlap in their functions. For example : The Executive (Council of Ministers) is part of the Legislature as they are members of parliament, The President (Executive) has legislative powers, such as issuing ordinances (Art 123) and veto power

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following is not correct? [CAPF 2023]

options.a: Legislation on criminal law, barring exceptions, is a subject under the Concurrent List.
options.b: Bankruptcy and Insolvency are subjects under the Concurrent List.
options.c: Inter-State trade and commerce is a subject under the State List.
options.d: Banking is exclusively under the Union List.

solution:
(C) Inter-State trade and commerce is a subject under the State List.
It is a subject under the Union List. The 7th schedule deals with the federal provisions. It specifies the Union list, State list and the Concurrent list (subjects on which central and state governments jointly make laws). Union list subjects - Defence, Banking, Currency, Foreign Affairs, Communication. State List subjects - Police, Trade, Agriculture, Irrigation, Health. Concurrent List subjects - Education, Forest, Weights and Measures, Marriage.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following is not a feature of centralization? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(D) Liberty
Centralization : concentration of power and decision-making authority in a central govt or authority. It can promote national unity, uniformity, and even prosperity (allows efficient allocation of resources and coordination of economic policies) but limits liberty.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following said that Democracy means a system of 'Government by Consent'? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(A) John Locke
John Locke, also called the "Father of Liberalism," emphasized that a legitimate government must be based on the consent of the governed. He wrote the book - Two Treatises of Government. Also, B.R. Ambedkar commented that democracy is not merely a form of government. It is primarily a mode of associated living.

subject: Polity
question_text: ‘Legal Positivism’ theory was developed by [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(C) John Austin
Legal positivism is a philosophical approach that views law as a human construct, created and enforced by social institutions, separate from morality and ethics.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not correct about 'Totalitarianism'? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: It is not akin to autocracy and authoritarianism.
options.b: It usurps political freedom of the individuals, but it doesn't usurp personal freedoms.
options.c: It implies abolition of civil society.
options.d: It is usually identified with a one-party state.

solution:
(B) It usurps political freedom of the individuals, but it doesn't usurp personal freedom.
Totalitarianism - total control over lives of citizens in all aspects including politics and individual lives. Autocracy - one person holds absolute power, but may not necessarily control all aspects of society. Authoritarianism - A system where the government holds significant power, but may still allow some personal freedom.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not correct? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: Taxes on agricultural income is a subject under the State List.
options.b: Price control is a subject under the Concurrent List.
options.c: Insurance does not come under the Union List.
options.d: 'Forests' is a subject under the Concurrent List.

solution:
(C) Insurance does not come under the Union List
Insurance falls in the Union List. Union List - 100 subjects, State List - 61 subjects, Concurrent List - 52 subjects. Subjects which are not present in any of the lists mentioned in the constitution are residuary subjects. Parliament has the law making power (Article 248).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not an Indian form of Socialism? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(B) Radical Socialism
Indian Socialism is associated with : Democratic : emphasizes democratic principles and social welfare; Communitarian : focuses on community based development; Nehruvian : advocated by Jawahar Lal Nehru, emphasizes state-led development and social welfare.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a characteristic feature of Indian Federalism? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: The federating units consented to form a union
options.b: Residuary powers vest with the Centre
options.d: An extensive Union and Concurrent list

solution:
(A) The federating units consented to form a union
Unlike in classical federations (e.g., the U.S.), where states voluntarily came together to form a union, the Indian Union was not formed by the consent of the states. Instead, the Indian Constitution was adopted by the Constituent Assembly, and the states were reorganized after independence.

subject: Polity
question_text:
As per the State List under the Seventh Schedule of the Constitution of India, the States have jurisdiction over agricultural land in connection with which of the following subjects? [CDS I 2024]
1. Taxes on agricultural income.
2. Duties in respect of succession of agricultural land.
3. Estate duty agricultural land in respect of agricultural land.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:

For explanation, refer Level II question 3.

question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Criminal law is exclusively a subject under the State List of the Constitution of India.
2. Code of Civil Procedure falls under the Concurrent List of the Constitution of India.
Select the answer using the code given below: [CDS II 2024]
solution:

Both Criminal law and Code of Civil Procedure fall under the Concurrent List.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following languages was added to the Eighth Schedule by a Constitutional Amendment in 1967? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Sindhi
Sindhi added by 21st CA,1967, Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali were added by 71st CA,1992. Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, Santhali were added by 92nd CA,2003. Currently, there are 22 official languages [Part XVII, Article 343-351]. Eighth Schedule deals with official languages.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a function of the Constitution of India? [NDA II 2021]

options.a: To ensure participation of good people in politics
options.b: To guarantee a set of rights to citizens
options.c: To define the power of the different organs of government
options.d: To create conditions for a just society

solution: (A) To ensure participation of good people in politics

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a feature of the Constitution of India? [NDA II 2021]

options.a: It provides a set of basic rules.
options.b: It specifies the power of the Government.
options.c: It is the supreme law of the land.
options.d: It specifies the supremacy of the judiciary.

solution:
(D) It specifies the supremacy of the judiciary
The constitution outlines the fundamental rules governing the country and is the supreme law of the land. It defines the powers and functions of the legislative, executive and judiciary but does not specify the supremacy of the judiciary.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which Schedule of the Constitution of India, provisions as to disqualification on grounds of defection are given? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(B) Tenth Schedule
The provision of defection was added by 52nd CA, 1985. Eleventh Schedule - Panchayats (73rd CA, 1993). It contains 29 subjects. Twelfth Schedule - Municipalities (74th CA, 1993). It contains 18 subjects.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists: [CBI 2023]
List-I (Provision of the Constitution of India) | List-II (Borrowed from)
a. First-past-the-post system | 1. Canadian Constitution
b. Charter of Fundamental Rights | 2. Irish Constitution
c. Residual Powers | 3. Constitution of the United States of America
d. Directive Principles of State Policy | 4. British Constitution
Code:
A) a-4, b-3, c-1, d-2
B) a-4, b-1, c-3, d-2
C) a-2, b-1, c-3, d-4
D) a-2, b-3, c-1, d-4
solution:
(A) A-4, B-3, C-1, D-2
Ireland - DPSP, Nomination of members in Rajya Sabha, Election method of President. Canada - Federalism, Centre appoints governor, Residuary power, Advisory Jurisdiction of SC. UK - Parliamentary form of government, Rule of law, Writs, Bicameralism

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following languages is not included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India? [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(D) Ladakhi
For explanation refer Level II question 13.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following Supreme Court Judgements established the principle that the 'basic structure' of the Constitution of India was unamendable? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.b: Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala
options.c: Sankari Prasad vs Union of India
options.d: Sajjan Singh vs State of Rajasthan

solution:
(B) Kesavananda Bharati vs State of Kerala, 1973
In this, the SC held that while Parliament has the power to amend the Constitution, it cannot alter its basic structure. Golaknath vs State of Punjab (1967) held that Parliament cannot curtail fundamental rights through any constitutional amendment. Sankari Prasad vs Union of India upheld the power of parliament to amend the Constitution without any limitations. Sajjan Singh vs State of Rajasthan involved a challenge on the constitutional validity of the 17th CA.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements as per the Third Schedule of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. A Member of Parliament is free to take the oath or affirmation by 'swearing in the name of God'.
2. Judge of the Supreme Court has to take the oath or affirmation by 'swearing in the name of the Constitution of India'.
Select the answer using the code given below: [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The third schedule provides options for oath-taking, allowing individuals to choose to swear in the name of God, any other deity or the Constitution itself. It contains the forms of oaths for: Judges (SC + HC), Ministers (Union + States), MPs, MLAs, CAG.

subject: Polity
question_text: Constitutional government means: [IAS 2021]

options.a: a representative government of a nation with federal structure.
options.b: a government whose Head enjoys nominal powers.
options.c: a government whose Head enjoys real powers.
options.d: a government limited by the terms of the constitution.

solution:
(D) A government limited by the terms of the constitution
A constitutional government is one where the powers and functions of all organs of government - executive, legislature, and judiciary - are clearly defined and limited by a written or unwritten constitution. No authority is above the Constitution. Power is distributed and the Supreme Court acts as a protector of Fundamental Rights and also holds the power of judicial review. (Article 137 - review of judgements or orders by the Supreme Court).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following factors constitutes the best safeguard of liberty in a liberal democracy? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(D) Separation of powers
Because it prevents concentration of power, provides checks and balance and prevents any branch from acting oppressively. A judiciary can be influenced by various factors and its commitment to protecting liberty can vary.

subject: Polity
question_text:
We adopted parliamentary democracy based on the British model, but how does our model differ from that model?
1. As regards legislation, the British Parliament is supreme or sovereign but in India, the power of the Parliament to legislate is limited
2. In India, matters related to the constitutionality of the Amendment of an Act of the Parliament are referred to the Constitution Bench by the Supreme Court.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [IAS 2021]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The British Parliament is supreme. There is no formal written constitution that restricts or limits its power whereas the Indian Constitution sets out specific subjects on which the parliament can legislate, it also provides for certain fundamental rights that cannot be curtailed. Also the judicial review of constitutional amendments is done by the constitutional bench unlike the British model.

subject: Polity
question_text: If a particular area is brought under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements best reflects the consequence of it? [IAS 2022]

options.a: This would prevent the transfer of land of tribal people to non-tribal people.
options.b: This would create a local self-governing body in that area
options.c: This would convert that area into a Union Territory.
options.d: The State having such areas would be declared a Special Category State.

solution:
(A) This would prevent the transfer of land of tribal people to non-tribal people.
The outcomes of bringing an area under the Fifth Schedule are: 1) Prohibition of land transfer. 2) Establishment of Tribal Advisory Councils to advise the governors of states. 3) Special Protection to tribal people and their land, culture and traditions.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to 'Scheduled Areas' in India, consider the following statements:
1. Within a State, the notification of an area as Scheduled Area takes place through an Order of the President.
2. The largest administrative unit forming the Scheduled Area is the District and the lowest is the cluster of villages in the Block.
3. The Chief Ministers of the concerned States are required to submit annual reports to the Union Home Ministry on the administration of Scheduled Areas in the States.
How many of the above statements are correct? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(B) Only two
Statements 1 and 2 are correct but statement 3 is incorrect as the Governors, not the chief ministers, are required to submit annual reports to the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct as per the Constitution of India? [IAS 2024]

options.a: Inter-State trade and commerce is a State subject under the State List.
options.b: Inter-State migration is a State subject under the State List.
options.c: Inter-State quarantine is a Union subject under the Union List.
options.d: Corporation tax is a State subject under the State List.

solution:
(C) Inter- State quarantine is a Union subject under the Union List
All the subjects mentioned in the options are under the Union List.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. It is the Governor of the State who recognizes and declares any community of that State as a Scheduled Tribe.
2. A community declared as a Scheduled Tribe in a State need not be so in another State.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(B) 2 only
It is the President who recognizes and declares any community as a scheduled tribe as per Article 342. Statement 2 is correct as a community declared as ST in one state need not be so in another state. The list of ST is state specific, and the President in consultation with the Governor of the concerned state, notifies the list of ST for each state.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following pairs of Schedule and Content of the Constitution of India:
1. First Schedule : Forms of Oaths or Affirmations
2. Third Schedule : Allocation of seats in the Council of States
3. Fifth Schedule : Provisions related to the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes
4. Ninth Schedule : Provisions related to the administration of tribal areas in certain States
How many of the above pairs is/are correctly matched? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(A) 1
Only statement 3 is correct. First schedule - Name of states and UTs, Third schedule - Forms of oaths and affirmations, Fourth schedule - Rajya Sabha seat allocation, Fifth schedule - Administration of scheduled areas and scheduled tribes, Sixth schedule - Provision related to the administration of tribal areas in certain States, Ninth schedule- Land reforms.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following word sequences is correct as per the Preamble to the Constitution? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic, republic
Sovereign - No authority over the country. India follows democratic socialism with a mixed economy and practices positive secularism (where the state has no official religion) involving government interference. Republic - Head of the country is elected.

subject: Polity
question_text: Any citizen of India can make a declaration renouncing his/her Indian citizenship under the______. [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Citizenship Act, 1955
The provisions of Citizenship have been amended 6 times out of which the Citizenship Amendment Act, 2019 is the latest one. It provided citizenship to religious minorities (Hindus, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis and Christians) from Afghanistan, Bangladesh and Pakistan who faced religious persecution.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which word has been substituted for the words 'Unity of the Nation' in the 42nd Amendment Act of the Preamble of the Constitution of India? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]

options.a: Unity and honesty of the nation
options.b: Unity and equity of the nation
options.c: Unity and integrity of the nation
options.d: Unity and morality of the nation

solution:
(C) Unity and integrity of the nation
Before the 42nd CA, 1976 the Preamble was written as Sovereign, Democratic, Republic. It was then changed to Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which part of the Constitution of India deals with 'Citizenship'? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Part II
Important articles : Article 8 - Citizenship of persons of Indian origin residing outside India. Article 9 - Persons voluntarily acquiring citizenship of a foreign state not to be citizens. Article 10 - Continuance of rights of citizenship. Article 11 - Parliament to regulate the right of citizenship by law.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Preamble, what does the promotion of fraternity assure? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]

options.a: Unity and integrity of the nation
options.b: Thought, expression, belief, faith and worship
options.d: Allows all to profess, preach and practice any religion

solution:
(A) Unity and integrity of the nation
Objectives of the Preamble - Justice, Liberty, Equality and Fraternity.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following noble ideals is mentioned in the Preamble of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]

options.a: Liberty of expression, civil, political, worship and belief
options.b: Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship
options.c: Liberty of belief, fiscal, personal, faith and worship
options.d: Liberty of opportunity, economic, social, faith and expression

solution:
(B) Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship
The principles of liberty, equality and fraternity are taken from France.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who moved the Objective Resolution that was later adopted as the Preamble of the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Jawaharlal Nehru
On 13th December, 1946 Jawaharlal Nehru moved the objective resolution which was adopted on 22nd January,1947 thus becoming the Preamble afterwards.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following words was NOT a part of the original Preamble of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Secular
For explanation refer question 2

subject: Polity
question_text: The Citizenship Act, 1955 does not deal with_______. [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Election
Acquisition by birth, descent, registration, or naturalization. Termination by renunciation, deprivation or cancellation. The act provides for the determination of citizenship in cases where a person's citizenship is in doubt or disputed.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who described the Preamble of Indian Constitution as the 'Political horoscope of the Indian Constitution'? [SSC CHSL 13/10/2020]
solution:
(B) Kanhaiyalal Maniklal Munshi
NA Palkhiwala - 'ID Card of the Constitution', Ernst Barker - 'Keynote of the Constitution' in his book 'Principles of social and political theory'. Pandit Thakur Das Bhargava said that 'Preamble is the soul of the Constitution'.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Constitution of India mentions 'Continuance of the rights of citizenship'? [SSC MTS 06/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Article 10
For explanation refer question 4.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is mentioned in Article 1 of the Indian constitution? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]

options.a: India that is Bharat as federation of states
options.b: India that is Bharat as parliamentary democracy
options.c: India that is Bharat shall be a Union of states
options.d: India that is Bharat as Presidential form of democracy

solution:
(C) India that is Bharat shall be a Union of states
Articles 1 to 4 deal with 'The Union and its Territory' and comes under the Part I of the constitution.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is an incorrect statement in regard to the meaning of 'Union of States' in the Indian context? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023]

options.a: Country was divided into different states for administrative convenience.
options.b: The Indian federation was the result of an agreement by sovereign units to join it.
options.c: Enumerates the power of the centre and states through various lists.
options.d: Federation not being the result of agreement, no state has a right to secede from it.

solution:
(B) The Indian federation was the result of an agreement by sovereign units to join it.
There is no agreement between the states to form a union and the states have no rights to secede from union. India is an indestructible union of destructible states.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Articles of the Constitution of India deal with the Union and its territory? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Article 1-4
Part I (Union and its territory) contains Articles 1-4. Article 1 - India, that is Bharat shall be a union of states. Article 2 - Establishment of new states. Article 3 - Formation of new states, alteration of areas, boundaries or names of existing states. Article 4 - Any law made under article 2 and 3 to provide for amendment of the 1st and 4th schedule but not to be dealt as an amendment under Article 368. Articles 5-11 [Part II] - Citizenship, Articles 12-35 [Part III] - Fundamental Rights, Article 36-51 [Part IV] - DPSP

subject: Polity
question_text: Who is the source of all the powers under the constitution in the "Preamble"? [SSC CISF HCM 30/10/2023]
solution:
(D) We the People of India
Source of authority of constitution - Peoples of India. Concept of the Preamble is taken from the USA and the language is derived from Australia. The Preamble is not legally enforceable in a court of law but it is used as an interpretative tool of the constitution and a guiding principle in the courts.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following terms is NOT included in the Preamble of Indian Constitution? [SSC CGL 09/12/2024]
solution:
(D) Federalism
Objectives of Preamble are: 1) Justice : Social , Economic and Political. 2) Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship. 3) Equality of status and opportunity. 4) Fraternity

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the formation of New States? [SSC CHSL 20/10/2020]
solution:
(D) Article 3
Article 3 involves: 1) formation of new state 2) increase the area of a state 3) decrease the area of a state 4) alteration of boundary 5) alter the name of a state. Provided that no Bill shall be introduced in either House of Parliament except on the recommendation of the President. The Bill is referred by the President to the Legislature of that state for expressing its views.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution are related to citizenship? [SSC CGL 14/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Articles 5 to 11
Part II deals with the provisions of citizenship. India follows the provision of single citizenship and has taken its concept from the UK. Articles 5-8 of Part II were enforced before 26 January, 1950 while the others were enforced after it. Article 5 - Citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution, Article 6 - Rights of citizenship of persons who have migrated to India from Pakistan, Article 7 - Rights of citizenship of migrants to Pakistan.

subject: Polity
question_text: What do you understand by 'dual citizenship' as in the case of the US? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]

options.a: Citizenship of two states simultaneously with two countries
options.b: Citizenship of a country along with that of a constituent State, where one resides/born
options.c: Citizenship of two states along with two countries

solution: (B) Citizenship of a country along with that of a constituent State, where one resides/born.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which year was the Citizenship Act passed in India? [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 1955
As per Citizenship Act 1955, one can acquire citizenship by the following ways: 1) By Birth 2) By descent 3) By registration 4) By Naturalisation 5) By incorporation of territories.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India vests plenary powers in Parliament to legislate on the subject of citizenship? [SSC CHSL 11/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 11
India has the feature of single citizenship taken from the UK.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a condition for acquiring the citizenship of India? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Holding Property
Indian citizenship is granted by birth, descent, registration, naturalization or incorporation of territory.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a way to acquire citizenship in India? [SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Renunciation
One can lose citizenship by the following ways : 1) By renunciation 2) By termination (by voluntarily acquiring citizenship of another country) 3) By deprivation (document malfunction).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is not correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: The term 'Secular Democratic Republic' was not there in the Preamble originally.
options.b: The Preamble contains the basic structure of the Constitution of India.
options.c: By itself, the Preamble is not enforceable in a court of law.
options.d: The Supreme Court has held that the Preamble cannot be considered as an aid to the legal interpretation of the Constitution, where the language is ambiguous.

solution:
(D) The Supreme Court has held that the Preamble cannot be considered as an aid to the legal interpretation of the Constitution, where the language is ambiguous.
The terms 'secular', 'integrity' and 'socialism' were later added to the Preamble by the 42nd CA. Also the Preamble is not enforceable by itself in a court of law. However, the Supreme Court has actually held that the Preamble can be considered as an aid to the legal interpretation of the Constitution, particularly where the language is ambiguous.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the creation of a new state or alteration of boundaries of states is/are correct? [CDS I 2021]
1. An amendment has to be moved in the Parliament under Article 368 of the Constitution of India
2. Ratification by one half of the states is necessary after the amendment is made by the Parliament.
3. The legislation can be passed by a simple majority of both Houses of Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(C) 3 only
A law altering state boundaries or creating a new state can be passed by a simple majority in both Houses of Parliament under Article 3. While Article 4 states that any law made under Article 2 and 3 is not to be dealt as an amendment under Article 368. However, if India transfers any of its land to some other country, it will be dealt as an amendment under Article 368. For e.g. 100th CA dealing with land exchange between India and Bangladesh.

subject: Polity
question_text: The federation of which one of the following States has been described as "an indestructible union of indestructible states"? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(A) India
For explanation refer question 13 (level 1).

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements with regard to the Preamble to the Constitution of India is/are correct? [CDS I 2022]
1. Equality of status and equality of opportunity find mention in the Preamble.
2. The expression 'unity and integrity of the Nation' was not there in the Preamble since the beginning.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The term 'Unity of the Nation' was substituted by 'Unity and integrity of the Nation' through the 42nd CA, 1976.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Through the Forty Second Amendment to the Constitution of India, which of the following was/were inserted into its Preamble? [CDS I 2024]
1. Liberty of thought, expression and belief was substituted by 'Liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship'.
2. The expression 'Unity of the Nation' was substituted by 'Unity and Integrity of the Nation'.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
solution: (B) 2 only

subject: Polity
question_text: The Citizenship Act, 1955 deals with the determination of citizenship on or after [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(A) 26th January, 1950
The constitution provided provisions for determining citizenship at the commencement of the Republic day under Articles 5 to 11. The CAA, 1955 regulates the acquisition and termination of Indian citizenship after this date.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following areas has been exempted from the purview of the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019? [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(C) The Sixth Schedule Areas
The Citizenship Amendment Act, 2019 does not apply to: 1) Tribal areas under the sixth schedule: Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram. 2) The 'inner line permit' regions under the Bengal Eastern Frontier Regulation.

subject: Polity
question_text: As per Article 371G of the Constitution of India, special provisions have been made with respect to the state of Mizoram. Accordingly, no act of Parliament shall apply on certain matters unless the Legislative Assembly of Mizoram so decides by a resolution. Which one of the following matters is not covered under this Article? [CDS II 2024]

options.a: Religious or social practices of the Mizos.
options.c: Administration of civil and criminal justice involving decisions according to Mizo customary law.
options.d: Ownership and transfer of natural resources.

solution:
(D) Ownership and transfer of natural resources
Article 371- special provision with respect to the states of Maharashtra and Gujarat. (Trick to remember articles 371A t 371J - NAM AASMA GK). 371A - Nagaland, 371B - Assam, 371C - Manipur, 371D - Andhra Pradesh, 371E - Central University in Andhra Pradesh, 371F - Sikkim, 371G - Mizoram, 371H - Arunachal Pradesh, 371I - Goa, 371J - Karnataka.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following terms was used for the States in the Draft Constitution presented by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar on 4th November, 1948? [CDS II 2024]
solution:
(A) Union of States
The final draft was presented by BR Ambedkar on 4th, November, 1948 who headed the Drafting Committee which had 7 members.

subject: Polity
question_text: With regard to the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements is not correct? [NDA I 2021]

options.a: The words — Socialist and Secular, were not originally part of the Constitution.
options.b: The Preamble states the objects of the Constitution of India.
options.c: The Preamble is enforceable in a Court of Law.
options.d: A Republic refers to the people as the source of all authority under the Constitution.

solution:
(C) The Preamble is enforceable in a Court of Law.
In Kesavananda Bharati Case (1973), the Supreme Court held that the Preamble is a part of the constitution but not legally enforceable. Statement 4 is correct as a Republic means that the head of state is elected and that the people are the source of all authority in the government.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution (35th Amendment) Act of 1974 is related to which one of the following States? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(B) Sikkim
The 35th CA granted Sikkim the status of an "Associate State" of India. However, in the 36th CA, 1975; Sikkim became a full-fledged state. The formation of Arunachal Pradesh took place by the 55th CA.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. There is only one citizenship and one domicile.
2. A citizen by birth only can become the Head of State.
3. A foreigner once granted the citizenship cannot be deprived of it under any circumstances.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(A) 1 only
India follows single citizenship. Similarly, the concept of domicile is singular in India. As per Article 58, a naturalized citizen is also eligible to become the President. The citizenship once granted can be revoked under the provisions of citizenship amendment act, 1955.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Under Article 371A of the Constitution of India, with respect to which of the following item(s) the Acts of the Parliament of India shall not apply to the State of Nagaland, unless the Legislative Assembly of the State of Nagaland by a resolution so decides?
1. Religious or social practices of Nagas.
2. Naga customary law and procedure.
3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources.
4. Boundaries of Nagaland with other states.
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) 1, 2 and 3 only
The boundaries of Nagaland with other states are determined by the Union and do not fall under the special protections of Article 371A.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following Indian States was admitted as an 'Associate State' to the Union of India in 1974? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) Sikkim
For explanation refer question 11

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not correct? [CISF AC 2023]

options.a: Goa was established as a State by separating it from the Union Territory of Daman and Diu in 1987.
options.b: Mizoram was earlier a Union Territory.
options.c: Karnataka was created by the States Reorganisation Act, 1956.
options.d: The State of Bombay was bifurcated into Gujarat and Maharashtra in 1960.

solution:
(A) Goa was established as a State by separating it from the Union Territory of Daman and Diu in 1987.
Goa was not separated from Daman & Diu but rather became a state while Daman & Diu continued as UT by the 56th CA, 1987. Also, the state of Bombay was bifurcated into Gujarat and Maharashtra on 1 May, 1960.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following in Indian polity is an essential feature that indicates that it is federal in character? [IAS 2021]

options.a: The independence of judiciary is safeguarded.
options.b: The Union Legislature has elected representatives from constituent units.
options.c: The Union Cabinet can have elected representatives from regional parties.
options.d: The Fundamental Rights are enforceable by Courts of Law.

solution:
(B) The Union Legislature has elected representatives from constituent units.
A federal system is characterized by the division of powers between a central government and its constituent units (states). A key feature of federalism is that states have representation in the central legislature, ensuring their role in national decision-making. However, India is not purely federal but "quasi-federal", meaning it functions as a federal system with a strong unitary bias.

subject: Polity
question_text: What was the exact constitutional status of India on 26th January, 1950? [IAS 2021]

options.d: A Sovereign Socialist Secular Democratic Republic

solution:
(B) Sovereign Democratic Republic
The Preamble was later changed to Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic, Republic by the 42nd CA, 1976.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which is the latest language added to the Eighth Schedule? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) Maithili
The most recent additions to the Eighth Schedule of the Indian Constitution were made through the 92nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 2003, which included Bodo, Dogri, Maithili, and Santali, bringing the total number of recognized languages to 22. Earlier, the 71st Amendment Act, 1992, added Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali to the Eighth Schedule.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Fundamental Rights is mentioned in which part of the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 29/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Part III
Part III (Article 12-35) deals with the fundamental rights. It is described as the Magna Carta of India. Part IV (Article 36-51) deals with DPSP. Part I (Article 1-4) deals with Union and its Territories. Part II (Article 5-11) deals with Citizenship.

subject: Polity
question_text: From which of the following Constitution's is the idea of 'Charter of Fundamental Rights' adopted in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(A) United States of America Constitution
The fundamental rights are inspired and borrowed from the 'Bill of Rights' of the USA.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many Fundamental Rights were initially provided in the Constitution of India? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(B) Seven
Originally, 7 FRs were present. However, Right to Property (Article 31 and 19(f)) has been removed by the 44th CA, 1978 [Then PM : Morarji Desai]. Now, it's a legal right placed under Article 300A.

subject: Polity
question_text: The concept of limited government is a major advancement to protect the ______ of the citizens from the ruling governments. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Fundamental Rights
The Constitution limits the power of the government by guaranteeing certain Fundamental Rights to all citizens. These rights act as a shield to protect individuals from any arbitrary or unfair actions by the State. Since they are enforceable by courts under Article 32, no government can override or violate them, making them the most effective way to restrict and control government power.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution limits the power of government in many ways. The most common way of limiting the power of government is to specify certain ______ that all of us possess as citizens and which no government can ever be allowed to violate. [SSC GD 20/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Fundamental rights
For explanation, refer to question 4.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution are related to Fundamental Rights? [SSC CHSL 18/07/2024]
solution:
(B) 12-35
There are six fundamental rights: 1) Right to Equality (14-18); 2) Right to Freedom (19-22); 3) Right against exploitation (23-24); 4) Right to Freedom of Religion (25-28); 5) Cultural and Educational Rights (29-30); 6) Right to Constitutional Remedies (32). (Also called 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution by BR Ambedkar).

subject: Polity
question_text: The Right to freedom includes the right to practice any profession, occupation, trade or business under which articles of the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 19-22
Right to Freedom deals with Article 19-22. Article 19 - Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech. Its subclause (g) provides for the right to practise any profession, occupation, trade or business. Article 20 - Protection in respect of conviction for offences. (1) no ex post facto laws, (2) No double jeopardy, (3) No self incrimination. Article 21 - Right to life and personal liberty. Article 22 - Protection against arrest in certain cases.

subject: Polity
question_text: Since complete equality is impossible, ______ of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to equality and equal protection of law subject to appropriate classification. Equal protection of law subject to appropriate classification. [SSC MTS 06/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Article 14
It talks about : 1) Equality before law : negative concept which is taken from the UK; 2) Equal protection of law : positive concept taken from the USA which implies that like should be treated alike. Exceptions: President/MLAs/MPs cannot be arrested on civil matters.

subject: Polity
question_text: Abolition of titles is mentioned in which Article of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 18
Article 18 (1) No title except military or academic distinction can be conferred by the state. (2) No citizen of India shall accept any title from any foreign state. The Bharat Ratna was instituted on 2 January 1954 by then President Dr. Rajendra Prasad, with Jawaharlal Nehru as the PM. In the landmark case Balaji Raghavan v. Union of India, the Supreme Court ruled that the Bharat Ratna is not a "title" under Article 18(1) as it does not confer any hereditary titles.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 12 of the Indian Constitution provides for _______. [SSC GD 26/02/2024]

options.d: Definition of Directive Principle of State Policy

solution:
(C) Definition of "State"
Article 12 defines the term "State". It can be the State government, Parliament, Government body, Central government, Local/ other authorities that are within the territory of India. According to Supreme Court, even a private body working as an instrument of the state falls under the meaning of state.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution is mentioned under the "Right to Equality"? [SSC GD 24/02/2024]

options.c: Protection of life and personal liberty

solution:
(B) Abolition of titles
Article 21A - Right to Education, Article 18 - Abolition of titles, Article 21 - Protection of life and personal liberty, Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 14 to 18 of Indian Constitution mention provisions related to which of the following rights? [SSC GD 28/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Right to Equality
"Right to Equality" deals with Articles 14-18. Article 14 - Equality before law and equal protection of law; Article 15 - Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth; Article 16 - Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment; Article 17 - Abolition of untouchability; Article 18 - Abolition of titles.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Clause of Article 19 of the Indian Constitution guarantees the right to move freely throughout the territory of India? [SSC MTS 05/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Clause (d)
For explanation refer question 7.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions that 'No person shall be prosecuted and punished for the same offence more than once'? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 20(2)
Article 20 - Protection in respect of conviction for offences. (1) No ex post facto law. (A person cannot be punished for an act that was not an offence at the time of its commission). (2) No double jeopardy. (It means that no person shall be punished for the same offence more than once). (3) No self - incrimination (No person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness against himself).

subject: Polity
question_text: Right to freedom of speech and expression is given under _______. [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 19
Article 19 - Protection of certain rights regarding freedom of speech. (1) All citizens shall have the right : (a) to freedom of speech and expression, (b) Assemble without arms, (c) Form associations/ unions or cooperative society, (d) Move freely throughout the territory of India, (e) to reside and settle in any part of India, (g) to practise any profession, occupation, trade or business.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to Article 32 of the Indian Constitution, the Right to Constitutional remedies is for the enforcement of _______. [SSC GD 07/03/2024]
solution:
(A) Fundamental Rights
The Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32) is the guardian of all the fundamental rights. For explanation refer to question 7.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 19(1)(B) of the Indian Constitution is related to which of the following? [SSC GD 29/02/2024]

options.a: To assemble peaceably and without arms
options.c: To move freely throughout the territory of India

solution:
(A) To assemble peaceably and without arms
For explanation refer to question 7.

subject: Polity
question_text: As of 2022, which of the following is NOT a part of Article 19 of the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]

options.b: To assemble peaceably and without arms
options.c: To move freely throughout the territory of India

solution:
(A) Right to property
It was under Article 19(1)(f) and was repealed by the 44th CA, 1978. Currently, Right to property is under Article 300A of part XII (Finance, Property, Contracts and Suits).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution mentions that no person accused of any offence shall be compelled to be a witness? [SSC GD 21/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 20
It comes under Article 20 (3). Also, Article 20(1) which deals with protection against ex-post facto laws applies exclusively to criminal law and does not extend to civil matters or taxation. For further explanation refer to question 14.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Indian Constitution, Right to protection of life and personal liberty is a _______. [SSC GD 26/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Fundamental Right of an individual
Article 21 deals with Right to life and personal liberty. It is the most fundamental right. It has been broadly interpreted by the Supreme Court to include various rights such as the right to privacy, clean environment, speedy trial and dignified life. Maneka Gandhi Vs Union of India is based on this article and the exception of this article is 'procedure established by law'.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which writ is issued by a high court or supreme court when a lower court has considered a case going beyond its jurisdiction? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Prohibition
Prohibition - 'to forbid'. It is issued by a higher court to a lower court or tribunal to prevent the latter from exceeding its jurisdiction. Whereas Certiorari which literally means 'to be certified' is issued to quash a decision of a lower court or tribunal typically on the grounds of lack of jurisdiction or an excess of jurisdiction. While Certiorari can be issued in cases where a lower court has exceeded its jurisdiction, Prohibition is more specifically targeted at preventing a lower court from acting beyond its jurisdiction.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Right against self-incrimination fundamental right ensures that: [SSC GD 05/03/2024]

options.a: No person can be compelled to provide evidence in court
options.b: No person can be compelled to be a witness against themselves
options.c: No person can be compelled to confess to a crime
options.d: No person can be compelled to testify against someone else

solution:
(B) No person can be compelled to be a witness against themselves.
For explanation, refer to question 14.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which judgement did the Supreme Court extend the scope of Article 21 and observed that 'the Right to education flows directly from the Right to life'? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]

options.a: Kharak Singh vs. State of UP
options.b: Malak Singh vs. State of Punjab
options.c: Neerja Choudhari vs. State of MP
options.d: Mohini Jain vs. State of Karnataka

solution:
(D) Mohini Jain v. State of Karnataka
Other cases related to Article 21 : 1) Gopalan case - In this case the Supreme Court gave a narrow stance of Article 21, limiting protection against arbitrary state action.; 2) Maneka Gandhi Case - the SC overruled the Gopalan case and gave a wide stance of Article 21 and ruled that Article 21 requires both procedure established by law and the due process of law.; 3) Olga Tellis Vs Bombay Municipal Corp Case.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Clause of Article 20 incorporates the doctrine of double jeopardy? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) 2
For explanation, refer to question 14.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 20 of the Constitution of India is related to _______. [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]

options.b: protection in respect of conviction for offenses
options.d: equality of opportunities in matters of public employment

solution:
(B) Protection in respect of conviction for offences
For explanation, refer to question 14.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles prohibits human trafficking and forced labor? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 23
Articles 23 and 24 come under the FR " Freedom against Exploitation ". Article 23 - Prohibition of human trafficking and forced labour. Article 24 - Prohibition of employment of children below the age of 14 years in hazardous places. The Child Labour (Prohibition and Regulation) Act, 1986 prohibits the employment of children below 14 years in hazardous occupations and regulates their working conditions in others. The 2016 amendment bans all child labour under 14, except in family enterprises or as artists (non-hazardous and non-disruptive to education), and also prohibits 'adolescents' (14-18 years) from hazardous work. It also increased penalties for violations.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following Articles of the Indian Constitution deal with "Right to Freedom of Religion"?[SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 25-28
The Right to freedom of Religion deals with Article 25-28. Article 25 - Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion. Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs. Article 27 - Freedom as to payment of taxes. Article 28 - Freedom as to attendance at religious instruction or worship in government aided schools.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions that every religious denomination or section shall have the right to own and acquire movable and immovable property? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 26(c)
Article 26 - Freedom to manage religious affairs. (a) establish and maintain institutions for religious and charitable purposes; (b) to manage its own affairs in matters of religion; (c) to own and acquire movable and immovable property; (d) to administer such property in accordance with law.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution mentions establishing and maintaining institutions for religious and charitable purposes? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 26
It is mentioned in Article 26(a). Also, Article 28 says that no religious teaching is allowed in government-funded schools. However, it is allowed in schools set up under trusts that require it. Also, no student can be forced to take part in religious activities without their or their guardian's consent.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which set of Articles in the Constitution of India are known as 'Cultural and Educational Rights'? [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 29-30
Article 29 - Right to protect the language, culture and script of every citizen for any section of the society. Article 30 - All minorities, whether based on religion or language, shall have the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Fundamental Rights has been deleted from the original list of fundamental rights provided in the Constitution of India? [SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Right to property
It was deleted by the 44th CA, 1978. Then PM - Morarji Desai. For explanation, refer to question 3.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Indian Constitution, Right against exploitation prohibiting all forms of ______ and traffic in human beings? [SSC GD 06/03/2024]
solution:
(D) Forced Labour
Rights against exploitation are contained in Article 23 and Article 24.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article provides that all minorities have the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice? [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 30
Article 30(1): All minorities, whether based on religion or language, have the right to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice. 30(1A): Compensation must be given if such property is acquired by the state. 30(2): The state cannot discriminate in granting aid to any educational institution on the ground that it is managed by a minority.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available for Indians as well as foreigners? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]

options.b: Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions
options.c: Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment
options.d: Right to protection of language, script and culture of minorities

solution:
(A) Right to elementary education
All the rights except the rights dealing with articles 15, 16, 19, 29, 30 are available to citizens as well as foreigners. Article 21A - Right to elementary education. Article 30 - Right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions. Article 16 - Equality of opportunity in matters of public employment. Article 29 - Right to protection of language, script and culture of minorities.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution CANNOT be suspended even during an emergency declared on the grounds of war or external aggression? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Articles 20 and 21
Rights under Article 20 and 21 can never be suspended. Rights under Article 19 cannot be suspended when National Emergency is imposed on grounds of Armed Rebellion. President can suspend any Fundamental Right using Article 359 except the ones mentioned under Article 20 and 21.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a writ issued by the Supreme Court of India? [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Jus Soli
The five writs namely Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Prohibition and Quo Warranto are mentioned in Article 32 which deals with the Right to Constitutional Remedies. The Supreme Court (Article 124) cannot refuse to issue writs but the high court (Article 214) can. Also, the writ jurisdiction of High Courts is wider than that of Supreme Court in India as the Supreme Court can issue writs for fundamental rights only but the High Court can issue writs for both fundamental rights and legal rights.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article ______ of the Indian Constitution gives an extensive original jurisdiction to the Supreme Court in regard to enforcement of Fundamental Rights? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 32
Article 32 provides an extensive original jurisdiction to the Supreme Court for the enforcement of fundamental rights. It allows individuals to directly approach the Supreme Court if their FRs are violated. Dr. BR Ambedkar called Article 32 the "heart and soul" of the constitution. Under Article 226, the citizens can approach the High Court if their rights are violated.

subject: Polity
question_text: During what circumstances can some Fundamental Rights be suspended, as per the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 21/02/2024]

options.b: During external emergencies, such as war or external aggression
options.c: Fundamental Rights cannot be suspended under any circumstances
options.d: During any emergency, whether national or local

solution:
(B) During external emergencies, such as war or external aggression
FRs can be suspended during a national emergency declared under Article 352 due to war, external aggression. Articles 20 and 21 can never be suspended, other FRs can be suspended by a Presidential order under Article 359. Also, during armed rebellion, rights under Article 19 cannot be suspended.

subject: Polity
question_text: Suppose a mine worker asks his son, aged 9 years, to help him in his work instead of going to school. Which Article of the Constitution of India will be violated in this case? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 24
Article 24 deals with prohibition of child labour. For explanation refer to question 26.

subject: Polity
question_text: Writ petition can be filled in the Supreme Court of India under: [SSC GD 29/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 32
The concept of prerogative writs is taken from the UK.

subject: Polity
question_text: Writ of Mandamus is a Fundamental Right classifiable under: [SSC CHSL 01/07/2024]

options.d: the right to freedom of religion

solution:
(C) Constitutional Remedies
Mandamus - 'We Command'. It is a directive to a public official requesting to perform official duties when failed to perform or refused to do so. Habeas Corpus - "Produce the body"; used to release someone unlawfully detained. Prohibition - "We forbid"; issued to a lower court to stop it from exceeding its jurisdiction. Certiorari - "to be certified"; issued to quash an order passed by a lower court or tribunal. Quo Warranto - "By what authority"; challenges a person's right to hold a public office.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many types of writs can be issued by the Supreme Court to protect the Fundamental Rights of Indian citizens? [SSC CHSL 02/07/2024]
solution:
(C) Five
For explanation, refer to question 41.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following fundamental rights has not been provided to a person? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: Protection against prosecution and punishment for the same offence more than once.
options.b: To refuse to give his/her sample of handwriting as evidence to support a prosecution against him/her.
options.c: To act as a witness against himself/herself.
options.d: Right not to be convicted of any offence except for violation of a law in force at the time of commission of the act charged as an offence.

solution:
(C) To act as a witness against himself/ herself
Article 20 (3) - No self incrimination.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about 'personal liberty' is not correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: State does not have the authority to deprive any person within the territory of India of his/her personal liberty without any rational basis.
options.b: Basis of depriving a person of his/her personal liberty must be in accordance with procedures established by law.
options.c: Personal liberty can be secured by the judicial writ of Habeas Corpus.
options.d: The majority view of the Supreme Court in A. K. Gopalan vs. State of Madras case invented 'due process of law'.

solution:
(D) The majority view of the Supreme Court in A.K. Gopalan vs. State of Madras case invented 'due process of law'.
In this case, the Supreme Court interpreted Article 21 narrowly, ruling that "procedure established by law" does not mean "due process of law". Thus, statement D is incorrect. Also, statement C is correct as personal liberty can indeed be secured by the judicial writ of Habeas Corpus, which is a remedy available to individuals who are detained or imprisoned without lawful authority.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution of India does not guarantee which one of the following as a fundamental right? [CAPF 2021]

options.d: Right to carry on trade and business

solution:
(C) Right to Property
The constitution originally did guarantee the Right to Property as a fundamental right under Article 31. However, it was repealed by the 44th CA,1978 and was placed as a legal right under article 300A. Right to carry on trade and business is a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(g).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements with regard to Maneka Gandhi vs. Union of India case, 1978 is not correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: It was held that Article 19 and Article 21 are not watertight compartments.
options.b: It was held that a law coming under Article 21 may not satisfy the requirements of Article 19.
options.c: A fair trial eliminates the biases against the accused in the trial.
options.d: The right to life under Article 21 does not include the right to die.

solution:
(D) The right to life under article 21 does not include the right to die.
This statement is not correct in the context of the Maneka Gandhi case as the case did not explicitly rule out the right to die as part of the right to life. In fact, subsequent judgements, such as the Gian Kaur vs State of Punjab case, have clarified that the right to life does not include the right to die.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following deals with Habeas Corpus? [CAPF 2023]

options.a: State of Uttar Pradesh vs. Raj Narain and Ors. (1975)
options.b: Additional District Magistrate, Jabalpur vs. Shivakant Shukla (1976)
options.c: Mrs. Maneka Gandhi vs. Union of India (1978)

solution:
(B) Additional District Magistrate, Jabalpur vs. Shivakant Shukla
This case, also known as the Habeas Corpus case, dealt with the suspension of the right to file a Habeas Corpus petition during Emergency. The Supreme Court ruled that during the Emergency, citizens could not challenge unlawful detention under Article 21 because FRs were suspended under Presidential order. This judgement was later overruled in the K.S. Puttaswamy vs. Union of India case. The State of Uttar Pradesh vs. Raj Narain : Related to government transparency and the right to information. Nandini Satpathy v. P.L. Dani : Related to protection against self incrimination.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Judgment of the Supreme Court in Peoples' Union for Civil Liberties vs. Union of India 2004 is related to which of the following? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Right to Information
In this case, the Supreme Court recognized the Right to Information as a fundamental right under Article 19(1)(a). Also, in the Romesh Thappar v. State of Madras (1950), the court ruled that freedom of speech and expression includes freedom of the press.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against the State only.
2. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable in favor of citizens only.
3. Some Rights contained in Part-III of the Constitution of India are enforceable against both, the state and individuals.
Select the answer using the code given below: [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Some rights are enforceable against the State only (Article 14, 19, 21). Some rights are enforceable in favor of citizens only (Article 19). Some rights are enforceable against both the State and individuals (Article 23, 25).

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which among the following fundamental rights is/are available to non-citizens?
1. Freedom of speech
2. Protection against self-incrimination
3. Freedom of conscience
4. Non-discrimination in matters of employment
