Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(C) 2 and 3 only
Rights under Articles 15, 16, 19, 29 and 30 are not available to non-citizens. For further explanation, refer to question 34 of level I.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following was eliminated by the 44th Amendment Act of the Parliament? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(C) Right to Property
It was eliminated as a fundamental rights and placed under Article 300A as a legal right.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a correct statement with reference to the Constitution of India? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: The Constitution (Eighty-Fifth) Amendment Act has inserted the 'Right to Education' under Article 21A.
options.c: Right to Constitutional Remedies is in Part-III of the Constitution.
options.d: Writ jurisdiction of High Courts is wider than the Supreme Court of India.

solution:
(A) The Constitution (Eighty-Fifth) Amendment Act has inserted the 'Right to Education' under Article 21A.
It was the 86th CA, 2002 that inserted the Right to Education under Article 21A. Article 45 - The State shall endeavor to provide free and compulsory education for all children up to the age of 14 years. The CA also added Article 51A (k) - to provide opportunities for education to his child or ward between the age of 6 and 14 years.

subject: Polity
question_text:
The Constitution of India guarantees freedom of speech and expression. But the freedom is subject to certain reasonable restrictions imposed by the State. These restrictions may relate to which of the following?
1. Defamation
2. Decency or morality
3. Incitement to an offence
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
Freedom of speech and expression under Article 19(1)(a) can be reasonably restricted under Article 19(2) in the interests of sovereignty and integrity of India, security of the State, friendly relations with foreign states, public order, decency or morality, contempt of court, defamation, and incitement to an offence.

subject: Polity
question_text: Equality before the law or equal protection of the laws within the territory of India is guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India? [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(A) Article 14
For explanation refer question 8 of level 1.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is the correct meaning of the term 'State', as defined in Article 12 of the Constitution of India? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: It refers only to the Government of India.
options.b: It refers only to the Government of India and the Parliament of India.
options.c: It refers only to the Government of India, Parliament of India, Governments and Legislatures of each State.
options.d: It refers to the Government of India, Parliament of India, Governments and Legislatures of each State, and all local or other authorities within the territory of India.

solution:
(D) It refers to the Government of India, Parliament of India, Governments and Legislatures of each state, and all local or other authorities within the territory of India.
The definition of "State" is given in Article 12.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following writs means 'you may have the body'? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(A) 'You may have the body'
Habeas Corpus is a writ issued by a court to produce a person who has been detained unlawfully before the court. The purpose of this writ is to safeguard individual freedom and prevent illegal detention. The Supreme Court can issue writs only for the enforcement of fundamental rights whereas the high court can issue writs for both fundamental and legal rights.

subject: Polity
question_text: The issue, 'whether Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code is violative of Articles 21, 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India' was decided in which one of the following cases? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: Shreya Singhal vs. Union of India
options.c: Shayara Bano vs. Union of India
options.d: Naz Foundation vs. Government of NCT of Delhi and others

solution:
(D) Naz Foundation vs. Government of NCT of Delhi and others.
It decriminalized consensual homosexual acts among adults in private. The court held that Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code was unconstitutional as it violated Articles 21 (right to life and personal liberty), 14 (right to equality) and 15 (prohibition of discrimination on grounds of sex). The Vishakha vs. State of Rajasthan case dealt with the issue of sexual harassment at the workplace and led to the establishment of Vishakha guidelines. The Shayara Bano vs. Union of India case dealt with the issue of triple talaq.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India, as it stands amended, includes which of the following?
1. Freedom of speech and expression
2. Assemble peaceably and without arms
3. To acquire and dispose property
4. To move freely throughout the territory of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 4 only
To acquire and dispose property was originally present in Article 19(1)(f). However, it was repealed by the 44th CA.

subject: Polity
question_text: By which one of the following Constitutional Amendment Acts, was the Article 21A (Right to Education) inserted into the Constitution of India? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) 86th Amendment Act
Article 21A provides free and compulsory education to children between the ages of 6 and 14 years. Also, Article 45 under DPSP states that the State shall provide free and compulsory education for all children until the age of 14.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements is correct? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Under Article 21A of the Constitution of India, the State shall provide free and compulsory education to all children up to the age of eleven years.
options.b: Every religious denomination has got absolute power under Article 26 of the Constitution of India to manage its own affairs in matters of religion.
options.c: Only religious and linguistic minorities find mention in Article 30 of the Constitution of India in reference to the right to establish educational institutions of their choice.
options.d: Parliament cannot empower, even by law, any court other than the Supreme Court of India the power to issue writs within local limits of its jurisdiction.

solution:
(C) Only religious and linguistic minorities find mention in Article 30 of the Constitution of India in reference to the right to establish educational institutions of their choice.
Option B is incorrect because Article 26 allows religious denominations to manage their own affairs subject to public order, morality, and health, meaning the power is not absolute. Option D is incorrect because the Parliament can authorize other courts to issue writs under certain circumstances.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following Fundamental Rights is not suspended when an Emergency is declared? [CDS II 2023]

options.a: Protection in respect of conviction for offences
options.c: Right to move freely throughout the territory of India

solution:
(A) Protection in respect of conviction for offences
For explanation, refer to question 38.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following conditions is/are necessary for the issue of a writ of certiorari in India?
1. There should be a tribunal or an officer having legal authority to determine questions affecting rights of subjects and having a duty to act judicially.
2. Such tribunal or officer has acted without jurisdiction.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CDS I 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
A writ of certiorari is issued by a Supreme court or High Court to quash the order of a lower court or tribunal due to an excess of jurisdiction, a lack of jurisdiction or a legal error. It is only issued against judicial and quasi judicial authorities and not the administrative ones.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by the action of a government authority.
2. Under Part III of the Constitution of India, individuals can enforce rights guaranteed by this Part when they are violated by not only the action of a government authority, but also by its inaction.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Individuals can enforce their FRs when they are violated by an action of a government body with the help of Article 32. The violation of FRs can occur also by the inaction of the State. Courts have recognized that inaction by the government such as failure to protect rights, can also be challenged.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following filed the Writ Petition that led to the famous verdict of the Supreme Court of India recognising the Right to Privacy as a Fundamental Right? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(C) Justice K.S. Puttaswamy
In this case, a nine judge bench of the Supreme Court held that privacy is a fundamental right under Article 21. There are 38 rights under this Article.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to B. R. Ambedkar, which one among the following Articles is the 'heart and soul' of the Constitution of India? [CDS I 2024]
solution:
(D) Article 32
Article 32 is the right to constitutional remedies which empowers the Supreme Court to protect fundamental rights.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following provides for the complete equality of men and women in India? [NDA I 2021]

options.a: Articles 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India
options.b: Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India
options.d: Article 20 of the Constitution of India

solution:
(A) Articles 14 and 15 of the Constitution of India
Article 14 - Equality before law and equal protection of law. Article 15 - Prohibition of discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a part of Fundamental Rights? [NDA II 2021]

options.b: Right to establish educational institutions by minorities
options.c: Right to be conferred with titles

solution:
(C) Right to be conferred with titles
Article 18 deals with abolition of titles under which no title except military or academic distinction shall be conferred by the state. It also prohibits Indian citizens from accepting any titles or honors from foreign governments without the President's consent. Article 17: Right against untouchability.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is a non-justiciable right? [NDA II 2021]

options.d: Right to life and personal liberty

solution:
(A) Right to adequate livelihood
The justiciable rights (Fundamental Rights in Part III) can be enforced in a court of law if violated while the Non - Justiciable rights (Directive Principles of State-Policy in Part IV) are not enforceable by courts but serve as guiding principles for the government to frame policies. The Right to adequate livelihood is mentioned in article 39(a) of the DPSP and is non justiciable. Articles 36-51 deal with the DPSP.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a writ? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(D) Severability
The five writs are - Habeas Corpus, Mandamus, Certiorari, Prohibition and Quo Warranto. For further explanation, refer to question 41.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements with regard to Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. Freedoms under this Article are not absolute
2. Reasonable restrictions can be imposed on the exercise of freedoms under this Article
Select the answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
For explanation, refer to question 7 of level 1.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Under Article 14 of the Constitution of India, the State is bound to impart equal treatment to dissimilarly situated persons.
2. The State can justify an unequal treatment between two similarly situated persons, if there is such a precedence of unequal treatment.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
Under Article 14, equals must be treated equally. Thus, if two persons are similarly situated, they must be treated equally unless there is a reasonable and valid classification backed by law.

subject: Polity
question_text: Barring exceptions, no law providing for preventive detention shall authorize the detention of a person for a period longer than [CBI 2023]
solution:
(C) Three months
It is mentioned under Article 22 which deals with Protection against arrest and detention in certain cases. (1) No person who is arrested shall be detained without being informed of the grounds for such arrest nor shall he be denied the right to consult a legal practitioner of his choice. (2) The arrested person shall be produced before the nearest magistrate within 24 hours of arrest. (3) The clauses (1) and (2) shall not apply to an enemy alien or a person under preventive detention. (4) The maximum period of preventive detention without Advisory Board approval is three months.

subject: Polity
question_text: Under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is the right to property a constitutional right? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(B) Article 300A
For explanation, refer to question 18 of level I.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements in terms of Article 14 of the Constitution of India is/are correct?
1. Article 14 would apply only when invidious discrimination is meted out to equals and similarly circumstanced without any rational basis.
2. Article 14 guarantees equality before the law or the equal protection of the laws within the territory of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Article 14 applies when equals are treated unequally without a rational basis. It also explicitly guarantees "equality before the law" and "equal protection of the law".

subject: Polity
question_text: A Writ of Prohibition is an order issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts to: [IAS 2024]

options.a: a government officer prohibiting him from taking a particular action.
options.b: the Parliament/Legislative Assembly to pass a law on Prohibition.
options.c: the lower court prohibiting continuation of proceedings in a case.
options.d: the Government prohibiting it from following an unconstitutional policy.

solution:
(C) The lower court prohibiting continuation of proceedings in a case.
A prohibition writ is a legal order issued by a higher court to prevent a lower court or tribunal from exceeding its jurisdiction or acting outside its legal authority. It aims to stop the lower court from making a decision in cases where it lacks proper jurisdiction. For explanation, refer to question 41 of level I.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not applicable to a writ of Habeas Corpus? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]

options.a: It is a powerful safeguard to citizens against arbitrary acts of the State.
options.b: It can be invoked for the enforcement of Fundamental Rights.
options.c: The writ may be addressed to any person, private or official, who has another person in his custody.
options.d: It secures the release of a person who has been imprisoned by a court of law on a criminal charge.

solution:
(D) It secures the release of a person who has been imprisoned by a court of law on a criminal charge.
Habeas Corpus cannot be used to secure the release of a person lawfully imprisoned by a court on a criminal charge. The writ is not issued where the : (a) detention is lawful, (b) the proceeding is for contempt of a legislature or a court, (c) detention is by a competent court & (d) detention is outside the jurisdiction of the court. Also, Habeas corpus can be issued against both public authorities as well as private individuals.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the position of the Right to Property in India? [IAS 2021]

options.a: Legal right available to citizens only
options.b: Legal right available to any person
options.c: Fundamental Right available to citizens only
options.d: Neither Fundamental Right nor legal right

solution:
(B) Legal right available to any person
The Right to Property is now a legal right placed under Article 300A of Part XII.

subject: Polity
question_text: A legislation which confers on the executive or administrative authority an unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power in the matter of application of law violates which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(A) Article 14
Article 14 guarantees the right to equality. It prohibits discriminatory laws and arbitrary state action. A legislation that confers unguided and uncontrolled discretionary power on the executive or administrative authority would be considered arbitrary and violative of Article 14, as it would allow for discriminatory application of the law.

subject: Polity
question_text: 'Right to Privacy' is protected under which Article of the Constitution of India? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(C) Article 21
The Right to Privacy was recognized as a fundamental right by the Supreme Court of India in the landmark case K.S. Puttaswamy v. Union of India (2017). Other important rights under Article 21 are : right to live with dignity, right to shelter, right to speedy trial.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following best defines the term 'State'? [IAS 2021]

options.a: A community of persons permanently occupying a definite territory independent of external control and possessing an organized government.
options.b: A politically organized people of a definite territory and possessing an authority to govern them, maintain law and order, protect their natural rights and safeguard their means of sustenance.
options.c: A number of persons who have been living in a definite territory for a very long time with their own culture, tradition and government.
options.d: A society permanently living in a definite territory with a central authority, an executive responsible to the central authority and an independent judiciary.

solution:
(A) A community of persons permanently occupying a definite territory independent of external control and possessing an organized government.
This definition encompasses the four essential elements of a state: 1) Population : A community of persons; 2) Territory : A definite territory; 3) Government : An organized government; 4) Sovereignty : Independent of external control.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements in respect of Bharat Ratna and Padma Awards:
1. Bharat Ratna and Padma Awards are titles under the Article 18(1) of the Constitution of India.
2. Padma Awards, which were instituted in the year 1954, were suspended only once.
3. The number of Bharat Ratna Awards is restricted to a maximum of five in a particular year.
Which of the above statements are not correct? [IAS 2021]
solution:
(D) 1, 2 and 3
1) In the Balaji Raghavan V Union of India, the Supreme Court ruled that the Bharat Ratna and Padma Awards are not "titles" under Article 18(1). Instead, they are awards given for distinguished service.
2) Padma awards, which were suspended twice - (once from 1977 to 1980 and again from 1993 to 1997).
3) The number of Bharat Ratna awards in a year is restricted to a maximum of three as per the official rule. However, the government can make exceptions by special decision (e.g. 4 awards were given in 1999 and 5 awards were given in 2024).

subject: Polity
question_text: In essence, what does 'Due Process of Law' mean? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(C) Fair application of law
It is closely linked to the "due process of law" as it emphasizes the need for fairness, impartiality, and the opportunity to present a defense. The due process of law means that a law must be reasonable, fair and just; mere procedural validity is not enough whereas the procedure established by law means that a law is valid if enacted following the prescribed procedure regardless of its fairness.

subject: Polity
question_text:
As per Article 12 of the Indian Constitution, 'State' includes:
1. Parliament of India
2. State Governments
3. State Legislatures
4. Local authorities
Code: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) 1, 2, 3, and 4
Article 12 serves as the gateway to Part III of the Constitution, defining the term 'State' as used throughout the Fundamental Rights provisions. It includes Parliament of India, State Governments, State Legislatures, Local authorities.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the parts of the Indian Constitution deals with the Directive Principle of State Policy? [SSC GD 20/02/2024]
solution:
(C) Part IV
The DPSP (Article 36 - 51) comes under Part IV. It is borrowed from Ireland. The provisions contained in this part shall not be enforceable by any court but the principles laid are fundamental in the governance of the country and it shall be the duty of the state to apply these principles in making laws. Part I - The Union and its Territory, Part II - Citizenship, Part III - Fundamental Rights.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following are enumerated in Part IV of the Indian Constitution, from Articles 36 to 51? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Directive Principles of State Policy
The Directive Principles of state policy are not justiciable in nature but they are fundamental in the governance of the country. BR Ambedkar described the DPSP as the Novel Feature of the Constitution. They are broadly categorized into 3 categories - Gandhian, Socialist and Liberal intellectual.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following features of the Indian Constitution was borrowed from Irish Constitution? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Directive Principles of State Policy
Concurrent List - Australia, Fundamental Rights - USA, Rule of Law - UK. AV Dicey gave the concept of the Rule of Law.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Directive Principle of State Policy strives to make India _______. [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(B) A welfare state
The DPSP seeks to establish a welfare state and aims to attain a social and economic democracy. The 42nd CA added Articles 39A and 43A which come under socialist principles and also Article 48A which is a part of liberal intellectual principles.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following has similarity to Article 29(1) of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights which states "everyone has duties to the community in which alone the free and full development of his personality is possible"? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Fundamental Duties
The Fundamental Duties in the Indian Constitution were borrowed from the Constitution of the USSR (Soviet Union). The idea of Fundamental Duties was added by the 42nd Amendment Act of 1976.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is a feature of Directive Principles of State Policy, and also distinguishes it from Fundamental Rights? [SSC CHSL 10/07/2024]

options.d: It protects the interest of the individual.

solution:
(A) It is non-justiciable
The provisions of DPSP are not enforceable by courts but serve as guiding principles for governance. In contrast, Fundamental Rights are justiciable, though not absolute.

subject: Polity
question_text: In India which of the following is a Gandhian principle of Directive Principles of State Policy? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]

options.d: To provide equal pay for equal work

solution:
(B) To organise village panchayat
Article 40 - To organize village panchayat. Article 50- to separate judiciary from executive, Article 44 - to secure uniform civil code, Article 39(d) - equal pay for equal work.

subject: Polity
question_text: "The State shall take steps to separate the judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State." This is mentioned under ______ of Constitution. [SSC GD 24/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Part IV
Article 50 deals with the separation of judiciary and executive. Articles 36-51 come under Part IV (DPSP). Part IVA deals with the Fundamental Duties and Part V deals with the provisions of the Union.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which year were four new Directive Principles added to the original list? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 1976
In the 42nd CA, 1976; four new DPSPs were added. They were: 1) Equal justice and free legal aid to the poor - Article 39A. 2) Participation of workers in management of industries - Article 43A. 3) Children are given opportunities to develop in a healthy manner and in conditions of freedom and dignity - Article 39(f). 4) Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests and wildlife - Article 48A.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following statements are correct about Directive Principles of State Policy of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]

options.b: They have moral and political sanctions.
options.c: They promote the welfare of the rich.

solution:
(D) They are positive in nature.
The DPSPs are positive in nature because they direct the government to take action for the welfare of the people. DPSPs guide the state towards social and economic development.

subject: Polity
question_text: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar described Directive Principles of State Policy as ______ of the Indian constitution. [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]

options.c: The most precious part of the constitution
options.d: a novel feature of the constitution

solution:
(D) A novel feature of the constitution
Other commentaries: 1) Granville Austin described DPSP as the 'Conscience of the Constitution'. 2) Sir BN Rau described it as moral precepts for the authorities of the state.

subject: Polity
question_text: ______ Directive principles of State Policy were added to the Indian Constitution by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act of 1976. [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Four
For explanation, refer to question 9.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles from Directive Principles of state policy mentions about organization of Village Panchayats? [SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 40
Article 38 - State to secure a social order for the promotion of welfare of the people. Article 42 - Promotion of just and humane conditions of work and maternity relief. Article 36 - Definition of "State" which is also mentioned in Article 12.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution mentions that 'The State shall, in particular, direct its policy towards securing that the ownership and control of the material resources of the community are so distributed as best to subserve the common good'? [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 39(b)
Article 39 - Certain principles of policy to be followed by the State: (a) adequate means of livelihood; (b) Equal distribution of resources; (c) Prevention of concentration of wealth; (d) Equal pay for equal work; (e) Health and strength of workers, men and women, and the tender age of children are not abused; (f) children are given opportunities to develop in a healthy manner and in conditions of freedom and dignity. Article 39A - Equal justice and free legal aid to the poor.

subject: Polity
question_text: Generally, Directive Principles are _______. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(B) positive directions
DPSPs are instruments of instructions for the State that help the government establish social and economic democracy. They also serve as beacon lights to the court as mentioned by the former attorney general MC Setalvad.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Directive Principle of State Policy in the Constitution are primarily aimed at achieving: [SSC GD 24/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Social and economic justice
This objective is clearly stated in Article 38, which directs the State to promote a social order based on justice-social, economic and political.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides that the State must secure a social order to promote the welfare of people? [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 38
Article 36 - Definition of "State". Article 37 - DPSPs are non-justiciable.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions that 'The State shall endeavour to promote cottage industries on an individual or co-operative basis in rural areas'? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 43
Article 42 - Just and humane conditions of work and maternity relief. Article 43 - The State shall endeavour to secure to all workers, agricultural, industrial or otherwise, work, a living wage, conditions of work ensuring a decent standard of life and full enjoyment of leisure and social and cultural opportunities. It also promotes cottage industries in rural areas. Article 43A - Participation of workers in management of industries. Article 43B - Promotion of co-operative societies. Article 44 - deals with Uniform Civil Code. Article 49 - Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance.

subject: Polity
question_text: What was the main objection against the Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constituent Assembly? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]

options.a: They were non justiciable in character
options.b: They were considered as novel features
options.c: The state was responsible for these policies
options.d: They were an instrument of instructions

solution:
(A) They were non justiciable in character
However, Article 31C introduced by the 25th CA, 1971 provides that no law that seeks to implement Article 39(b) and 39(c), shall be void on the ground of contravention of Fundamental Rights under Article 14 or Article 19.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a part of the Directive Principles of State Policy of the Constitution of India? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]

options.a: Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests and wildlife
options.b: Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance
options.c: Provision for early childhood care and education to children below the age of six years
options.d: Provision for protection of minority regions in Rajasthan

solution:
(D) Provision for protection of minority regions in Rajasthan
Article 48A - Protection and improvement of environment, Article 49 - Protection of monuments, Provision for early childhood care and Article 45 - education to children below the age of six.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions about the 'Promotion of educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and other weaker sections'? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 46
Article 48 - Organization of agriculture and animal husbandry. Article 47 - To raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living and to improve public health, to prohibit the consumption of intoxicating drinks and drugs.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution provides for protecting and improving the environment and safeguarding forests and wildlife? [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 48-A
Article 48A is a Liberal - Intellectual principle.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which directive principle was added by the 97th Amendment Act 2011? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]

options.c: Uniform civil code for the citizens

solution:
(B) Promotion of co-operative societies
The 97th CA, deals with co-operative societies and added Article 19(a)(c) and Article 43B (promotion of co-operative societies). It also added Part IXB (Article 243ZH to 243ZT) to the constitution. Part IX - Panchayats, Part IXA - Municipalities.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution mentions that the State shall maintain just and honorable relations between nations? [SSC GD 28/02/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 51
Article 51 (a)- Promotion of international peace and security. (b)- maintain just and honourable relations between nations. (c) foster respect for international law and treaty obligations. (d) encourage settlement of international disputes by arbitration. The Fundamental Duties are contained in Part IV from Article 36-51.

subject: Polity
question_text: In a situation wherein A (female) and B (male) are working in the same office, same designation, same nature of work but unequal payment, which of the following Articles of the Constitution will be applicable? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 39(d)
In the case of unequal payment for work of the same nature, Article 39(d) which deals with equal pay for equal work is applicable.

subject: Polity
question_text: Biological Diversity Act 2002 was passed by the Government of India. It fulfilled ideals of which article of Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution of India? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 48A
Other important environmental laws based on the ideals of Article 48A include : The Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 - for the protection of wild animals, birds, and plants. The Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 - for the protection and improvement of the overall environment. The Forest Conservation Act, 1980 - for the conservation of forests and regulating deforestation. The Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981 and the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974 - for controlling pollution.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following does NOT come under Directive Principle of State Policy? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]

options.c: Promotion of the welfare of the people

solution:
(B) Promotion of Indian Local Languages
Article 43 - Promotion of Cottage industries , Article 38 - Promotion of the welfare of the people, Article 44 - Uniform Civil Code.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 43: The State shall endeavor to promote cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas, it is a Directive based on ______ principles. [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Gandhian
Promotion of cottage industries on an individual or cooperative basis in rural areas is based on Gandhian principle. Article 43 also deals with provisions like securing a living wage, decent standard of living, full enjoyment of leisure and social and cultural opportunities which are based on the Socialist principles. Other Gandhian Principle articles are - 40, 43, 43B , 46, 47 and 48.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles provides for 'equal justice and free legal aid'? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 39A
Article 43A - Participation of workers in management of industries. Article 39 - certain principles of policy to be followed by the state: (1) adequate means of livelihood, (2) equal distribution of material resources, (3) prevent concentration of wealth, (4) equal pay for equal work, (5) health and strength of workers, (6) children are given facilities to develop in a healthy manner and in conditions of freedom, dignity and without exploitation.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the aim of Article 44 of the Indian Constitution? [SSC MTS 01/09/2023]

options.a: To protect every monument or place of artistic or historic interest.
options.b: To separate judiciary from the executive in the public services of the State.
options.c: To protect and improve the environment and safeguard the forests and wildlife of the country.
options.d: To secure for the citizen, a uniform civil code through the territory of India.

solution:
(D) To secure for the citizen, a uniform civil code through the territory of India.
Uttarakhand is the first state to implement UCC.

subject: Polity
question_text: Under which Article of the Directive Principles of State Policy, a working woman on maternity leave, must receive all maternity benefits from the employer? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Article 42
Article 42 and 43 are based on socialist principles while the Article 44 and 45 are based on the liberal - intellectual principles.

subject: Polity
question_text: Fundamental Duties have _______ social and economic significance. [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(A) Ethical
Fundamental Duties have significant ethical importance, as they emphasize the moral obligations of citizens towards the state and society.

subject: Polity
question_text: The government scheme, Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana aims for crop insurance service for farmers. This type of government efforts falls under _______ of the Directive Principles of the Indian Constitution. [SSC CHSL 07/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 48
It provides for the organization of agriculture and animal husbandry and prohibits the slaughter of cattle. It is based on the Gandhian ideology.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mandates that the state should protect every monument or place of artistic or historic interest? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 49
Article 49 is a liberal - intellectual principle.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India provides early childhood care and education for all children until they complete the age of six years? [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 45
The 86th CA, 2002 made elementary education (6-14 years) a fundamental right under Article 21A and modified Article 45 to provide early childhood care and education (0-6 years) and added the 11th fundamental duty under Article 51A (k) stating that it is the duty of every parent/guardian to provide education to their child aged 6 to 14 years.

subject: Polity
question_text: The working conditions also include prevention of sexual harassment of working women and making arrangements in the organisation for it, this falls under _______. [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(B) Article 42
Article 42 makes provision for securing just and humane conditions of work and for maternity relief thereby emphasizing safety and welfare measures including protection against workplace harassment. Also recall Article 23 and 24 - Right against Exploitation.

subject: Polity
question_text: Poshan Abhiyaan is a programme to achieve nutritional status of children from 0 to 6 years. Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides for raising the nutrition level? [SSC CHSL 14/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Article 47
Article 47 - Duty of the State to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living and to improve public health. Article 45- to provide early childhood care and education (0-6years).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which is NOT a Directive Principle to be followed by the State for policy making? [SSC CHSL TIER-II 10/01/2024]

options.a: Tender age of children is not abused
options.c: The operation of economic system results in concentration of wealth
options.d: The ownership of material resources of the community are for common good

solution:
(C) The operation of economic system results in the concentration of wealth.
Article 39(c) instead directs the State to ensure that the economic system does not result in the concentration of wealth, but promotes fairness. Article 39(e) - tender age of children is not abused and that the children are given opportunities to develop in a healthy manner. Article 39(b) - The ownership and control of the material resources of the community are so distributed as best to subserve the common good. Article 39(c) actually directs the state to prevent the concentration of wealth.

subject: Polity
question_text: Fundamental duties are _______ and not enforceable by law but are taken into account by the courts while adjudicating any matter. [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Statutory
In 1992, in the case of Minerva Mills Ltd. v. Union of India, the Supreme Court observed that while Fundamental Duties are not justiciable, they are still significant in guiding the interpretation of laws. The National Honours Act enforces Fundamental Duty under Article 51A(a) by providing a legal framework to ensure respect for national symbols.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following does not come under the Fundamental Duties in the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]

options.a: Safeguard public property and to abjure violence
options.b: Cherish and follow the noble ideals that inspired the national struggle for freedom
options.d: Uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India

solution:
(C) Exercise adult franchise
The 61st Constitutional Amendment, 1988 reduced the age of voting from 21 years to 18 years.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle based on the ideals of Mahatma Gandhi? [SSC MTS 08/09/2023]

options.a: To promote industrialisation in urban areas
options.c: To prohibit intoxicating drinks and drugs that are injurious to health
options.d: To promote cottage industries in rural areas

solution:
(A) To promote industrialisation in urban areas
Gandhian principles in DPSP focus on rural development, self sufficiency, upliftment of small-scale industries and social welfare.

subject: Polity
question_text: The directive principles are largely classified into three broad categories. Which of the following is NOT part of this classification? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(D) Authoritative
The DPSP are classified into three categories: 1) Socialist Principles - Aim at achieving social and economic justice [Article 39 (a), (b), (c) and (d), Article 39A, 41, 43, 45]. 2) Gandhian Principles - Reflect Mahatma Gandhi's vision of rural development and self sufficiency. [Article 40, 43, 46, 47, 48]. 3) Liberal - intellectual Principles- Focus on legal and administrative matters [Article 44, 48A, 50, 51].

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT about the Directive Principles of State Policy? [SSC GD 10/09/2024]

options.a: They promote the welfare of individuals. Hence, they are personal and individualistic.
options.b: These have moral and political sanctions.
options.c: They aim at establishing social and economic democracy in the country.
options.d: They are positive, as they require the State to do certain things.

solution:
(A) They promote the welfare of individuals. Hence, they are personal and individualistic.
DPSPs promote collective welfare, not just individual interests. They focus on social justice, economic equality, and community well being rather than personal benefits.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to the Directive Principles of State policy contained in the Constitution of India, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Article 37 of the Constitution of India provides that the Directive Principles of State Policy are enforceable in the High Courts only.
2. Article 38 provides that the State shall strive to minimise the inequalities in income amongst individuals and groups of people.
3. Article 39 provides that the State shall direct its policy towards securing that there is equal pay for equal work for both men and women.
[SSC CHSL 09/07/2024]
solution:
(D) Only 2 and 3
Article 37 provides that the DPSP shall not be enforceable by any court. Thus statement 1 is incorrect. Article 38 provides that the state shall endeavour to promote the welfare of the people and secure a social order. Also, the State shall strive to minimise the inequalities in income amongst individuals and groups of people. This provision was added by the 44th CA,1978.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following statements is not correct about the Directive Principles of State Policy? [SSC MTS 14/09/2023]

options.a: To ensure the above mentioned employment and clean working environment for men, women and children.
options.b: Providing adequate means of livelihood for both men and women
options.c: Ensuring equal remuneration for equal work only for men
options.d: Reorganizing the economic system to prevent concentration of wealth in a few hands

solution:
(C) Ensuring equal remuneration for equal work only for men
Article 39(d) directs the State to ensure equal pay for equal work for both men and women.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which was the first country to incorporate Fundamental Duties in its Constitution? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
solution:
(B) USSR
The USSR was the first country to incorporate the Fundamental Duties. Later, the concept was borrowed by India from the USSR.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT correct about Directive Principles of State Policy? [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]

options.a: They ensure social and economic development.
options.c: They provide guidelines for the governance of the country.
options.d: They are morally binding on the government.

solution: (B) They are justiciable in nature.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a Directive Principle State Policy mentioned in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]

options.a: Organization of agriculture and animal husbandry
options.b: Uniform Civil Code for the citizens
options.d: Promotion of international peace and security

solution:
(C) Separation of legislature from executive
Article 50 provides for the separation of judiciary from executive.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution contains Fundamental Duties? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 51A
Part IVA (Article 51A) deals with the Fundamental Duties. They were not part of the constitution originally. Instead, a total of 10 Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd CA, 1976. Later the 11th FD was added by the 86th CA, 2002.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Committee identified the existence of legal provisions for implementation of some of the Fundamental Duties? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Justice Verma Committee
The Justice Verma Committee set up in 1999 identified the existence of legal provisions for the implementation of some of the Fundamental Duties. The committee found that many existing laws such as the Prevention of Insults to National Honour Act (1971) and the Protection of Civil Rights Act (1955), already helped enforce some of these duties. Justice Mulla Committee - Focused on prison reforms. Justice Sapre Committee - Worked on road safety. Justice VR Krishna Iyer Committee - Addressed women prisoners' welfare and electoral reforms.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article in the fundamental duty applies, when a construction worker takes his 8-year son to the site rather than sending him to school? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 51A(k)
It is the 11th duty added by the 86th CA. It provides that it is the duty of every parent or guardian to provide opportunities for education to their child between the ages of six and fourteen years.

subject: Polity
question_text: Swaran Singh Committee was established by the government under which Prime Minister in 1976 to make recommendations regarding fundamental duties? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Indira Gandhi
Then President - Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed

subject: Polity
question_text: To cherish and follow the noble ideals that inspired our national struggle for freedom is a _______ fundamental duty in the list of fundamental duties enshrined in our Indian Constitution. [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Second
[Article 51A(a)] - To abide by the constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem. [Article 51A(b)] - To cherish and follow the noble ideals that inspired our national struggle for freedom. [Article 51A(c)] - To uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India. [Article 51A(d)] - To defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so. [Trick - A (Anthem), B (Bapu) - freedom struggle, C (3 terms), D (defend), E (equality) - common brotherhood, F (Fort) - composite culture, G (Greenery), H (Humanism), I (eye for an eye) - abjure violence and property, J (Jadeja) - all sphere excellence].

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following committees was responsible for making recommendations that led to the 42nd Constitutional Amendment for the declaration of Fundamental Duties? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Swaran Singh Committee
Conclusively, 10 Fundamental Duties were added in the 42nd CA, 1976. The 11th FD was later added by the 86th CA. Abid Hussain Committee - Worked on Trade and Industrial Policies. Khusro Committee - Focused on Agriculture and Rural Credit. Raja Chelliah Committee - Tax Reforms.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following duties has been NOT prescribed by the Indian Constitution as Fundamental Duties?
1. To defend the country
2. To pay income tax
3. To cast the vote in election
4. To safeguard the public property
[SSC CGL 17/09/2024]
solution:
(C) 2 and 3 both
Article 51A(d) - To defend the country; Article 51A(i) - To safeguard public property and to abjure violence. The Sardar Swaran committee recommended to add the fundamental duty to pay income taxes, but it was not included.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article in the fundamental duty is invoked when you see in a private party that the Indian flag has been used as a covering for the speaker's desk? [SSC CGL 23/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 51A(a)
For explanation, refer question 53.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which of the following Articles is Fundamental duties, like protecting public property and renouncing violence, stated? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 51A
It is mentioned in Article 51A(i) - To safeguard public property and to abjure violence.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many items did the Swaran Singh Committee recommend to be incorporated in the Constitution of India as duties of the Indian citizen? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(B) 8
The Swaran Singh Committee originally recommended 8 Fundamental Duties, but only 5 of these recommendations were accepted and included.

subject: Polity
question_text: For strengthening the fundamental duties, the Verma Committee identified a few existing acts by which a proper implementation of such duties can be accomplished. Which of the following was NOT referred by him? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Wildlife Segregation Act,1970
The Justice Verma Committee recommended ways to strengthen the implementation of Fundamental Duties. The acts referred to by the committee include: 1) Representation of People Act, 1951; 2) Protection of Civil Rights Act, 1955; 3) Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act, 1967.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT correct regarding Fundamental Duties mentioned in the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]

options.a: Fundamental Duties are mentioned in Article 51A in the Indian Constitution
options.b: The Constitution (Eighty-Sixth Amendment) Act, 2002 is related to the Fundamental Duties.
options.c: The Constitution (Forty-second Amendment) Act, 1976 is related to the Fundamental Duties
options.d: Fundamental Duties are mentioned in PART VA in the Indian Constitution

solution:
(D) Fundamental Duties are mentioned in Part VA in the Indian Constitution
Fundamental Duties are mentioned in Part IV A of the constitution under Article 51A added by the 42nd CA, 1976.

subject: Polity
question_text: As of 2024, when was the last fundamental duty added to Article 51A? [SSC GD 26/02/2024]
solution:
(B) 2002
The 11th Fundamental Duty [51A (k)] was added by the 86th CA, 2002.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles says that 'It shall be the duty of every citizen of India who is a parent or guardian to provide opportunities for education to his child or, as the case may be, ward between the age of six and fourteen years'? [SSC CHSL 02/08/2023]

subject: Polity
question_text: A father was speaking about the rich cultural heritage of India to his young son and his teenager friends. The fundamental duty in which Article was the father promoting? [SSC CHSL 08/08/2023]
solution:
(A) Article 51A(f)
Article 51A(f) - to value and preserve the rich heritage of our composite culture. Article 51A(e) - to promote harmony and the spirit of common brotherhood amongst all the people of India, to renounce practices derogatory to the dignity of women. Article 51A(d) - To defend the country and render national service when called upon to do so. Article 51A(c) - To uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity, and integrity of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles about fundamental duties is related to the respect of the National Anthem? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 51A (a)
For explanation, refer question 53.

subject: Polity
question_text: Fundamental duties were not placed in Part III of the Indian Constitution because Part III is _______. [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Justiciable
The Part IV is non justiciable and so is the Part IVA which contains the Fundamental Duties.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution mentions the fundamental duty "sovereignty, unity and integrity of India" is mentioned? [SSC MTS 16/06/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 51A (c)
Article 51A(c) - to uphold and protect the sovereignty, unity and integrity of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution Review Commission chaired by _______ recommended some initiatives to be taken by the government for successful implementation of Fundamental Duties. [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution: (B) MN Venkatachaliah

subject: Polity
question_text: How many fundamental duties of citizens have been enumerated by the 42nd Amendment of the Constitution, adopted in 1976? [SSC JE 07/06/2024]
solution:
(B) 10
10 Fundamental Duties were originally added by the 42nd CA, 1976.

subject: Polity
question_text: The committee to 'Operationalise the Suggestions to Teach Fundamental Duties to the Citizens of the Country' is popularly known as the _______. [SSC CHSL 10/08/2023]
solution:
(D) JS Verma Committee
It was appointed by the then Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee. For further explanation, refer to question 50.

subject: Polity
question_text: Disrespecting of National Flag is a violation of which of the following Fundamental Duties mentioned in the Indian Constitution? [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 51A (a)
Article 51A (a) - to abide by the constitution and respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag and the National Anthem.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Directive Principles of State Policy is not correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: They are not enforceable in a court of law.
options.b: They can override all the fundamental rights.
options.c: They can be related to social and economic justice.
options.d: They can be related to giving free legal aid

solution:
(B) They can override all the fundamental rights.
Fundamental Rights Vs DPSP: 1) Champakam Dorairajan Case (1951) - Fundamental Rights would prevail over DPSP and that the FRs are amendable. 2) Golaknath Case (1967) - The Supreme Court held that the Parliament cannot take away the FRs. After this the Parliament made the 24th CA giving it the powers to amend FRs and the 25th CA which held that no law which sought to implement Article 39 (b) & (c) shall be declared null and void on the grounds of Article 14 and Article 19. It also held that no such law shall be questioned in any court. 3) Kesavananda Bharati Case (1973) - It held that Parliament can amend the Fundamental Rights however it cannot disturb the Basic Structure of the Constitution. 4) Minerva Mill Case - The Supreme Court held that the Indian Constitution is found on the bedrock of the balance between FR and DPSP.

subject: Polity
question_text: Under the Indian Constitution, concentration of wealth violates [IAS 2021]

options.b: the Directive Principles of State Policy

solution:
(B) The Directive Principles of State Policy
It comes under Article 39(c).

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following considered the Directive Principles of State Policy as aiming at 'furthering the goals of social exclusion'? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(B) Granville Austin
His book "The Indian Constitution: Cornerstone of a Nation" highlights how the DPSPs were designed to shape India into a welfare state.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a part of Article 51 of the Constitution of India? [CDS I 2023]

options.b: Maintain just and honourable relations between nations.
options.c: Use of UN peacekeeping force for resolution of international disputes.
options.d: Encourage settlement of inter-national disputes by arbitration.

solution:
(C) Use of UN peacekeeping force for resolution of international disputes.
For explanation, refer to question 24.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The Fundamental Duties are enlisted in Part IV-A of the Constitution of India.
2. The 44th Amendment to the Constitution of India added the word 'Secular' to the Preamble of the Constitution of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(A) 1 only
It was the 42nd CA that added the word "Secular", "Integrity" and "Socialist". The Objective Resolution was introduced on 13th December 1946 by Jawaharlal Nehru in the Constituent Assembly and adopted on 22nd January, 1947 laying down the foundational principles for the Preamble.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following provisions of the Constitution of India was not adopted from the British Constitution? [GEOSCIENTIST 2022]
solution:
(B) Directive Principles of State Policy
DPSPs were adopted from the Irish constitution, which indeed adopted the same from the constitution of Spain.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which statements are correct as per the Indian Constitution?
1. The 86th Amendment added the Right to Education to Directive Principles.
2. Right to Work and Right to Education are both Fundamental Rights and Directive Principles.
Code: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Under the 86th Amendment Act of 2002, the Right to Education became a Fundamental Right (Article 21A in Part III) for children aged 6-14, and Article 45 in Part IV (Directive Principles) was amended concerning early childhood education. The Right to Work is mentioned in Part IV as a Directive Principle, not as a Fundamental Right.

subject: Polity
question_text: Under which Article of the Indian Constitution shall the executive power of the Union be vested in the President? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 53
It states that the executive power of the Union shall be vested in the President (Article 52). Also, the supreme command of the Defence Forces of the Union shall be vested in the President. Article 55 - Manner of election of President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India expressly provides that the President is eligible for re-election? [SSC CGL 11/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 57
The President can be re-elected any number of times. Naturalized citizens can become President. Article 59 outlines the conditions of the President's office, stating that the President cannot be a member of any legislature or hold any other office of profit, and is entitled to official residence, salary and privileges which cannot be reduced during the term.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian constitution talks about Oath or affirmation by the President? [SSC MTS 08/05/2023]
solution:
(B) Article 60
The President shall subscribe an oath in the presence of the Chief Justice of India, or in his absence, the senior most judge of the Supreme Court available. Article 72 empowers the President of India to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment, particularly in cases involving offences under Union laws, court-martial judgments, and death sentences.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article deals with the election of the President? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 54
It states that the President who is the de jure head, shall be elected by an electoral college which consists of all the elected (MPs + MLAs), using the single transferable vote system and secret ballot to ensure proportional representation and confidentiality. Nominated members do not take part in the election of the President but they take part in the process of impeachment (Article 61) of the President meanwhile the MLAs do not.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which part of the Constitution of India contains the provisions of the Union Executive? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(A) Part V
Part V deals with the Union (Article 52 to 151). It consists of five chapters : Chapter I - The Executive; Chapter II - Parliament; Chapter III - Legislative powers of the President; Chapter IV - The Union Judiciary; Chapter V - CAG. Part III - Fundamental Rights, Part IV- DPSP, Part VI- The States.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Union Executive? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 52 to 78
The articles 52-78 under the Part V (Chapter I) deal with the Union Executive.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution mentions that "The President shall, notwithstanding the expiration of his/her term, continue to hold office until his successor enters upon his/her office? [SSC PHASE XII 25/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 56
Article 56 states that the President shall hold the office for a term of five years. It also states that the President can give his resignation to the Vice President. Article 61- Impeachment of the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions that an election to fill a vacancy caused by the expiration of the term of office of the President shall be completed before the expiration of the term? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 62
Article 62 - Time of holding election to fill vacancy in the office of President. The election shall be completed before the expiration of the term of the President. In case of death, resignation or removal, the election to fill the vacancy shall be held within 6 months at maximum.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India mentions that any person to become the President of India has to be a citizen of India? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 58
Article 58 - Qualification for election as President. (a) Citizen of India; (b) has completed the age of 35 years; (c) should be qualified to be appointed as a member of Lok Sabha; (d) should not hold office of profit.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which of the following Articles of the Indian constitution is the procedure for impeachment of the President of India mentioned? [SSC CHSL 13/03/2023]
solution:
(D) Article 61
Article 61 deals with the impeachment of the President. The only ground for the impeachment is the "violation of the Constitution". All MPs (Elected + Nominated) take part in the procedure of impeachment.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 57 of the Indian Constitution states that _______. [SSC MTS 06/10/2021]

options.a: A person who holds, or who has held, office as President shall, subject to the other provisions of this Constitution, be eligible for re-election to that office.
options.b: The executive power of the Union shall be vested in the President and shall be exercised by him either directly or through officers subordinate to him in accordance with this Constitution.
options.c: The President shall hold office for a term of Five years from the date on which he enters upon the office.
options.d: The President shall be elected by the members of an electoral college consisting of - (A) the members of both houses of Parliament; and (B) the elected members of the legislative assemblies of the States.

solution:
(A) A person who holds, or who has held, office as President shall, subject to the other provisions of this Constitution, be eligible for re-election to that office.
Maximum serving President : Dr Rajendra Prasad. Also, Dr Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan served as Vice-President twice.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to Article 58 of the Constitution of India, no person shall be eligible for election as President unless he is a citizen of India, has completed the age of ______ and is qualified for election as a member of the House of the People. [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 35 years
The age qualification required to be a member of the Lok Sabha is 25 years.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following has given extraordinary powers during an emergency? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(B) The President
During an emergency, the President can: suspend fundamental rights (except Article 20 and 21), take over state governments under President's Rule (Article 356), extend the Parliament's term beyond 5 years, control financial matters under Article 360. Article 359 - Suspension of FR during emergencies.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution lays the condition that the President, once elected, shall NOT be a member of either the House of Parliament or state legislature? [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Article 59
Article 59 deals with the Conditions of the President's office. It states that the President shall not be the member of either House of the Parliament or a House of the State Legislature, and if a member be elected President, he shall be deemed to have vacated his seat before being appointed President. It also states that the emoluments and allowances shall not be diminished during his term of office.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct regarding removal of the President of India? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
A. The impeachment process can be initiated in either House of Parliament.
B. The impeachment charges must be signed by the one-third members of the House in which the process is initiated.
C. In the impeachment process, a 30 days' notice should be given to the President.
D. The impeachment resolution is passed by a majority of two-thirds of the total membership of that House.
solution:
(D) A and D only
The charge shall be preferred in either Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha. A 14 days prior notice signed by not less than 1/4th of the total members of the house has to be given before moving the resolution. The motion must be passed by a two-thirds majority of the total membership (Special Majority) of that House. If passed, the motion moves to the other House for investigation of the charges. The President has the right to defend themselves. If the second House also passes the motion by a two-thirds majority of the total membership, the President is removed from the office.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who, being the Commander-in-Chief of the Indian Armed Forces, takes the salute of different regiments of the armed forces during the march-past on Republic Day? [SSC CGL 25/07/2023]
solution:
(D) The President of India
As per Article 53, the President is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces. Trick to learn articles related to the President - PREEt TREQ COIT. (Pr - President, E - Executive, E - Election, M - Manner of election, T - Term, Re-Reelection, Q - Qualification, C - Conditions, O - oath, I - impeachment, T - Time of holding election).

subject: Polity
question_text: When the President of India keeps a bill (except money bill and constitutional amendment bill) pending for an indefinite period, it is called _______. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Pocket veto of the President
The President has three types of veto powers under Article 111: a) Absolute Veto - The President rejects the bill completely. b) Suspensive Veto - Returning the bill to Parliament for reconsideration. If both Houses pass the bill again without any changes, the President must give assent. c) Pocket Veto - No action is taken on the bill indefinitely. Unlike the US President (who has 10 days to act), there is no time limit for the Indian President to act on the bill. Because of this, the pocket veto of the Indian President is bigger than that of the American President. Giani Zail Singh was the first President to use the pocket veto in 1986 on the Indian Post Office (Amendment) Bill.

subject: Polity
question_text: The President of India decides on the question as to the disqualification of Members of Parliament in consultation with the _______. [SSC CPO 04/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Election Commission of India
Article 103 states that if any question arises regarding the disqualification of an MP, the President takes the final decision after consultation with the Election Commission of India. The ECI's opinion is binding on the President.

subject: Polity
question_text:
The President of India shall be elected by the members of an electoral college consisting of:
I. The elected members of both Houses of Parliament.
II. The elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of the States.
[SSC MTS 03/05/2023]
solution:
(A) Both I and II
Article 54 states that the President is elected by an electoral college consisting of Elected (MLAs + MPs). Article 55 deals with the manner of election of the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following Presidents of India was also the deputy chairman of the Planning Commission? [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(D) K.R. Narayanan
He served as the 9th President of India. He also held the office of Vice President (1997-2002) and Deputy Chairman of the Planning Commission.

subject: Polity
question_text: The system based on individual leadership is a _______ system. [SSC CGL 03/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Presidential
Examples - USA, Brazil, Mexico. In the USA, the President serves a 4 year term and can only serve a maximum of two terms.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which is NOT correct about the President of India? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]

options.a: The President holds office for a period of five years.
options.b: He is a part of the Parliament.
options.c: Every bill passed by the Parliament goes to the President for his assent before it becomes a law.
options.d: He has veto power by which he can withhold or refuse to give assent to Bills including Money Bill passed by the Parliament.

solution:
(D) He has veto power by which he can withhold or refuse to give assent to Bills including Money Bill passed by the Parliament.
A Money Bill (Article 110) must be given assent by the President and cannot be rejected or returned since Money Bills are brought with the prior recommendation of the President itself.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who administers the Union Territories of India? [SSC MTS 19/06/2023]
solution:
(A) President
As per Article 239 under Part VIII (The Union Territories), the UTs in India are administered by the President. The President appoints an Administrator or Lieutenant Governor (LG) to govern the UT on their behalf. The other appointments that the President makes are of the Attorney General (Article 76), CEC and ECs (Article 324), UPSC / JPSC chairman (Article 316).

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct regarding the benefits of the retired President of India? [SSC CGL TIER II 02/03/2023]
I. The former President of India will also be entitled to a monthly pension of Rs. 2.5 lakh.
II. He/She is entitled to a secretarial staff comprising a private secretary, a personal assistant, an additional private secretary, two peons, and office expenses up to Rs. 1 lakh per annum.
III. He/She is also entitled to free highest-class travel anywhere in India, accompanied by one person, by rail, air, or steamer.
solution:
(D) A, B and C
The President's Emoluments and Pension Act, 1951 entitles these benefits to a retired President of India. The salary and emoluments of the President cannot be diminished during his term of office.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following is the 'Visitor' to all central universities of India? [SSC MTS 14/10/2021]

options.c: Union Minister of Human Resources Development

solution:
(A) President of India
He has the authority to oversee and ensure proper functioning, appoint committees for university inspections, and address issues related to academic and administrative matters. Also, the Governor, in many states, acts as the Chancellor of state universities.

subject: Polity
question_text: The President of India has the same powers and functions as _______? [SSC MTS 22/08/2019]
solution:
(D) Queen of Britain
The President of India functions as a Nominal head, similar to the British Monarch. Both hold the De Jure position and act on the advice of the Council of Ministers. Article 74 of the Indian Constitution provides for a Council of Ministers that acts on the aid and advice of the President. In contrast, the President of the USA, China and Turkey have executive powers, making them more powerful in governance compared to the Indian President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who nominates members to the Rajya Sabha based on their excellence in literature, science, art and social service? [SSC MTS 06/09/2023]

options.c: The Chairman of the Rajya Sabha

solution:
(B) The President
Article 80 which deals with the Composition of the Council of States mentions that the President nominates members to the Rajya Sabha based on their excellence in literature, science, art and social service.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following is NOT appointed by the President of India? [SSC CGL 05/08/2022]

options.b: Comptroller and Auditor General of India

solution:
(D) Solicitor General of India
The Solicitor General is appointed by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet (ACC), headed by the Prime Minister. He is the 2nd highest law officer, subordinate to attorney general.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following never became the Vice President of India? [SSC CGL 16/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Gulzarilal Nanda
Gulzarilal Nanda served twice as Acting Prime Minister, but was never Vice President. Zakir Hussain - 1962 - 1967, VV Giri - 1967-1969, BD Jatti - 1974 - 1979.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who unfurls the flag at Rajpath on the Republic Day? [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(A) The President of India
On Republic Day (26 January), the President unfurls the national flag at Kartavya Path (formerly Rajpath). While, on Independence Day (15 August), the Prime Minister hoists the flag at the Red Fort.

subject: Polity
question_text: How much security deposit has to be tendered by a candidate for the post of President of India? [CISF HCM 30/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Rs. 15,000
Every candidate who contests an election for the posts of President and Vice President of India has to tender a security deposit amount of Rs. 15,000 in the Reserve Bank of India. The deposit is liable to be forfeited if the candidate fails to secure one-sixth of the votes polled.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who administers the oath of office to the Vice President of India? [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(C) The President of India
The oath of the Vice President is administered by the President as mentioned in Article 69. Also, Article 63 states that there shall be a Vice President of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the tenure of the Vice-President of India? [SSC MTS 13/09/2023]

options.b: Until he wants to hold the office

solution:
(D) 5 years
Article 67 mentions the Term of office of the Vice President which is 5 years. 1) The Vice President shall address his resignation to the President. 2) The resolution for his removal can be initiated only in Rajya Sabha and passed by the majority of all the then members and agreed to by the Lok Sabha, but a 14 days prior notice has to be given before moving the resolution. 3) A Vice President shall continue to hold office until his successor enters upon his office.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the minimum age prescribed for a person to be eligible to become Vice-President of India? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 35 years
The minimum age to be eligible to become Vice-President is 35 years as mentioned in Article 66 which deals with the election of the Vice President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Article 77 of the Indian Constitution deals with the _______. [SSC CGL 09/09/2024]

options.a: term of office of the Vice-President
options.b: conduct of business of the Government of India
options.d: oath of office by the Vice-President

solution:
(B) Conduct of business of the Government of India
It states that all executive action of the Government of India shall be expressed in the name of the President. Article 74- Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: The provision for the post of Vice-President in the Indian Constitution was taken from _______. [SSC CHSL 03/08/2023]
solution:
(B) United States of America
The Offices of both the President and Vice President are taken from the USA. Article 75 - Deals with the appointment, tenure, and responsibilities of the Prime Minister and Council of Ministers. Article 148 - CAG.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article deals with the election of the Vice President? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(A) Article 66
Article 66 - Election of Vice President. (1) The Vice President shall be elected by the members of an electoral college consisting of all MPs in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote with voting by the secret ballot. (2) He shall not be a member of either House of the Parliament and State Legislature and if a member be elected Vice President, he shall be deemed to have vacated his seat on the date on which he enters upon his office as Vice President. (3) Eligibility : citizen of India, Age : minimum 35 years, he should be qualified to be elected as a member of Rajya Sabha. (4) A Vice President shall not hold any office of profit.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following was the third Vice - President of India? [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(D) VV Giri
BD Jatti - Fifth Vice-President ; Gopal Swarup Pathak - Fourth Vice-President.

subject: Polity
question_text: The election of the next Vice-President is to be held within _______ of the expiry of the term of office of the outgoing Vice-President. [SSC CHSL 02/06/2022]
solution:
(B) 60 days
As per Article 68, the time of holding the election to fill vacancy arising due to resignation, death or removal is 60 days from the date of the vacancy. It also states that the election of the next Vice-President must be held before the expiration of the term of the current Vice-President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following statements about the Vice President of India is INCORRECT? [SSC CGL 02/12/2022]

options.a: Members of State legislatures do not take part in his elections.
options.b: He acts as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
options.c: The Vice President is elected for five years.
options.d: He can be removed from his office by the President approved through a resolution passed by the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

solution:
(D) He can be removed from his office by the President approved through a resolution passed by the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
The Vice-President is not removed by the President. Instead, he can only be removed by a resolution passed by the Rajya Sabha with an effective majority (majority of all the then members) and agreed to by the Lok Sabha.

subject: Polity
