question_text: In which year did Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan assumed office as the first Vice President of India? [SSC PHASE XII 21/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 1952
He was the first and the longest serving Vice-President of India. He served in this position twice until May 1962, before becoming the second President of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Vice President of India serves as the ex-officio Chairman of which legislative body? [SSC GD 21/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Rajya Sabha
Article 64 states that the Vice-President acts as the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.

subject: Polity
question_text: A candidate seeking election as Vice-President is required to make a security deposit of _______. [SSC CHSL 31/05/2022]
solution:
(D) 15,000
For explanation, refer to question 31.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who was the second Vice-President of India? [SSC CGL TIER-II 26/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Dr. Zakir Hussain
First - Dr. S Radhakrishnan; Second - Dr. Zakir Hussain; Third - V.V. Giri.

subject: Polity
question_text: _______ is NOT part of the Union Executive. [SSC PHASE XII 24/06/2024]
solution:
(D) The Supreme Court of India
It includes the President, Vice President, Prime Minister, Council of Ministers, and Attorney General of India.

subject: Polity
question_text:
The Electoral College for electing the President of India does not include which of the following?
1. All elected members of the Rajya Sabha.
2. All elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of States.
3. All members of the Legislative Councils of States.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2021]
solution:
(C) 3 only
The electoral college for electing the President is defined in Article 54 which includes all Elected (MPs + MLAs). It does not include the nominated members of Parliament, the nominated members of State Legislative Assemblies and the members of State Legislative Councils (both elected and nominated).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following powers has not been conferred on the President of India? [CAPF 2021]

options.b: To decide at his/her discretion any question regarding disqualification of a Member of the Parliament.
options.c: To grant pardon to a person who has been punished by a court-martial
options.d: To remove a Judge of the High Court

solution:
(B) To decide at his/her discretion any question regarding disqualification of a Member of the Parliament.
As per Article 103, the President while taking decisions on questions as to disqualification of members, the President should know the opinion given by the ECI which is binding. Also Article 72 empowers the President to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions of punishment in cases involving a Court Martial.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who, among the following Presidents of India, sent back the Post Office (Amendment) Bill, 1986 for the reconsideration of the Parliament? [CAPF 2024]
solution:
(C) Giani Zail Singh
In 1986, President Zail Singh used the pocket veto on the Indian Post Office (Amendment) Bill. R Venkataraman was the 2nd President to use absolute veto in 1991 on the Salary, Allowances and Pension of Members of Parliament (Amendment) Bill.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Vice-President of India is not correct? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: He is not eligible for re-election.
options.b: He must not be less than 35 years of age.
options.c: He is elected by members of an electoral college consisting of members of both Houses of Parliament.
options.d: His term of office is five years.

solution:
(A) He is not eligible for re-election
The Vice-President is eligible for re-election and can serve multiple terms.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements is not correct? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: The executive powers of the Union shall be vested in the President of India.
options.b: Parliament, by law, can confer functions on authorities other than the President of India.
options.c: The President of India is elected by an electoral college consisting of all Members of Parliament and all Members of all State Legislatures.
options.d: A person, having held office as the President, shall be eligible for election to that office for the second term.

solution:
(C) The President of India is elected by an electoral college consisting of all Members of Parliament and all Members of all State Legislatures.
The electoral college consists not all of the members but only the Elected (MPs + MLAs). The second statement is correct as the Parliament can delegate functions to other authorities under Article 77 and 258.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following is given discretionary powers under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India to set up a Tribes Advisory Council in a State which has Scheduled Tribes but not Scheduled Areas? [CDS I 2023]

options.a: The Governor of the concerned State

solution:
(B) The President of India
In a state having scheduled tribes but no scheduled areas, the President of India has the discretion to direct the Governor to establish a Tribes Advisory Council.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the election of the President of India is not correct? [CDS I 2023]

options.a: A Union Minister shall not be eligible for election as the President.
options.b: The President is elected by the elected members of the Parliament and the Legislative Assemblies of States and Union Territories.
options.c: The Electoral College does not include elected members of the Legislative Councils.
options.d: The system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote is followed.

solution:
(A) A Union Minister shall not be eligible for election as the President.
A Union Minister is eligible to contest the Presidential election. However, if elected as President, they must resign from their ministerial or legislative post before taking office. While the Article 54 deals with electoral college, the Article 55 deals with the manner of election of the President in which the President is elected using proportional representation via the single transferable vote with voting by secret ballot. Also, there is no provision for defection in the case of election of President.

subject: Polity
question_text: The President of India may resign from his/her office by writing under his/her hand. To whom of the following is his/her resignation addressed to? [CBI 2023]

options.b: The senior most available Judge of the Supreme Court, in the absence of the Chief Justice of India
options.d: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

solution:
(C) The Vice-President of India
As per Article 56(1)(a) of the constitution, the President can resign by writing under his/her hand addressed to the Vice President of India. While the Vice President resigns to the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following served as the President of India for the shortest period of time? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) Zakir Husain
He served for less than 2 years as the President because he passed away while in office. Pratibha Patil (2007- 2012) is the first woman President and Dr. S Radhakrishnan is the longest serving Vice-President.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Vice President of India shall be elected by the electoral college consisting of the members of [CISF AC 2021]

options.d: Both Houses with the support of State Legislatures.

solution:
(C) Both Houses of Parliament
Article 66 defines the electoral college for the election of Vice President which consists of all the Members of both Houses of Parliament.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who amongst the following has not occupied the office of both President and Vice President of India? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(D) Neelam Sanjiva Reddy
He served only as the President of India but never held the office of Vice President.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the President of India is/are correct?
1. The President of India is elected by an electoral college in accordance with the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote.
2. The elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of Union Territories of Delhi and Puducherry take part in the election of the President of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the elected members of all the Legislative Assemblies of States and UTs take part in election of the President. While all MPs (elected + nominated) take part in the impeachment of the President.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. If the election of the President of India is declared void by the Supreme Court of India, all acts done by him/her in the performance of duties of his/her office of President before the date of decision become invalid.
2. Election for the post of the President of India can be postponed on the ground that some Legislative Assemblies have been dissolved and elections are yet to take place.
3. When a Bill is presented to the President of India, the Constitution prescribes time limits within which he/she has to declare his/her assent.
How many of the above statements are correct? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(D) None
(1) Article 71(2) states that if the election of a person as President is declared void by the Supreme Court, it does not affect the validity of acts done by him/her before the decision. (2) Even if some State Assemblies are dissolved, the MLAs of dissolved Assemblies do not participate in voting, but the election proceeds. (3) The Constitution does not prescribe any time limit for the President to take a decision on a Bill. Under Article 111, the President can : (a) give assent to the Bill, (b) withhold assent (veto), (c) Return it for reconsideration (except Money Bills), (d) Use Pocket Veto.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The President of India may specify a 'race' to be treated under the Scheduled Caste category for all purposes under the Constitution of India.
2. The President of India may specify a 'tribe' to be treated under the Scheduled Caste category for all purposes under the Constitution of India.
3. Parliament by law can include a caste or tribe in the list of Scheduled Castes.
How many of the above statements is/are correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]
solution:
(C) 3
All the statements are correct as under Article 341(1), the President after consultation with the Governor, may specify the castes, races or tribes to be treated under the Scheduled Caste category for all purposes under the Constitution of India and as per Article 341(2) the Parliament by law can include a caste, race or tribe in the list of Scheduled Castes.

subject: Polity
question_text: As per the Constitution of India, which one among the following statements is correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2024]

options.a: The Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) is appointed by the President of India and remains in office during the pleasure of the President of India.
options.b: Administrative expenses of the office of the CAG are charged upon the Contingency Fund of India.
options.c: The reports of the CAG relating to the accounts of the Union and States are submitted to the President of India.
options.d: The President of India shall cause the reports of the CAG relating to the accounts of the Union to be laid before each House of Parliament.

solution:
(D) The President of India shall cause the reports of the CAG relating to the accounts of the Union to be laid before each House of Parliament.
Under Article 148(1), the CAG has a fixed tenure of either 6 years or until the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier. Also, the administrative expenses of the CAG's office are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India [Article 266(1)], not the contingency fund (Article 267). The CAG submits audit reports to : The President (For Union government accounts) and The Governor (for State government accounts).

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following statements in respect of election to the President of India:
1. The members nominated to either House of the Parliament or the Legislative Assemblies of States are also eligible to be included in the Electoral College.
2. Higher the number of elective Assembly seats, higher is the value of vote of each MLA of that State.
3. The value of vote of each MLA of Madhya Pradesh is greater than that of Kerala.
4. The value of vote of each MLA of Puducherry is higher than that of Arunachal Pradesh because the ratio of total population to total number of elective seats in Puducherry is greater as compared to Arunachal Pradesh.
How many of the above statements are correct? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(A) Only one
Only statement 4 is correct. (1) Nominated members do not have voting rights in the Presidential election. (2) The value of an MLA's vote is - (total population of state) / (Total number of Elected MLAs) X 1/1000. Thus, a higher number of elective Assembly seats does not necessarily mean a higher vote value per MLA. (3) The vote value of an MLA depends on the state's population-to-seat ratio. Kerala's MLA vote value is higher. (4) Puducherry has a higher population-to-MLA ratio than Arunachal Pradesh, so its MLA vote value is higher.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: There shall be a _______ with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President of India. [SSC CHSL 30/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Council of Ministers
Article 74(a) - Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President who shall act in accordance with the advice. Article 74(b) - The question of whether any advice was tendered by the Council of Ministers to the President shall not be inquired into in any court. The President may require the Council of Ministers to reconsider the advice once but has to act in accordance with the advice tendered after such reconsideration.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: As per Article _______ of the Indian Constitution, the Council of Ministers are collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha. [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(A) 75(3)
Article 75 - Other provisions as to Ministers. (3) COMs are collectively responsible to Lok Sabha. (4) The ministers are administered an oath by the President before entering the office. (5) A Minister who for any period of six consecutive months is not a member of either House of Parliament shall cease to be a Minister after the period ends. (6) - Salary and allowances : decided by the Parliament.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution deals with the relationship between the Prime Minister and the President? [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 75
Article 75(1) - The Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and the other Ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister. Article 75(2) - Ministers are individually responsible to the President and hold office at the pleasure of the President. Also, the President shall act on the advice of the CoMs, led by the Prime Ministers.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Which of the following are the features of the Parliamentary form of government? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
1. The President is the nominal or de jure executive.
2. The Prime Minister is the titular executive.
3. The President is the head of state.
4. The Prime Minister is the head of government.
solution:
(A) Only 1, 3 and 4
In a Parliamentary form of government, in India, the President serves as the titular or de jure head of state while the Prime Minister is the de facto or real executive authority, leading the government and exercising actual power.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who among the following was the shortest-serving Prime Minister of India? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Atal Bihari Vajpayee
Atal Bihari Vajpayee (1996) - 16 days (16 May - 1 June, 1996)
Charan Singh (1979-80) - 170 days (28 July 1979 - 14 Jan, 1980)
Chandra Shekhar (1990-91) - 223 days (10 Nov 1990 - 21 June, 1991)
Gulzarilal Nanda (1966 & 1964) - 13 days (interim PM twice)

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: The minimum strength of the Council of Ministers in a state as per the Indian Constitution is _______. [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 12 persons
As per Article 164(1A) introduced by the 91st CA, the total strength of the Council of Ministers in a state : 1) shall not be less than 12 members, including the Chief Minister. 2) shall not exceed 15% of the total strength of the Legislative Assembly of that state. However, in the case of Delhi Legislative Assembly, the total strength of the CoMs shall not exceed 10% of the total strength of the Assembly.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which of the following Articles states that the Prime Minister should be appointed by the President? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 75
Article 62 - Deals with the time of holding elections to fill a vacancy in the office of President. Article 71 - Related to disputes regarding the election of the President/Vice-President. Article 66 - Pertains to the election of the Vice-President.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which of the given Articles of the Constitution of India mentions the following? "There shall be a council of Ministers with the Prime Minister at the head to aid and advise the President who shall in the exercise of his functions, act in accordance with such advice." [SSC CHSL 04/08/2021]
solution:
(C) Article 74(1)
42nd CA, 1976 - Made the President bound by the advice of the Council of Ministers. Later, the 44th CA, 1978 gave the President one opportunity to ask for reconsideration before being bound by the advice. Article 74(2) - The advice given by the Council of Ministers to the President cannot be challenged, questioned or inquired in any court.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which of the following is NOT correct about the Prime Minister of India? [SSC PHASE XII 25/06/2024]

options.a: He is the Head of the Government.
options.b: He is the principal communicator between the President and the Council of Ministers.
options.c: He recommends the name of persons who can be appointed as ministers by the President.
options.d: He and his ministers are collectively responsible to the Upper House of the Parliament.

solution:
(D) He and his ministers are collectively responsible to the Upper House of the Parliament.
The Prime Minister and his ministers are collectively responsible to the Lower House of the Parliament. (Lok Sabha). Article 75(3) - Council of Ministers are responsible to the Lok Sabha.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Lal Bahadur Shastri served as the Prime Minister of India from _______ to _______. [SSC CPO 25/11/2020]
solution:
(D) 1964; 1966
Lal Bahadur Shastri was the second Prime Minister of India and served until his untimely death in 1966 in Tashkent, Uzbekistan.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who was our first Prime Minister? [SSC MTS 12/09/2023]
solution:
(C) Jawaharlal Nehru
He was the longest serving PM (16 years 286 days).

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who was the Prime Minister of India when the Fundamental Duties were first incorporated in the Constitution of India? [SSC MTS 13/09/2023]
solution:
(B) Indira Gandhi
The Fundamental Duties were incorporated by the 42nd CA, 1976. The Swaran Singh Committee recommended the inclusion of Fundamental Duties. This amendment added Article 51A to the constitution, with 10 Fundamental Duties (later added one more duty through 86th CA in 2002).

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who among the following served the shortest tenure as the Prime Minister of India? [SSC CGL 27/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Chandra Shekhar
Chandra Shekhar (1990 - 1991) - 223 days; H.D. Deve Gowda (1996-1997) - 324 days; V.P. Singh (1989-1990) - 343 days; I.K. Gujral (1997-1998) - 333 days.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who among the following replaced Morarji Desai as the Prime Minister of India in 1979? [SSC CGL 13/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Charan Singh
Charan Singh was the fifth Prime Minister.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who among the following was the prime minister of India in 1978? [SSC MTS 18/10/2021]
solution:
(D) Morarji Desai
He served as Prime Minister from March 1977 to July 1979. He was the first non-congress PM of India, leading the Janata Party. He was serving as the PM when both 44th CA 1978 and The Rolling plan was introduced in place of five year plans.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: As per the Indian Constitution, the size of the Council of Ministers shall NOT exceed: [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]

options.a: 20 percent of total number of members of the House of the People B
options.b: 10 percent of total number of members of the House of the People
options.c: 15 percent of total number of members of the House of the People
options.d: 25 percent of total number of members of the House of the People

solution:
(C) 15 percent of total number of members of the House of the People.
It is mentioned in Article 75 (1A) of the constitution added by the 91st CA. Article 75 (1) - Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and Other ministers shall be appointed by the President on the advice of the PM.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which is NOT correct about India's Prime Minister? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]

options.a: He is the head of the government.
options.b: He is the leader of the majority party in Lok Sabha.
options.c: He can be removed if a no confidence motion is passed by both the Houses of Parliament with 2/3rd majority.
options.d: He is appointed by the President of India.

solution:
(C) He can be removed if a no confidence motion is passed by both the Houses of Parliament with 2/3rd majority.
A Prime Minister can be removed from office if a no-confidence motion is passed in the Lok Sabha (not both Houses) by a simple majority (more than 50% of the members present and voting). The Rajya Sabha does not vote on a no-confidence motion. If a censure motion is passed by a majority, it does not necessitate the dissolution of the government; however, if a no-confidence motion is passed, the government must resign.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who runs the government at the national level under the parliamentary system? [SSC CGL 12/12/2022]

options.a: The President and Council of Ministers
options.b: The Prime Minister and Council of Ministers

solution:
(B) The Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
Article 77 - All executive actions shall be expressed to be taken in the name of President. Article 78 - Deals with the Duties of the Prime Minister with respect to furnishing of information to the President.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: There is a Council of Ministers with the Prime Ministers as its head to advice the _______ who is the constitutional head of India. [SSC GD 01/03/2024]
solution:
(D) President
The President of India is the nominal head / de jure head / titular head while the Prime Minister is the real head / de facto head.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: National Disaster Management Authority is headed by: [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(A) The Prime Minister
Other institutions headed by the PM are : NITI Aayog, National Security Council, Inter State Council, National Development Council.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India requires the Prime Minister of India to furnish information related to decisions of the Council of Ministers to the President of India? [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(A) Article 78
Article 78 - Deals with the Duties of the Prime Minister with respect to furnishing of information to the President. (The Prime Minister (PM) is often called the "linchpin" of the Indian government said by Pt. Nehru).

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Attorney General of India is not correct? [NDA II 2021]

options.a: He has the right of audience only in the Supreme Court of India.
options.b: He shall receive such remuneration as the President may determine.
options.c: He shall be qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court.
options.d: He shall give advice to the Government of India on all legal matters.

solution:
(A) He has the right of audience only in the Supreme Court of India.
Article 76 - Attorney-General for India. (1) The President appoints a person as Attorney General who shall be qualified to be appointed as a Judge of the Supreme Court. (2) He shall advise the Government on legal matters. (3) He has the right of audience in all courts in the territory of India. (4) He shall hold office during the pleasure of the President and shall receive such remuneration as the President may determine. Article 165 - Advocate General.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Who among the following had the shortest span in office as the Prime Minister of India? [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) Chaudhary Charan Singh
He served for approximately 170 days from July 1979 to January 1980.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Consider the following statements about Attorney-General in India:
1. She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India.
2. She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote.
3. She/he is appointed by the President of India.
4. She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law.
How many of the statements given above is/are correct? [NDA II 2023]
solution:
(C) 3
The fourth statement is incorrect as the AG is not a full-time counsel for the government. They can engage in private legal practice as well. Also, as per Article 88 - The AG has the right to speak or take part in proceedings of either House, any joint sitting of the Houses and any committee of Parliament but no right to vote.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The duties of the Attorney General of India are to give advice to the Government of India upon such legal matters, and to perform such other duties of a legal character, as may from time to time be referred to or assigned to him by the President of India.
2. The Solicitor General of India is the secondary Law Officer of the country, who assists the Attorney General, and is himself assisted by several Additional Solicitor Generals of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [NDA II 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Statement 1 is mentioned in Article 76(2).

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The President of India appoints the Attorney General of India and the Solicitor General of India.
2. Both the Attorney General of India and the Solicitor General of India are constitutional posts.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
The Solicitor General is appointed by the Central Government and not the President. Also, the Attorney General of India is a constitutional post under Article 76 while the Solicitor General is not a constitutional post.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not correct? [CBI 2023]

options.a: As per the Constitution of India, the Union Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Parliament.
options.b: Residual Powers are vested in the Parliament.
options.c: The President of India is the Constitutional Head of the Executive of the Union.
options.d: As per the Constitution of India, there is provision for independence of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India.

solution:
(A) As per the Constitution of India, the Union Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Parliament.
The CoM is collectively responsible to Lok Sabha [Article 75(3)], not the Parliament which also includes Rajya Sabha. Under Article 248, Parliament has the power to make laws on subjects not mentioned in the State List or Concurrent List, known as residual subjects. Also, Article 148 ensures the independence of the CAG including security of tenure, removal procedures similar to a Supreme Court judge and provisions that prevent arbitrary salary reductions.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text: Which one of the following statements with regard to the Attorney-General of India as per Article 76 of the Constitution of India is not correct? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]

options.a: The Attorney-General of India is appointed by the Chief Justice of India.
options.b: A person who is qualified to be appointed a Judge of the Supreme Court of India is appointed as the Attorney-General of India.
options.c: In the performance of his/her duties, the Attorney-General shall have the right of audience in all courts in the territory of India.
options.d: The Attorney-General shall hold office during the pleasure of the President of India.

solution:
(A) The Attorney-General of India is appointed by the Chief Justice of India.
The Attorney General of India (AGI) is appointed by the President of India under Article 76(1) of the Indian Constitution. For other options, refer to question 22 and 24 of Level II.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
With reference to anti-defection law in India, consider the following statements:
1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House.
2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
As per the 10th schedule, a nominated legislator can join a political party within six months of being appointed. If they join a party after six months, they will be disqualified under the anti-defection law.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Attorney General of India and Solicitor General of India are the only officers of the Government who are allowed to participate in the meetings of the Parliament of India.
2. According to the Constitution of India, the Attorney General of India submits his resignation when the Government which appointed him resigns.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
Statement 1 is incorrect as the Solicitor general does not have the right to participate in parliamentary proceedings. Only the Attorney General has the right to speak in Parliament but not to vote (Article 88). Since the Attorney General is appointed by the President on the advice of the council of ministers.

subject: Polity
chapter_name: Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
question_text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister.
2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
The classifications in statement 1 are based on executive practice, not constitutional provisions. Hence, it is incorrect.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Indian Parliament comprises the _______. [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(B) President and the two Houses
As per Article 79, the Parliament consists of: The President, Lok Sabha and The Rajya Sabha. Article 52 - There shall be a President of India. Article 80 - Composition of Council of States. Article 81 - Composition of the House of the People.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution provides for a bicameral Parliament of India? [SSC CGL 17/07/2023]
solution:
(C) Article 79
Article 79 states that the Parliament consists of the President and two Houses: 1) Lok Sabha - Lower House / House of people / first chamber; 2) Rajya Sabha - Upper House / House of elders / second chamber / Council of States. Bicameralism is adopted from the British Parliament.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian constitution specifies the composition of the Council of States? [SSC CHSL 14/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Article 80
Article 80 deals with the composition of the Rajya Sabha. Key provisions: 1) Maximum strength - 250 members out of which 12 members are nominated by the President having expertise in the fields of Literature, science, art and social science (Trick- Lisas) and the 238 members represent the States and Union Territories. 2) The allocation of seats in Rajya Sabha shall be in accordance with the 4th schedule. 3) The representatives of States are elected by the Legislative Assemblies of the respective States through proportional representation by means of a single transferable vote.

subject: Polity
question_text: The term 'Parliament' refers to the _______. [SSC CGL 01/12/2022]
solution:
(D) National legislature
The Parliament is the legislative body at the Union Level. It consists of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. The Legislative Assembly operates at the State level.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which chapter of the Constitution of India, discusses the Articles of Parliament? [SSC MTS 25/07/2022]
solution:
(C) Chapter II
Chapter II of Part V (Article 79 - 122) deals with the Parliament. Chapter I - The Executive, Chapter III - Legislative powers of the President, Chapter IV - The Union Judiciary, Chapter V - CAG.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution does India mention that business in Parliament shall be transacted in Hindi or English? [SSC CGL 05/12/2022]
solution:
(A) Article 120
Article 120 - Language to be used in Parliament. It states that business in Parliament shall be transacted in Hindi or English. Article 121 - Restriction on discussion in Parliament. Conduct of any judge of the Supreme Court or of a High Court cannot be discussed in Parliament. Article 122 - Courts not to enquire into proceedings of Parliament. Article 210 deals with the language to be used in the Legislature of a State.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India defines for a separate secretarial staff for each House of the Parliament? [SSC CGL 18/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Article 98
Article 98 deals with the Secretariat of Parliament. Article 123 - Power of President to promulgate ordinances during recess. Article 155 - Appointment of Governor. Article 34 - allows Parliament to restrict fundamental rights and provide legal protection to officials for actions taken during martial law.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India lays down the maximum strength of the Rajya Sabha as 250? [SSC MTS 30/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Article 80
Maximum strength - 250 members out of which 12 members who have special knowledge or practical experience in the fields such as literature, science, art, and social service are nominated by the President and the 238 members represent the States and Union Territories.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the bicameral legislature of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]

options.a: It is a legislative body with four houses.
options.b: It is a legislative body with two houses.
options.c: It is a legislative body with one house.
options.d: It is a legislative body with three houses.

solution:
(B) It is a legislative body with two houses
The bicameral legislature consists of the Rajya Sabha and the Lok Sabha. As of now, six states also have a bicameral legislature, meaning they have both Legislative Assembly and the Legislative Council.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India provides for the power to the President to convene a joint sitting of both the Houses of Parliament? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(C) Article 108
It empowers the President to convene a joint sitting to resolve a deadlock when a bill is passed by one House but rejected or not passed by the other. The President calls a Joint Sitting of both the Houses for the purpose of deliberating and voting on the bill which becomes a law if passed by simple majority. There have been 3 Joint Sittings of Parliament so far - 1) Dowry Prohibition Bill, 1961; 2) Banking Service Commission Bill, 1977; (3) Prevention of Terrorism Bill, 2002.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution of India deals with the Sessions of the Parliament? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(A) Article 85
Article 85 - Sessions of Parliament, prorogation and dissolution. The President summons each House from time to time. The time gap between two sessions of Parliament cannot exceed six months. The President can prorogue (end) a session of one of both Houses or can dissolve the Lok Sabha before its term ends.

subject: Polity
question_text: The First hour of a sitting of the House allotted for asking and answering of questions is called as _______. [SSC STENOGRAPHER 12/10/2023]
solution:
(D) Question Hour
Question Hour (11am - 12pm) : the first hour of sitting. Zero Hour (12pm - 1pm) : Time immediately after the question hour, where MPs raise urgent matters without prior notice. It is an Indian innovation and finds no mention in the constitution. Censure Motion : moved by the opposition to criticize the government's policies. Privilege Motion : raised when a member feels that the privileges of Parliament or its members have been breached.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who was the first speaker of the Lok Sabha? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(B) GV Mavalankar
He is known as the 'father of Lok Sabha'. He headed the Rules committee.

subject: Polity
question_text: When was the first general election for Lok Sabha held in India? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(D) 1951-52
The Indian National Congress (INC) secured a massive majority and won 364 out of 489 seats. Second Place : Communist Party of India - Won 16 seats.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following pairs is/are correctly matched regarding the terminology used in the Parliament? [SSC CGL 18/07/2023]
A. Appropriation Bill - A Money Bill passed for providing for the withdrawal or appropriation from and out of the Consolidated Fund of India
B. Demand for Grants - Earmarking of budgetary allocation for meeting only planned expenditure of a Ministry/Department.
C. Short Duration Discussion - For raising a discussion on a matter of urgent public importance for which a notice has to be given by a Member and supported by 50 Members.
solution:
(D) A and C only
Article 114 deals with the Appropriation Bill. Demand for Grants refers to the budgetary allocation for both planned and non-planned expenditure.

subject: Polity
question_text: After how many days of absence from Parliament without permission will a Member of Parliament be disqualified? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(A) 60 Days
Article 101 - Vacation of seats. (1) A person cannot be a member of both Houses of Parliament at the same time. (2) Disqualification on Holding Offices of Profit. (3) Resignation by writing to the presiding officer. (4) If an MP is absent for 60 consecutive days without permission, their seat may be declared vacant.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the most powerful weapon that enables the Parliament to ensure executive accountability? [SSC MTS 04/09/2023]
solution:
(B) The no-confidence motion
Under Article 75(3), the CoMs is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha. Any Lok Sabha MP having the support of at least 50 MPs can move the no-confidence motion. Voting is done only in Lok Sabha and if the government fails to secure a majority, it resigns.

subject: Polity
question_text: A maximum of _______ members can be nominated by the President of India to become a member of the Rajya Sabha. [SSC MTS 16/05/2023]
solution:
(A) 12
Under Article 80, the President nominates 12 members of Rajya Sabha having special knowledge or practical experience in fields like literature, science, art and social service. Earlier, under Article 331, the President could nominate 2 members from the Anglo-Indian community to the Lok Sabha. However, this provision was abolished by the 104th Amendment Act, 2019.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the tenure of a Rajya Sabha member? [SSC MTS 15/06/2023]
solution:
(C) 6 years
As per Article 83(1) which deals with the duration of Houses of Parliament, the Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution. Instead, one-third of its members retire every two years. This makes the tenure of a Rajya Sabha member to be 6 years.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following can alter the boundary of a state or change its name? [SSC CGL 08/12/2022]
solution:
(B) Parliament
Article 3 provides for the formation of new states and alteration of areas, boundaries or names of existing states by the Parliament provided that the bill is introduced in either House on the recommendation of the President. Then the bill is referred by the President to the concerned state's legislature, but its recommendation is not binding. The final authority to approve, modify or reject such a bill rests with Parliament.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a kind of question related to Indian Parliamentary Proceedings? [SSC CPO 09/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Long Notice Question
There are three categories of questions : 1) Starred Question - Requires an oral answer from the minister in the House, followed by supplementary questions. 2) Unstarred Question - Requires a written answer, and no supplementary questions are allowed. 3) Short Notice Question - Asked with 10 days prior notice on urgent matters, and it is answered orally. They are part of the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business of Parliament, not the Constitution.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is called 'the temple of the country's democracy'? [SSC CPO 10/11/2022]
solution:
(D) Parliament
The Parliament of India is referred to as "the temple of country's democracy" because it is the supreme legislative body where representatives of the people discuss, debate and make laws. It symbolizes the voice of the people.

subject: Polity
question_text: Archana wishes to contest for Lok Sabha elections in India. What is the maximum limit of constituencies that can she contest? [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(D) Two
The Representation of the People Act, 1951 provides for a candidate to contest from a maximum of two constituencies in a Lok Sabha or a State Assembly election. However, if a candidate wins from both the constituencies, they must vacate one seat leading to a by-election in that constituency.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Lok Sabha (House of the People) was duly constituted for the first time on _______. [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(B) 17 April, 1952
Constitution of : (i) Lok Sabha - 17 April ,1952 ; (ii) Rajya Sabha - 3 April,1952. Both the Houses had their first sitting on 13 May,1952.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following states has two houses in its state legislature? [SSC MTS 05/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Maharashtra
Article 168 deals with the constitution of legislatures in States and specifies that some states have bicameral legislatures and some have just the Legislative Assembly. While Article 169 empowers Parliament to create or abolish a Legislative Council in a state if the state's Legislative Assembly passes a resolution with a special majority. As of now, only 6 states have bicameral legislatures. (Trick : KABUTM) K - Karnataka, A - Andhra Pradesh, B - Bihar, U - Uttar Pradesh, T - Telangana, M - Maharashtra.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of parliamentary government in India? [SSC MTS 19/07/2022]

options.a: Membership of the ministers in the legislature
options.c: Presence of nominal and real executives
options.d: Collective responsibility of the executive to the legislature

solution:
(B) Undissolvable lower house
As per Article 85, the President can dissolve the Lok Sabha in cases when the government fails to prove its majority in the House. Other options are correct as the ministers must be the members of the Parliament and the President is the nominal executive while the Prime Minister and the CoMs are the real executives. Also, if the Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion, the entire government must resign.

subject: Polity
question_text: An alliance formed by political parties after elections when no party has been able to get adequate seats to form a clear majority is called _______. [SSC MTS 20/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Coalition
A coalition government is a power sharing arrangement. Coalition governments can be pre-poll or post-poll.

subject: Polity
question_text: The period between prorogation of Parliament and reassembly in a new session is called _______. [SSC MTS 21/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Recess
The period between the prorogation (end of a parliamentary session) and the start of the next session is called a recess. During this time, no legislative business takes place. However, the President can promulgate an ordinance under Article 123 if he/she is satisfied that there is an urgent need for a law. The ordinance remains valid for a maximum of six months and six weeks that is until Parliament reconvenes and decides on it. Article 213 - Power of Governor to promulgate ordinances.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Indian Constitution, the House of People shall consist of not more than _______ members to represent the Union territories. [SSC MTS 12/07/2022]
solution:
(B) 20
Article 81 deals with the composition of the Lok Sabha which states that not more than 530 members shall be chosen by direct election and not more than 20 members shall represent the Union Territories. Also, the CoMs cannot exceed 15% of the total strength of the House.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many organs of government are there according to the Indian Constitution? [SSC MTS 12/07/2022]
solution:
(D) 3
The government is divided into three organs: 1) Legislative - makes the laws (Parliament and State Legislature); 2) Executive - Implements and enforces the laws (President, Prime Minister, Council of Ministers, Bureaucracy); 3) Judiciary - Interprets the laws and ensures justice (Supreme Court, High Court, District Courts).

subject: Polity
question_text: _______ is a motion moved by a member to cut short the debate on a matter before the House. [SSC MTS 13/07/2022]
solution:
(A) Closure
Closure Motion is used to cut short the debate on a matter before the House and proceed to voting. It requires the approval of the Speaker and is used to prevent unnecessary delays. Censure Motion is used to criticize the government for its specific policies, but it does not lead to its removal.

subject: Polity
question_text: In the absence of the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, who presides over the joint sitting of the two houses of Parliament? [SSC CHSL 24/05/2022]

options.b: Any senior member of Rajya Sabha
options.d: Any senior member of Lok Sabha

solution:
(C) Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha
The joint sitting is presided by the Speaker of Lok Sabha or in his absence the Deputy speaker of Lok Sabha. If both the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker are absent, the Deputy chairman of Rajya Sabha presides over it.

subject: Polity
question_text: In the Lok Sabha, discussion under which rule of the Constitution of India does not involve a formal motion before the House? [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Rule 193
It allows for a short-duration discussion on an important issue without a formal motion. The purpose is to allow members to express their views on matters of public importance without the need for a decision. Thus, no voting takes place after the discussion.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following appoints the Speaker 'Pro tem' of the Lok Sabha? [SSC CGL 23/08/2021]
solution:
(C) President
Article 99 which deals with the oath and affirmation by members states that every MP shall make and subscribe an oath before the President or some person appointed on that behalf by him. The President appoints the Speaker Pro Tem mainly to conduct the swearing-in of members. The most senior member of the Lok Sabha is usually chosen for this role. Article 85 of the Indian Constitution deals with the sessions of Parliament, prorogation, and dissolution of the Lok Sabha.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who has the power to abolish or create a legislative council of the state? [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]

options.b: High court of the concerned State
options.d: Chief minister of the concerned state

solution:
(A) Parliament
As per Article 169, the Parliament may by law provide for the abolition or creation of Legislative Council of a state, if the Legislative Assembly of a state passes a resolution by a majority of total membership of the assembly and by a majority of not less than two-thirds of the members of the Assembly present and voting (special majority) and the Parliament itself passes the resolution by simple majority.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Indian constitution deals with the parliament and its proceedings? [SSC CGL 24/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Article 79 to 122
Article 79-122 comes under the Chapter II of Part V. It deals with the Parliament and its proceedings. Article 36 to 51 - DPSP. Article 51A - Fundamental Duties. Article 12 to 35 - Fundamental Rights.

subject: Polity
question_text: The salary and allowances of leaders of opposition in parliament are governed by the Act passed for the first time by parliament in the year _______. [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(C) 1977
The Salary and Allowances of Leaders of Opposition in Parliament Act, 1977 provides official recognition to the Leader of Opposition in both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha and grants them salary, allowances and facilities equivalent to a Cabinet Minister. Article 106 - Salary and allowances of MPs.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Speaker of the House of the People may submit his resignation to the: [SSC MTS 18/10/2021]

options.a: Deputy Speaker of the House of the People

solution:
(A) Deputy Speaker of the House of the people
Article 94 - Vacation and resignation of, and removal from the offices of Speaker and Deputy Speaker. It states that the Speaker of the Lok Sabha may resign by submitting his resignation to the Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha. While the Deputy Speaker submits his resignation to the Speaker of Lok Sabha. While the Chairman of Rajya Sabha submits his resignation to the President and the Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha submits his resignation to the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, who is the Vice President of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT a device of parliamentary proceeding? [SSC CGL 13/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Full-day discussion
The half-an-hour discussion is held in the last half an hour of a sitting. A member has to give notice in writing at least three days in advance of the day on which the discussion is desired to be raised. The short duration or the two-hour discussion is for raising a discussion on a matter of urgent public importance for which a notice has to be given by a Member supported by two other Members. Zero hour discussion is an informal device where members raise urgent matters during the zero hour (12pm to 1pm) without prior notice.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following options is the legislative organ of the Union government? [SSC CGL 10/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Parliament
The Parliament consisting of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha is the legislative organ of the Union Government of India. It is responsible for making laws and representing the people. While the Legislative Assembly is also a legislative organ, it operates at the State level rather than the Union Level.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who presents the annual budget before the Lok Sabha? [SSC MTS 20/06/2023]

options.c: Comptroller and Auditor General of India

solution:
(A) Union Finance Minister
Although Article 112 which deals with the Annual Financial Statement mentions that the President presents the Budget to Parliament, the President delegates this responsibility to the Finance Minister under Article 77. Article 202 - Annual financial statement in state legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India shall a Money Bill not be introduced in a Legislative Council? [SSC CGL 23/08/2021]
solution:
(B) Article 198
A Money Bill shall not be introduced in a Legislative Council. It can only be introduced in the Legislative Assembly of a State. After passing in the Assembly, the Bill is transmitted to the Legislative Council, which can make recommendations. The Assembly may choose to accept or ignore these recommendations, and if the Council does not return the bill within 14 days, it is deemed passed in its original form.

subject: Polity
question_text: _______ of the Indian Constitution mentions about office of Speaker of the House of the People. [SSC GD 07/03/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 93
Article 93 which deals with the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker of the House of the People states that the House shall choose two members to be respectively the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker within themselves. Conventionally, the speaker is chosen from the ruling party while the Deputy Speaker is chosen from the opposition party.

subject: Polity
question_text:
The Parliamentary government is also known by which other names? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
1. Cabinet government
2. Responsible government
3. Westminster model of government
4. Fixed executive system of government
solution:
(B) Only 1, 2 and 3
The Parliamentary government is known by the names: Cabinet government - since the cabinet is formed from the legislature, Responsible Government - because the executive is accountable to the legislature, Westminster model of government - Based on the system of the UK where the Parliament has supreme authority. The fixed executive system is incorrect as the executive is not fixed and can change with votes of confidence in the legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which system of India is based on the Westminster model? [SSC JE 06/06/2024]
solution:
(A) Indian Parliamentary system
The Westminster model System of UK signifies the supreme authority of the Parliament which is also followed by the Indian parliamentary system.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the maximum strength of the Rajya Sabha, and how many members are nominated by the President? [SSC GD 06/03/2024]

options.a: Maximum strength is 245, and 12 members are nominated by the President
options.b: Maximum strength is 545, and 25 members are nominated by the President
options.c: Maximum strength is 550, and 238 members are nominated by the President
options.d: Maximum strength is 250, and 12 members are nominated by the President

solution:
(D) Maximum strength is 250, and 12 members are nominated by the President.
Article 80 (Composition of Council of states) states that the maximum strength of the Rajya Sabha is 250 and 12 members having special knowledge in matters related to literature, science, art and social service are nominated by the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: The _______ has the power to summon and prorogue either House of the Parliament or to dissolve Lok Sabha? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(B) President
Article 85 - Sessions of Parliament, prorogation and dissolution. The President summons the Houses during the 3 sessions (Budget, Monsoon and Winter). While the President prorogues a session, it is the Presiding officer of a House who adjourns a sitting of that House. Adjournment sine die - Presiding officer ends the meeting with no specified date for resumption. Typically, sessions are adjourned sine die at the end of a session or when there is no further business to address.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Constitution mentions that "the Parliament consists of the President and two houses known as the Council of States (Rajya Sabha) and the House of People (Lok Sabha)"? [SSC CHSL 03/07/2024]
solution:
(A) Article 79
Article 79 mentions that the Parliament comprises the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. Article 70 - Discharge of President's functions in other contingencies. Article 82 - Readjustment after each census. Article 73 - Extent of executive power of the Union.

subject: Polity
question_text: How are the representatives of the UT in Rajya Sabha elected? [SSC GD 01/03/2024]

options.c: Indirectly elected by members of an electoral college
options.d: By members of State legislative Assemblies

solution:
(C) Indirectly elected by members of an electoral college.
In the case of Delhi and Puducherry, members of the Legislative Assembly vote to elect Rajya Sabha representatives. For other UTs, the process may involve elected members of the municipalities or other local bodies.

subject: Polity
question_text: A money bill under Article 111 of the Constitution of India, presented to the President for assent, has to be endorsed by a Certificate of the _______ signed by him/her that it is a money bill. [SSC CPO 03/10/2023]

options.a: Secretary General of the Lok Sabha
options.b: Secretary General of the Rajya Sabha
options.d: Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha

solution:
(C) Speaker of the Lok Sabha
Article 110 defines a Money Bill. It states that every Money Bill when it is transmitted to the Rajya Sabha or when it is presented to the President for assent under article 111, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha must certify that it is a Money Bill.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following is a subject of Union Lists in the Constitution of India? [SSC MTS 04/05/2023]
solution:
(C) Extradition
State List - Liquor, Public Order and Police, Public Health and Sanitation, Agriculture and Irrigation, Betting and Gambling, Prison and Reformatories. Concurrent List - Social planning, Boilers, Education, Forests and Protection of Wildlife, Labour Welfare, Drugs and Poisons.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many members of the Rajya Sabha are elected indirectly and are representatives of states and UT's? [SSC GD 22/02/2024]
solution:
(B) 238
Under article 80 (composition of Rajya Sabha), 238 members are elected indirectly and represent states and UTs while other 12 members are nominated.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who is the presiding officer of the Rajya Sabha (Council of States) in India? [SSC GD 23/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Vice President
Article 64 states that the Vice President is to be the ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States. Also, under Article 65, the Vice President is to act as President or to discharge his functions during casual vacancies or in the absence of the President. Article 63 - There shall be a Vice President of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who was the first speaker of the Lok Sabha? [SSC MTS 11/09/2023]
solution:
(B) GV Mavalankar
Articles related to Speaker - Article 93 - The Speaker and the Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be chosen among the members of the House. Article 94 - deals with their vacation, resignation and removal. The Speaker resigns to the Deputy Speaker, and the Deputy Speaker resigns to the Speaker. They may be removed from their office by a resolution of the Lok Sabha passed by simple majority. Article 95 - Power of Deputy Speaker to perform the duties of the speaker in case of vacancy or absence. Article 96 - The Speaker or the Deputy Speaker not to preside while a resolution for his removal from office is under consideration.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Bill was passed in the Parliament in 2022 to authorize payment of certain sums from and out of the Consolidated Fund of India for the services of the financial year 2022-23? [SSC MTS 15/05/2023]
solution:
(C) The Appropriation Bill
Article 114 deals with the Appropriation Bills. It is a money bill that authorizes the government to withdraw funds from the Consolidated Fund of India to meet its expenditure. Article 117 deals with special provisions as to financial bills.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution deals with the definition of the Money Bill? [SSC CHSL 09/06/2022]
solution:
(D) Article 110
Article 110 defines a Money Bill. It states that it is the Speaker who decides whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not. Article 109 deals with the special procedure in respect of Money Bills as per which the Rajya Sabha can only hold the bill for 14 days and its support is not mandatory. If the recommendations are not returned by the Rajya Sabha, the Bill shall be deemed to have been passed at the expiration of the said term. Article 199 - Definition of Money bill in state legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: 'Police and Public Order' are State subjects under the _______ Schedule of the Constitution of India. [SSC MTS 26/10/2021]
solution:
(C) 7th
Trick to remember schedules - TEARS OF OLD PM. 1st - Territories( Names of states and UTs), 2nd - Emoluments, 3rd - Oaths and Affirmations, 4th - Rajya Sabha seat allocation, 5th - Administration of Scheduled Areas, 6th - Other Scheduled Areas, 7th - Federal Provisions, 8th - Official languages, 9th - Land reform, 10th - Disqualification due to Defection, 11th - Panchayats, 12th - Municipalities.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India shall a Money Bill NOT be introduced in a Legislative Council? [SSC CGL 17/08/2021]
solution:
(A) Article 109
Article 109(1) states that a Money Bill cannot be introduced in the Rajya Sabha. Also, under Article 117 a Money Bill cannot be introduced except on the recommendation of the President. After passing, the President has no right to hold his assent or return the bill.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following subjects is covered in the Eleventh Schedule of the Constitution of India? [SSC CPO 23/11/2020]
solution:
(B) Health and Sanitation
The 11th schedule (added by the 73rd CA,1973) lists 29 subjects under the jurisdiction of Panchayati Raj Institutions, including health and sanitation. Also, the 12th schedule dealing with Municipalities (added by the 74th CA) lists 18 subjects.

subject: Polity
question_text: A Money Bill should be returned to the Lok Sabha within _______ days by Rajya Sabha, either with recommendations or without recommendations. [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(C) 14
For explanation, refer to question 56.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following fixes the date of election of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha? [CAPF 2021]

options.c: The Secretary General of the Lok Sabha.
options.d: The Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha.

solution:
(A) The President of India.
Article 95 - Power of the Deputy Speaker to perform the duties of the Speaker. However, if the offices of both the Speaker and Deputy speaker are vacant, the President appoints a member of Lok Sabha to preside. In case of absence, a member of the panel of 10 Chairpersons (appointed by the Speaker) presides over the proceedings. For further explanation, refer to question 54.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not correct? [CAPF 2022]

options.a: A censure motion can be moved against a single Minister of the Council of Ministers.
options.b: A motion of no-confidence cannot be moved against the Prime Minister alone.
options.c: A censure motion cannot be moved against the entire Council of Ministers.
options.d: A censure motion can be moved against a group of Ministers.

solution:
(C) A censure motion cannot be moved against the entire Council of Ministers
A censure motion can be moved against an individual minister, a group of ministers, or the entire Council of Ministers. The reason for censure must be specified and if passed, does not lead to the resignation of the government. On the other hand, a no-confidence motion is directed at the entire Council of Ministers and if passed, results in the resignation of the government.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which among the following is/are the function/functions of the Cabinet Secretariat?
1. To ensure interministerial coordination.
2. Management of major crisis situations in the country.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
The Cabinet Secretary is appointed by the Prime Minister. Tenure - usually 2 years, but it can be extended at the discretion of the government. Several important functions of the Cabinet Secretary are - coordination between Ministries, crisis management and special parliamentary sessions, administrative oversight of Parliamentary approvals.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who among the following was the first pro-tem Speaker to be unanimously elected as the Speaker of the Lok Sabha? [CAPF 2023]
solution:
(C) Somnath Chatterjee
Somnath Chatterjee was the pro tem Speaker of the 14th Lok Sabha, but was later elected as the Speaker. This made him the second person to achieve this distinction after GV Mavalankar. The President appoints the senior-most member of the Lok Sabha as Pro-tem Speaker under parliamentary conventions.

subject: Polity
question_text: A motion of no-confidence is moved against [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(B) The Council of Ministers
A no-confidence motion in the Lok Sabha can be initiated by any MP who has support of at least 50MPs. The motion is then admitted by the speaker. If passed by a simple majority, the government is required to resign.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the composition of the Parliament is not correct? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: Representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha are elected directly by the people.
options.b: Representatives from Union Territories in Lok Sabha are chosen by direct elections.
options.d: Lok Sabha has seats reserved for SCs and STs.

solution:
(A) Representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha are elected directly by the people.
Article 80 provides for the indirectly elected representatives of the states in Rajya Sabha. Article 81 which deals with the composition of the Lok Sabha states that it can consist not more than 530 members from states and 20 members from UTs. Article 330 - Reservation of seats for SCs and STs in Lok Sabha. Article 332 - Reservation of seats for SCs and STs in Legislative Assemblies.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following motions cannot be made while introducing an ordinary Bill in the Parliament? [CDS I 2021]

options.a: That the Bill be taken into consideration.
options.b: That the Bill be circulated for the purpose of eliciting public opinion.
options.c: That the Bill be referred to a Select Committee.
options.d: That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House.

solution:
(D) That the Bill be referred to a Joint Committee of the House without the concurrence of the other House.
When an ordinary bill is introduced, it can be: 1) Taken into consideration immediately or on a later date, 2) Referred to a Select Committee of the House. 3) Referred to a Joint Committee of both Houses (but only with the concurrence of the other House). 4) Circulated for public opinion.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the 'casting vote' in the Parliament is/are correct?
1. It is cast by the speaker or a person acting as such.
2. It is cast in addition to voting in the first instance.
3. It is cast in the case of equality of votes.
4. It is always cast to maintain the status quo.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2021]
solution:
(B) 1 and 3 only
Under Article 100 which deals with the voting in Houses and the quorum, the Chairman or Speaker, or person acting as such does not vote initially and only votes in case of a tie. It is not necessarily cast to maintain the status quo; the speaker may use discretion while casting the vote. As per Article 100(3), the quorum for a meeting is one-tenth of the total members and if it is not present, the Speaker or Chairman must adjourn the House or suspend the meeting until a quorum is met. Article 189 is the same for the state legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following is the largest State in terms of seats in the Rajya Sabha? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(B) Bihar
In the given options, Bihar has the highest number of seats. Bihar - 16, Andhra Pradesh - 11, Rajasthan - 10, Karnataka - 12. However, Uttar Pradesh is the largest state in terms of seats in the Rajya Sabha with 31 seats.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following is not a condition for the disqualification of a Member of Parliament? [CDS II 2021]

options.a: Voluntary acquisition of citizenship of a foreign country.
options.b: Holding the office of the Chairperson of the National Commission for Women.
options.c: The Member abstains from voting in the House without prior permission.
options.d: The Member holds the office of the Chairman of the Board of Directors of the National Coal Development Corporation Ltd.

solution:
(B) Holding the office of the Chairperson of the National Commission for Women.
Article 102 - Disqualification of membership. (1) holding an office of profit, (2) unsound mind, (3) undischarged solvent, (4) not a citizen of India, (5) disqualification on the grounds of defection. However, under the (Prevention of Disqualification) Act includes the National Commission for Women. Hence, holding the office of the Chairman of the NCW is not a condition for disqualification. Article 103 - Decision on questions as to disqualification of members taken by the President after taking the ECI's opinion which is binding in nature.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Speaker of Lok Sabha is not correct? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: He shall vacate his office if he ceases to be a member of the House of the People.
options.b: He may, at any time, resign by writing his resignation to the President of India.
options.c: He may be removed from his office by a resolution of the House of the People passed by majority of all the then members of the House.
options.d: While the office of the Speaker is vacant, the duties of the office shall be performed by the Deputy Speaker.

solution:
(B) He may, at any time, resign by writing his resignation to the President of India.
As per Article 94 (Vacation, resignation and removal from the offices of Speaker + Deputy Speaker), the speaker resigns to the Deputy Speaker and the Deputy Speaker resigns to the Speaker. The speaker cannot preside over the House when a resolution for his removal is under consideration. Also, Article 101(Vacation of seats) states that an MP may resign by writing to the presiding officer and that if a member is absent for 60 days without permission, his seat may be declared vacant.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a provision related to a Money Bill? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: Imposition, abolition, remission, alteration or regulation of any tax.
options.b: Appropriation of money out of the Consolidated Fund of India.
options.c: Imposition of fines by local authority for local purpose.
options.d: Custody of the Consolidated Fund of India or the Contingency Fund of India.

solution:
(C) Imposition of fines by local authority for local purpose.
Article 110(2) states that a Bill shall not be considered a Money Bill solely because it includes provisions for imposing fines, pecuniary penalties, fees for licenses, or fees for services rendered. The definition of Money Bill is given in Article 110 for the Union level and Article 199 for the State level. All Money Bills are Financial Bills, but not all Financial Bills are Money Bills.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following is not the strength or chief benefit of Bicameralism? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Second chambers check the first chambers and prevent majoritarian rule.
options.b: It checks the powers of the executive.
options.c: The second chambers can act as a constitutional safeguard.
options.d: It often acts as a check on democratic rule, particularly when their members are non-elected or indirectly elected.

solution:
(D) It often acts as a check on democratic rule, particularly when their members are non-elected or indirectly elected.
Bicameralism generally functions to check majoritarian rule, balance executive power, and serve as a constitutional safeguard. Some articles related to it are - Article 79 - Establishes a bicameral Parliament with the President and two Houses, Article 168 - Constitution of Legislatures in States mentioning that some states have bicameral legislatures with both a Legislative Assembly and a Legislative Council, Article 169 - Abolition or creation of legislative councils, Article 80 - Composition of Rajya Sabha.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a category under which the President of India nominates Members of Parliament? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) State Service
Under Article 80, the President nominates members to the Rajya Sabha based on their contributions to art, literature, science and social service. Under Article 171 (Composition of the Legislative Councils) : (i) 1/3rd members are elected by MLAs, (ii) 1/3rd are elected by local bodies, (iii) 1/6th are nominated by Governor based on their contributions to literature, science, art, social service and cooperative society, (iv) 1/12th are elected by teachers, (v) 1/12th are elected by the graduates.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is correct? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: Money Bill cannot be introduced in the Council of States.
options.b: The Council of States has no power to reject or amend the Money Bill.
options.c: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha has the sole and final power in deciding whether a Bill is a Money Bill or otherwise.
options.d: The Council of States has no power to discuss the Money Bill.

solution:
(D) The Council of States has no power to discuss the Money Bill.
For explanation, refer to question 56.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Consider the following:
1. M.A. Ayyangar
2. G.S. Dhillon
3. Balram Jakhar
4. P.A. Sangma
How many of them were the Speaker of the Lok Sabha? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) 4
All four M.A. Ayyangar (1956 - 1962), G.S. Dhillon (1969-1971), Balram Jakhar (1980-1989), P.A. Sangma (1996-1998) have served as Speakers in the Lok Sabha. First Speaker - G.V. Mavalankar (father of Lok Sabha).

subject: Polity
question_text: The executive is given an advance grant to meet temporary running requirements of Government of India in the beginning of the new financial year until the demands are voted by the legislature. This is known as: [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(B) Vote on Account
Article 116 - Votes on account, votes of credit and exceptional grants. Article 113 - Procedure in Parliament with respect to estimates. Article 114 deals with Appropriation Bills. Without passing this Bill, the government cannot take out money from the Consolidated fund. No voting is allowed in this Bill. Article 115 - Supplementary, additional or excess grants. Article 117 - Special provisions as to financial bills.

subject: Polity
question_text: Any question pertaining to the disqualification of a member of the Lok Sabha on the ground of defection is decided by: [CDS II 2022]

options.b: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

solution:
(B) The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
Article 102 provides for the disqualification of membership on grounds of defection. The grounds for defection are - (1) An independent member joins any political party, (2) Nominated member joins the political party after 6 months from date of his nomination, (3) Voting or abstaining from voting in the House against the party's whip, (4) if an MP joins any other party. Exception - If two-thirds of the members merge with another political party. The judicial review is permitted after the decision on disqualification is made as in the Kihooto Hollohan Vs. Zachillhu Case.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:
List-I (Speaker of Lok Sabha):
A. Manohar Gajanan Joshi
B. Somnath Chatterjee
C. Meira Kumar
D. Sumitra Mahajan
List-II (Tenure):
1. 14th Lok Sabha
2. 13th Lok Sabha
3. 16th Lok Sabha
4. 15th Lok Sabha
Code: [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(A) A-2, B-1, C-4, D-3
Manohar Gajanan Joshi (1999-2004) - 13th, Somnath Chatterjee (2004-2009) - 14th, Meira Kumar (2009-2014) - 15th (She is the first woman speaker of the Lok Sabha), Sumitra Mahajan (2014-2019) - 16th. The current Lok Sabha is the 18th one.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a power of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha? [NDA I 2021]

options.a: Speaker shall preside over the House of the People.
options.b: Speaker will cast vote in the first instance in the House.
options.c: Speaker will have power to maintain order within the House of the People.
options.d: Speaker can adjourn the House or suspend the meeting if there is a quorum.

solution:
(B) Speaker will cast vote in the first instance in the House.
As per Article 100 - Voting in Houses, power of Houses to act notwithstanding vacancies and quorum, the Speaker casts his vote only in the case of tie. For explanation, refer to question 8.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following best describes the electoral system of India? [NDA II 2021]
solution:
(A) First-Past-the-Post System
Under this system, the candidate who secures the highest number of votes in a constituency wins, regardless of whether they have an absolute majority. However, the elections of the President, Vice President and Rajya Sabha members follow the Proportional Representation system using the Single Transferable Vote.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Parliament of India is primarily based on the model of [NDA I 2022]
solution:
(B) British Parliament
