The Indian Parliament is primarily based on the British parliamentary system. Also, many adoptions have been made from the GoI Act of 1935. However, it differs in : 1) India is a republic whereas the UK has a hereditary monarchy, 2) Unlike the UK Parliament which is sovereign, the Indian Parliament has limited powers exercised in the scope of the written constitution.

subject: Polity
question_text: In the passing of a Money Bill, the Rajya Sabha has limited powers in that it shall return the bill, with or without any recommendation, within the stipulated time of _______. [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) 14 days
For explanation, refer to question 56 of Level I.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following Lok Sabhas had the shortest span? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) 12th Lok Sabha
The 12th Lok Sabha (1998-99) had the shortest tenure of about 13 months due to the failure of the Atal Bihari Vajpayee led government as it lost a vote of confidence by just one vote.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The Parliament of India consists of the President of India, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha.
2. The Rajya Sabha was constituted for the first time in 1950.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(A) 1 only
As per article 79, the Parliament consists of the President, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha. The Rajya Sabha was first constituted on April 3, 1952, While the Lok Sabha was constituted on April 17, 1952. Both the Houses had their inaugural session on May 13, 1952. The first general elections of the Lok Sabha were conducted in 1951-52.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following is not a function of the Parliament of India? [CBI 2023]

options.c: Appointing Judges to the Supreme Court

solution:
(C) Appointing Judges to the Supreme Court
The judges of the Supreme Court are appointed by the President of India based on the recommendation of a collegium. Article 124 - Establishment and constitution of the Supreme Court. Article 125 - salaries of Judges, Article 126 - Appointment of acting chief justice, Article 127 - Appointment of ad hoc judges. Trick to remember Supreme Court articles - (SC ne SAARC ko SEAT Offer karii).

subject: Polity
question_text: When a Member of the Parliament desires an oral answer from a Ministry, which one of the following types of questions will be suitable? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(B) Starred Question
A starred question is distinguished by an asterisk. Under this, the MP seeks an oral answer from the concerned minister in the House and is allowed to ask supplementary questions while an unstarred question gets a written reply and does not allow follow-up questions. Only 20 such questions are allowed per day during the Question Hour. A Short Notice Question is asked on urgent matters with less than 10 days' notice.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following States has the largest number of Rajya Sabha seats? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) West Bengal
West Bengal has 16 Rajya Sabha seats. Andhra Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh have 11 seats and Rajasthan has 10 seats.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one among the following political parties came second in terms of seats won in the first Lok Sabha election in India? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(B) Communist Party of India
In the first Lok Sabha elections, the India National Congress won with 364 seats. The Communist Party of India stood second with 16 seats. Socialist party - 12 seats, Kisan Mazdoor Praja Party - 9 seats.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following States has less than two Lok Sabha seats? [CISF AC 2023]
solution:
(D) Mizoram
Lok Sabha seats for each of the states mentioned: Arunachal Pradesh - 2 seats, Goa - 2 seats, Meghalaya - 2 seats, Mizoram - 1 seat. Article 81 deals with the Composition of Lok Sabha according to which its maximum strength shall be 552 members. (By the 104th CA, 2019, 2 seats reserved for anglo Indians were removed). The seats are allocated to states based on population.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following of States of India does not have a Legislative Council? [CISF AC 2024]
solution:
(B) West Bengal
As of now, only 6 states have legislative councils. (Trick - KABUTM) They are - Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Telangana, and Maharashtra. Article 169 - Creation and abolition of Legislative Councils, Article 171 - Composition of Legislative Councils. The minimum strength of LC is 40 members while the maximum strength could be 1/3rd of the total strength of the State Legislative Assembly.

subject: Polity
question_text: If any question arises as to whether a Member of the Parliament has become subject to any of the disqualifications mentioned in Article 102(1), who among the following is empowered to take the final decision? [CISF AC 2024]

options.c: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

solution:
(A) The President of India
Under Article 103, decision on questions as to disqualifications is taken by the President after taking the opinion of the ECI which is binding in nature. Article 191 deals with disqualification of members at the state level. Article 192 empowers the Governor to take the decision on questions as to disqualifications after taking ECI's opinion which is final.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which part of independent India had held the first election based on universal adult franchise? [GEOSCIENTIST 2023]
solution:
(B) Manipur
Manipur was the first part of independent India to hold elections based on universal adult franchise in June 1948. This was before India's first general elections (1951-52).

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:
1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix.
2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party.
3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as of the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings.
4. The well established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister.
Which of the statements given above are correct? [IAS 2022]
solution:
(A) 1 and 3 only
1) As per Article 118 dealing with Rules of procedure of Parliament, the election of Deputy Speaker is held on a date fixed by the Speaker. 2) It is not mandatory but a convention that the election of Deputy Speaker shall be from either the ruling party or the opposition party. 3) Article 95(1) states that the Deputy Speaker performs the duties of the Speaker when the Speaker is absent and enjoys the same powers. 4) The motion is moved by a member of the House, usually from the ruling party, and put to vote.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha?
1. To ratify the declaration of Emergency.
2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers.
3. To impeach the President of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [IAS 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
The declaration of National Emergency (Article 352), President's Rule (Article 356), and Financial Emergency (Article 360) must be approved by both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha. The impeachment of the President (Article 61) requires both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha to participate in the process. It is not an exclusive power of the Lok Sabha. The impeachment procedure is taken from the US Constitution.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to Finance Bill and Money Bill in the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:
1. When the Lok Sabha transmits Finance Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it can amend or reject the Bill.
2. When the Lok Sabha transmits Money Bill to the Rajya Sabha, it cannot amend or reject the Bill, it can only make recommendations.
3. In the case of disagreement between the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha, there is no joint sitting for Money Bill, but a joint sitting becomes necessary for Finance Bill.
How many of the above statements are correct? [IAS 2023]
solution:
(B) Only two
Only statements 1 and 2 are correct. Article 117 - Provisions as to Finance Bills. A Financial Bill Category 1 [Article 117(1)] can only be introduced in Lok Sabha while the Financial Bill Category 2 [Article 117(3)] can be introduced in either House of Parliament. When it is transmitted to the other House, it can be amended or rejected. Joint sitting is possible in case of a Finance Bill but it is not necessary.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:
1. Prorogation of a House by the President of India does not require the advice of the Council of Ministers.
2. Prorogation of a House is generally done after the House is adjourned sine die but there is no bar to the President of India prorogating the House which is in session.
3. Dissolution of the Lok Sabha is done by the President of India who, save in exceptional circumstances, does so on the advice of the Council of Ministers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(C) 2 and 3 only
Article 85 deals with the sessions of Parliament, prorogation and dissolution as per which the President prorogues a House on the advice of the Council of Ministers. Prorogation usually happens after adjournment sine die, however there is no constitutional restriction against this. Article 86 - Right of President to address and send messages to Houses, Article 87 - Special address by the President, Article 28 - Rights of Ministers and Attorney Generals as respects Houses that a Minister has the right to speak in LS / RS but can only vote in the House that they belong to. The Attorney General is not a member of either House but has the right to speak in both the Houses.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which statements are correct of the following statements in respect of a Money Bill in the Parliament?
1. Article 109 mentions special procedure in respect of Money Bills.
2. A Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Council of States.
3. The Rajya Sabha can either approve the Bill or suggest changes but cannot reject.
4. Amendments to a Money Bill suggested by the Rajya Sabha have to be accepted by the Lok Sabha.
Select the answer using the code given below [IAS 2024]
solution:
(C) 1, 2 and 3
Article 109 mentions special procedures in respect of Money Bills. It states that a Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Rajya Sabha. The Rajya Sabha can either approve or suggest changes within a period of 14 days but cannot reject the Bill. The Lok Sabha may or may not accept the recommendations. If no action is taken within 14 days, the Bill shall be deemed to have been passed by the Rajya Sabha.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to Union Budget, consider the following statements:
1. The Union Finance Minister on behalf of the Prime Minister lays the Annual Financial Statement before both the Houses of Parliament.
2. At the Union level, no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(B) 2 only
As per Article 112, the Annual Financial Statement is presented by the Finance Minister on behalf of the President, not the Prime Minister. Under Article 113 (Procedure in Parliament with respect to estimates), no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:
While any resolution for the removal of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is under consideration
1. He/She shall not preside.
2. He/She shall not have the right to speak.
3. He/She shall not be entitled to vote on the resolution in the first instance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(A) 1 only
Under Article 96, when a resolution for the removal of the Speaker or Deputy Speaker is under consideration, they cannot preside over the session. However, they retain the right to participate in the proceedings and vote in the first instance, but they do not have a casting vote in case of a tie.

subject: Polity
question_text:
With reference to the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements:
1. A bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses on its dissolution.
2. A bill passed by the Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
3. A bill in regard to which the President of India notified his/her intention to summon the Houses to a joint sitting lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(B) 1 and 2
Article 107 - Provisions as to introduction and passing of Bills. As per Article 107 (5) - A Bill which is pending in Lok Sabha or which having been passed by Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha shall lapse on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha. If the President has already notified a joint sitting under Article 108, the Bill does not lapse even after the Lok Sabha is dissolved.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many Delimitation Commissions have been constituted by the Government of India till December 2023? [IAS 2024]
solution:
(D) Four
Four commissions were set up under the Delimitation Acts of 1952, 1962, 1972 and 2002 to redraw the boundaries of Parliamentary and Assembly constituencies based on the Census data. The 42nd CA froze delimitation based on the 1971 Census until 2001 to control population growth. The 84th CA extended the freeze on delimitation until 2026. Article 82 - Readjustment after each census.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which descriptions about Parliamentary Privilege for MPs in India are correct?
1. The Speaker can refer suo moto any privilege question for investigation.
2. A privilege question has priority over other business.
3. Privilege includes freedom from preventive arrest.
Code: [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(C) 1 and 2 only
When parliamentary privileges of the Parliament or its members are breached, the presiding officer of each House is authorized to refer the matter to the Committee of Privileges for examination. Such breach of privilege issues take precedence over the House's routine business. Notably, while members have certain protections including immunity from arrest in civil cases during sessions, this immunity does not extend to preventive detention or criminal arrests.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which state has the highest seats in the Rajya Sabha? [CDS I 2025]
solution:
(D) Tamil Nadu
Tamil Nadu has the highest number of Rajya Sabha seats with 18 seats, followed by Bihar with 16 seats, and then by both Madhya Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh with 11 seats each. Uttar Pradesh holds the highest number of Rajya Sabha seats (31) nationally.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which statement about an adjournment motion is not correct? [CDS I 2025]

options.a: It draws attention to urgent public matters.
options.c: It can be invoked for state government matters.
options.d: The Chair has absolute discretion to refuse it.

solution:
(C) It can be invoked for state government matters.
An adjournment motion, exclusive to the Lok Sabha, serves to highlight a definite matter of urgent public importance, thereby interrupting regular proceedings. This motion must concern a subject under Central Government purview and is subject to the Speaker's approval. The Rajya Sabha does not permit adjournment motions, however, Rule 267 can be utilized for similar discussions on urgent matters.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Parliament of India is correct? [NDA I 2025]

options.a: A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote.
options.b: The Deputy Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha is nominated by the President from among the members of the Rajya Sabha.
options.c: The Lok Sabha cannot be dissolved before the completion of its five year term.
options.d: The members of Lok Sabha also vote in the election of the members of Rajya Sabha.

solution:
(A) A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote.
The Rajya Sabha (Article 80) has a maximum of 250 members: 12 nominated by the President and 238 elected by states/UTs via single transferable vote. Eligibility includes Indian citizenship, a minimum age of 30 years, and other parliamentary criteria. The Deputy Chairperson (Article 89) is elected internally by the Rajya Sabha members.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions abolition or creation of Legislative Council in States? [SSC GD 26/02/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 169
Article 169 of the Indian Constitution allows Parliament to create or abolish a Legislative Council in a state if the state's Legislative Assembly passes a resolution to that effect. The resolution must be supported by a majority of the total membership and not less than two-thirds of the members present and voting (special majority) and then it is to be passed in the Parliament with a simple majority. Article 161 - Power of Governor to grant pardons, Article 165 - Advocate-General for the state, Article 173 - Qualification for membership of the State Legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Chairman of the Legislative Council is: [SSC CHSL 01/06/2022]

options.a: elected by the Chief Minister and their Cabinet
options.b: appointed by the Chief Minister of that state
options.c: elected by the members of the Legislative Council
options.d: appointed by the Governor of that state

solution:
(C) Elected by the members of the Legislative Council
Article 182 states that the Legislative Council of a state shall choose two members to be its Chairman and Deputy Chairman, respectively. Article 183 - Vacation, resignation and removal of the Chairman and Deputy Chairman. They are removed from their office by simple majority. The Chairman resigns to the Deputy Chairman and the Deputy Chairman resigns to the Chairman.

subject: Polity
question_text: The state legislative assembly has a strength of ______, with exceptions for smaller states. [SSC MTS 06/07/2022]
solution:
(B) 60 to 500
Article 170 which deals with the composition of the Legislative Assemblies provides for its strength to be 60 to 500 members with exceptions like: Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim, Goa with a strength of 30 members. Mizoram has 40 members and Nagaland has 46 members. Also, the Legislative Council shall consist of not more than one-third of the total strength of the Legislative Assembly and not less than 40 members as per Article 171.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Upper House of a State Legislature is known as the ______. [SSC CGL 18/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Vidhan Parishad
The Vidhan Parishad is the Upper House while the Vidhan Sabha is the Lower House of a state legislature. The Vidhan Parishad is a permanent body and is not subject to dissolution. Members are elected indirectly serving a 6-year term, with one-third retiring every two years (similar to Rajya Sabha) while Legislative Assemblies, unless sooner dissolved, serve a tenure of 5 years (similar to Lok Sabha) as mentioned in Article 172 which deals with the duration of State Legislatures.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which part of the Indian Constitution deals with the organisation, composition and powers of the state legislature? [SSC CGL 30/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Part VI
Part VI deals with The States and contains 6 Chapters. Chapter I contains Article 152 which defines the State. Chapter II - The Executive, Chapter III - the State Legislature, Chapter IV - Legislative power of the Governor (Article 213), Chapter V - High Courts, Chapter VI - Subordinate Courts.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles and Parts of the Indian Constitution deal with the State Legislature? [SSC CGL 26/09/2024]
solution:
(C) Articles 168 to 212 in Part VI
Article 168 provides for the constitution of legislatures in states. All states and 2 UTs (Delhi and Puducherry) have a Legislative Assembly. While a state may or may not have a Legislative Council. The Legislative Assembly holds more power than the Legislative Council - it can pass money bills, control the executive, and has the final say on ordinary bills. The Council can only delay or suggest changes but cannot reject bills or hold executive accountable.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions the qualification for membership of the State Legislature? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 173
Article 173 - Qualification for membership of the State Legislatures states that a member shall be a citizen of India, shall not hold any office of profit, minimum age for Legislative Assembly shall be 25 years and for the Legislative Council, it shall be 30 years. Article 179 - Vacation, resignation and removal of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker, Article 180 - Power of the Deputy Speaker to perform the duties of the Speaker, Article 181 - Speaker or Deputy Speaker not to preside while a resolution for his removal from office is under consideration.

subject: Polity
question_text: Among the following, which is not a qualification for membership in the Vidhan Sabha? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]

options.a: Have attained the age of 25 Years
options.b: Not hold any office of profit

solution:
(C) Knowledge of Politics
Knowledge of politics is not a constitutional or legal requirement. For explanation, refer to question 7.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who elects the Presiding Officer (the speaker) in the State legislature? [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Member of Vidhan Sabha
The Speaker of the Vidhan Sabha is elected by the members of the Vidhan Sabha from among themselves as per Article 178. The Speaker presides over the sessions of the Legislative Assembly while the members of the Vidhan Parishad choose among themselves the Chairman and the Deputy Chairman of the Legislative Council as mentioned in Article 182.

subject: Polity
question_text: In India, subject to the provisions of article 333, the Legislative Assembly of each State shall consist of not more than______ members. [SSC GD 20/02/2024]
solution:
(C) Five Hundred
As per Article 170, the Legislative Assembly shall consist of not more than 500 members and not less than 60 members. Article 333 earlier allowed the Governor to nominate one member from Anglo-Indian community though this provision was abolished by the 104th CA 2019.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct in the context of the Indian Constitution? [SSC CPO 29/06/2024]
1. There are three major organs of the government, namely Legislature, Executive and Judiciary.
2. A separation of functions rather than powers is followed.
3. All organs of the government are independent.
solution:
(A) 1 and 2 only
India follows a separation of functions rather than a separation of powers. While each organ has specific roles, they often interact and overlap like the judicial review. Also, the organs are interdependent as the Executive is drawn from the Legislature and the Courts rule out their judgements based on the laws made by the Legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: One-third of the members of the Vidhan Parishad retire every ______ years. [SSC CHSL 06/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Two
Article 172 which deals with the duration of State Legislatures states that the Legislative Council shall not be subject to dissolution and nearly one-third of the members shall retire every second year which makes the tenure of an MLC to be of 6 years.

subject: Polity
question_text: In India, the total number of members in the Legislative Council of a State having such a Council shall not exceed ______ of the total number of members in the Legislative Assembly of that State. [SSC GD 28/02/2024]
solution:
(C) One-third
The Legislative Council shall consist of not more than one-third of the total strength of the Legislative Assembly and not less than 40 members as per Article 171. However, the actual strength is determined by the Parliament. It also mentions the composition of a Legislative Council as - (1) 1/3rd members are elected by MLAs, (2) 1/3rd elected by local bodies, (3) 1/12th elected by graduates of 3 years, (4) 1/12th elected by teachers, (5) 1/6th nominated by the Governor in matters relating to literature, science, art, social service and cooperative societies.

subject: Polity
question_text: In the state legislative assembly, the Money bill can be introduced with the prior permission of ______. [SSC CGL 12/09/2024]
solution:
(D) Governor
In a state Legislative Assembly, a Money Bill can only be introduced with the prior recommendation of the Governor, as per Article 198. Also, a Money Bill cannot be introduced in Vidhan Parishad and it is the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly who decides whether a bill is a Money Bill or not.

subject: Polity
question_text: As per Article 172 (2) ______ of a State shall NOT be subject to dissolution. [SSC CGL 19/07/2023]
solution:
(B) Legislative Council
Article 172 (Duration of State Legislatures) states that the Legislative Council / Vidhan Parishad is a permanent body while the Legislative Assembly, unless dissolved earlier, serves a term of 5 years. Article 83 mentions the same for the Rajya Sabha and the Lok Sabha. For further explanation, refer to question 10.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India provides that there shall be a Legislative Assembly for the National Capital Territory of Delhi? [SSC CGL 27/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Article 239AA
Article 239AA of the Indian Constitution provides for the Legislative Assembly and Council of Ministers for the National Capital Territory (NCT) of Delhi. It was inserted by the 69th CA, 1991, which gave Delhi a special status and created a legislative framework for governance. Also the 91st CA, 2003 limits the size of the CoMs in Delhi to 10% of the total strength of the legislative assembly.

subject: Polity
question_text: In India within the legislative branch are the ______ houses of Parliament? [SSC GD 29/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Two
India has a bicameral legislature with Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha at the central level and the Legislative Assembly and Legislative Councils at the state level. However, only 6 states have a Legislative Council as of now. They are Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Telangana and Maharashtra. (Trick - KABUTM).

subject: Polity
question_text: In which of the following forms of government does the executive enjoy the right to dissolve the Legislature? [SSC JE 05/06/2024]
solution:
(C) Parliamentary form of government
The Prime Minister can advise the President to dissolve the Parliament, leading to general elections. Article 75(3) states that the CoMs is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the term of the State legislative assembly in India as per Indian constitution? [SSC MTS 14/06/2023]
solution:
(B) 5 years
The normal term of a State Legislative Assembly is 5 years. It can be dissolved earlier or extended during a national emergency (by Parliament) for one year at a time, but not beyond six months after the emergency ends.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Indian Constitution, what is the tenure of the members of the State Legislative Council in India? [SSC MTS 14/07/2022]
solution:
(D) 6 years
Not all Indian states have an Legislative Council. It's optional and created/abolished under Article 169 of the Constitution. For further information, refer to question 12 and 13.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who has the power to abolish or create a legislative council in the state? [SSC MTS 18/07/2022]

options.b: High court of the concerned State
options.d: Chief minister of the concerned state

solution:
(A) Parliament
As per Article 169, Parliament has the power to create or abolish a Legislative Council in a state. This can only happen if the Legislative Assembly of that state passes a resolution (by a special majority) recommending such creation or abolition. Article 163 - Council of Ministers to aid and advise the Governor, Article 164 - Appointment of Chief Minister and other Ministers. The 91st CA sets the maximum limit at 15% of the total members of the Legislative Assembly and the minimum at 12 ministers. Article 214 - Establishment of High Courts.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following Constitutional Amendment Act of the Indian constitution reduced the age of voting from 21 to 18 for elections to the Lok Sabha and to the Legislative Assemblies of states? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 61st
The 61st CA, 1988 reduced the age of voting from 21 to 18 for elections to the Lok Sabha and to the Legislative Assemblies. In 1989, the Election Commission of India became a multi-member body consisting of a Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners. Article 324 - Superintendence, direction and control of elections to be vested in an Election Commission.

subject: Polity
question_text: In India, during a meeting of the Legislative Assembly or Council, if there is no quorum, it shall be the duty of the ______ to adjourn the House or suspend the meeting. [SSC CHSL 27/06/2023]
solution:
(B) Speaker/Chairman
Article 189 - Voting in Houses, power of Houses to act notwithstanding vacancies and quorum. It states that a minimum one-tenth of the total members must be present to hold a meeting. If quorum is not met, the meeting must be adjourned or suspended by the Speaker / Chairman. It also mentions that the Speaker / Chairman does not vote in the first instance, but exercises a casting vote in case of a tie.

subject: Polity
question_text: Who will cause every year, annual financial statements to be laid before the Legislative Assembly of the State? [SSC STENOGRAPHER 13/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Governor
As per Article 202 (Annual Financial Statement), the Governor presents the state's annual budget before the Legislative Assembly each year. The budget shall show expenditure in 3 parts: I) Charged expenditure on the Consolidated Fund of the State (not subject to vote). II) Votable expenditure - other expenditures that need approval of the Assembly. III) Sums required to meet liabilities from previous grants.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which year was the Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Council abolished? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(A) 2019
The Jammu and Kashmir Legislative Council was abolished in 2019 under the J&K Reorganisation Act. It abrogated Article 370 which ended J&K's special status.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions about the right of the Governor to address and to send messages to the House (or Houses) of the state legislature? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 175
Article 175 - Right of Governor to address and send messages to the House or Houses, Article 176 - Special Address by the Governor at the commencement of first session after each general election or the first session of each year, Article 174 - Sessions of the State Legislature, prorogation and dissolution. It empowers the Governor to summon, prorogue, and dissolve the State Legislative Assembly, and ensures that no more than 6 months can pass between two sessions.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian Constitution mentions about the right of the Advocate-General of a state to speak in the State Legislature? [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(D) Article 177
As per Article 177, every Minister and the Advocate-General shall have the right to speak in or take part in the proceedings of both the Houses but no right to vote. Article 165: the Governor appoints the Advocate General, the top legal advisor of the state. He must be qualified to be a High Court judge and holds the office at the Governor's pleasure.

subject: Polity
question_text: According to the Constitution of India, the executive power of the state shall be vested in the ______. [SSC MTS 25/07/2022]
solution:
(B) Governor of the state
Article 153 - There shall be a Governor for each State. Article 154 - The executive power of the state shall be vested in the Governor of the state. Article 155 - The Governor shall be appointed by the President. Article 156 - Term of office of Governor which states that he holds office for 5 years at the pleasure of the President and can resign to the President.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which is NOT correct about the powers of the Chief Minister? [SSC PHASE X 04/08/2022]

options.b: He presides over the meetings of the Council of Ministers.
options.c: When he resigns or dies, it has no effect in the functioning of the council of ministers.
options.d: The Governor appoints ministers on His recommendations.

solution:
(C) When he resigns or dies, it has no effect in the functioning of the council of ministers.
The CoMs is appointed by the Governor on the advice of the Chief Minister. So, when the Chief Minister resigns or dies, the entire Council of Ministers dissolves, as they hold office at the pleasure of the Governor only as long as the CM is in office.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct regarding the State Executive? [SSC CGL 24/07/2023]
A. Article 152 of the Indian Constitution states 'Unless the context otherwise requires, the expression "State" does not include Jammu and Kashmir'.
B. The 7th Constitutional Amendment Act has provided for the appointment of the same person as Governor for two or more states.
C. The Supreme Court clearly mentioned that the office of the Governor is under the control of the Central Government.
solution:
(D) A and B only
Article 152 defines a state. The Supreme Court has held that while the Governor is appointed by the President (Central Government), the Governor is not under his direct control and he is expected to act independently and impartially, especially in constitutional matters.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is NOT correct about the power of Speaker of the state Legislative Assembly? [SSC CPO 10/11/22]

options.a: He maintains order and decorum of the House.
options.b: He decides whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not.
options.c: He calls for a joint session of the Assembly and Council to resolve the deadlock between the two Houses over an ordinary Bill.
options.d: He adjourns the Assembly or suspends the meeting in absence of a quorum.

solution:
(C) He calls for a joint session of the Assembly and Council to resolve the deadlock between the two Houses over an ordinary bill.
As per Article 199, the Speaker decides whether a Bill is a Money Bill or not and also maintains the decorum of the House. There is no provision for a joint sitting in the State Legislature (unlike Parliament under Article 108). Hence, the Speaker cannot call for a joint session. Rule 189- The Speaker can adjourn or suspend the sitting if there is no quorum. Quorum refers to a minimum 10% of total members required to be present.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following states has the highest number of Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) Constituencies? [SSC MTS 13/10/2020]
solution:
(C) Uttar Pradesh
Uttar Pradesh has 403 Legislative Assembly states and 100 Legislative Council states. Maharashtra has 288 LA seats, Rajasthan has 200 seats and Madhya Pradesh has 230 seats.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Governor of an Indian state before entering upon his office takes an oath or affirmation before _______. [SSC CGL 25/07/2021]

options.a: the Chief Justice of the High Court exercising jurisdiction in relation to the State
options.c: the Chief Minister of the concerned state

solution:
(A) The Chief Justice of the High Court
Under Article 159 (Oath or affirmation by the Governor), every Governor shall take an oath in the presence of the Chief Justice of the High Court exercising jurisdiction in relation to the State. Article 157 - Qualifications for appointment as Governor states that he shall be a citizen of India and is not less than 35 years of age. Article 155: The Governor of a State shall be appointed by the President of India.

subject: Polity
question_text: The salary and allowances of leaders of opposition in parliament are governed by the Act passed for the first time by parliament in the year _______. [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(C) 1977
The Salary and Allowances of Leaders of Opposition in Parliament Act was passed in 1977. The Leader of Opposition must be from the largest opposition party and must have at least 10% of the total strength of the House.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of the Indian Constitution mentions Oath or affirmation by the Governor? [SSC GD 27/02/2021]
solution:
(B) Article 159
For explanation, refer to question 33.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution mentions power of Governor to grant pardons, etc; and to suspend, remit or commute sentences in certain cases? [SSC GD 06/03/2021]
solution:
(A) Article 161
Article 161 - Power of Governor to grant pardons and to suspend, remit or commute sentences in certain cases. The Governor can grant pardons, reprieves, respites or remissions of punishment, or to suspend, remit or commute the sentence of any person convicted of any offences under state laws. This is similar to the President's power under Article 72, but the Governor's power is limited to state matters only as it does not include the power to grant pardon in cases of death penalty. Only the President has that power. Article 162 - Extent of executive power of the State, Article 163 - CoM to aid and advise the Governor, Article 164 - Other provisions as to Ministers.

subject: Polity
question_text: As per article 158, the Governor shall NOT be a member of either House of Parliament or of a House of the Legislature of any State specified in ______ Schedule. [SSC CGL 25/09/2021]
solution:
(A) First
The First Schedule lists all the states and UTs of India. Article 158 - Conditions of Governor's office states that the Governor cannot be a member of Parliament or any State Legislature (First Schedule), cannot hold any other office of profit and is entitled to an official residence and receives a salary and allowances which cannot be reduced during their term.

subject: Polity
question_text: A Governor shall, notwithstanding the expiration of his term, continue to hold office until _______. [SSC CPO 03/10/2020]

options.c: he/she has aid and advice of the Chief Minister

solution:
(D) his/her successor enters his/her office
A Governor shall continue to hold office, even after the expiry of their 5-year term, until their successor assumes charge as stated in Article 156. This ensures no vacancy in the office of the Governor.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following articles of Indian Constitution mentions "Special address by the Governor to the State Legislature"? [SSC GD 29/02/2021]
solution:
(A) Article 176
Article 176 deals with the special address by the Governor, while Article 173 - Qualification for membership of the State Legislature.

subject: Polity
question_text: What is the minimum age (in years) a person should be if he/she is willing to be appointed as the Governor of a state in India? [SSC MTS 19/05/2023]
solution:
(C) 35
The minimum age for the appointment of a Governor is 35, which is similar to that of the President and the Vice President.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following is correct regarding Conditions of the Governor's office? [SSC CPO 27/06/2024]
I. The Governor can hold any other office of profit
II. The Governor shall be entitled without payment of rent to the use of his official residences.
solution:
(A) Only II
As per Article 158(2), the Governor shall not hold any other office of profit while under Article 158(3), the Governor is entitled to his official residence without rent.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which of the following states does the Governor have special responsibility under Article 371H of the Constitution with respect to law and order and in discharge of his functions in relation thereto? [SSC CGL 20/08/2021]
solution:
(D) Arunachal Pradesh
Article 371 - Special provisions with respect to the States of Maharashtra and Gujarat. (Trick to learn from 371A to 371J - NAMA AASMA GK). 371A - Nagaland, 371B - Assam, 371C - Manipur, 371D - Andhra Pradesh, 371E - Andhra Pradesh University, 371F - Sikkim, 371G - Mizoram, 371H - Arunachal Pradesh, 371I - Goa, 371J - Karnataka.

subject: Polity
question_text: As per the Constitution of India, what is the maximum number of states for which the same person can be appointed as Governor? [SSC MTS 07/10/21]
solution:
(A) There is no limit
The 7th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1956 allows the same person to be appointed as Governor for two or more states. There is no maximum limit mentioned.

subject: Polity
question_text: The landmark case of D. C. Wadhwa vs. State of Bihar in the Supreme Court is related to which one of the following powers of the Governor? [CAPF 2022]

options.d: To revise the emoluments and allowances of the MLAs

solution:
(A) To repromulgate ordinances
Article 213 empowers the Governor to issue ordinances when the State Legislature (or both Houses) is not in session. These ordinances must be approved by the legislature within 6 weeks of reassembly, or they lapse. The maximum life of an ordinance is 6 months and 6 weeks. The Governor can issue ordinances only on State subjects, and repeated repromulgate without legislative approval was declared unconstitutional in the DC Wadhwa vs. State of Bihar (1987) case.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following States has/have bicameral legislature?
1. Andhra Pradesh
2. Telangana
3. Bihar
4. Uttar Pradesh
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(D) 1, 2, 3 and 4
Article 168 provides for bicameral or unicameral legislature. Article 169 gives Parliament the power to create or abolish a Legislative Council on the recommendation of the State Assembly. As of now, only 6 states have Legislative Councils. They are Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh, Telangana and Maharashtra.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Under Article 191 of the Constitution of India, a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state if the person holds any office of profit under:
1. the Government of India. 2. any State Government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [NDA I 2024]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
Article 191 states that a person shall be disqualified for being an MLA or an MLC of a state if he- (i) holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State, except an office declared by the Legislature not to disqualify its holder, (ii) is of unsound mind, (iii) is an undischarged insolvent, (iv) not a citizen of India, (v) disqualified under any law made by the Parliament. He is subject to defection.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Governor of a State may tender his/her resignation to [CBI 2023]

options.c: the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly
options.d: the Advocate General of the State

solution:
(A) The President of India
As per Article 156(2), the Governor may by writing under his hand, addressed to the President, resign his office. For further explanation, refer to question 28.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Not all Union Territories have Legislative Assemblies.
2. Union Territory of Puducherry has a Legislative Assembly.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CBI 2023]
solution:
(C) Both 1 and 2
India has eight Union Territories, but only two of them have their own Legislative Assemblies - Delhi and Puducherry. The 69th CA granted Delhi the status of NCT with a Legislative Assembly under Article 239AA. Puducherry has a Legislative Assembly through the Government of Union Territories Act, 1963 passed under Article 239A.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following Constitutional Amendment Acts further extended the reservation of seats for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha and the State Assemblies from seventy years to eighty years? [CBI 2023]
solution:
(D) 104th Amendment Act
The 104th CA, 2019 extended reservation for SCs and STs in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies from 70 to 80 years and also ended the reservation for Anglo-Indians. 101st CA, 2016 introduced GST. 102nd CA, 2018 gave constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes under Article 338B. 103rd CA enables 10% EWS reservation in education and jobs.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which report is NOT associated with Fundamental Rights? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(C) Sarkaria Commission Report
The Sarkaria Commission (1983) was set up to review Centre-State relations. On the other hand, the Motilal Nehru Report (1928) was the first Indian attempt to draft a Constitution and proposed Fundamental Rights like equality, freedom of speech and religious freedom. The Tej Bahadur Sapru Committee and JB Kripalani Report also emphasized civil liberties and fundamental rights during the freedom movement. Other important committees on Centre-State relations include the Rajamannar Committee (1969) and the Punchhi Commission (2007).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following commissions recommended that "the appointment of Governor should be non-partisan"? [SSC CGL 09/12/2022]
solution:
(C) Sarkaria Commission
The Sarkaria Commission (1983), set up to review Centre-State relations, recommended that the Governor's appointment should be non-partisan, and the person should be eminent, apolical, and not closely connected to the Centre. Fazal Ali Commission (1953) : Suggested reorganization of states on linguistic basis. Rajamannar Commission (1969): Centre state relations. Mandal Commission (1979) : identifying backward classes and recommended reservations. Also, the appointment of governor is taken from Canada.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Constitution Review Commission chaired by ______ recommended some initiatives to be taken by the government for successful implementation of Fundamental Duties. [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(B) MN Venkatachaliah
It recommended various initiatives to ensure the effective implementation of Fundamental Duties, such as spreading awareness and incorporating them into education.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many members are there in the Committee on Public Undertakings in India? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(A) 22
The Committee on Public Undertakings was established in 1964 on the recommendation of Krishna Menon Committee. It consists of 22 members out of which 15 are from Lok Sabha and 7 from the Rajya Sabha. Chairman : member of Lok Sabha. It examines reports and accounts of Public Undertakings. Earlier, it consisted of 15 members which increased to 22 members in 1974.

subject: Polity
question_text: The parliamentary standing committees have a tenure of ______ years. [SSC CHSL 08/07/2024]
solution:
(B) one
Parliamentary standing committees are permanent in nature. These committees are composed of private members (i.e., MPs who are not ministers) with a tenure of 1 year. They are elected by the respective House from among its members using the principle of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which year was the Estimates Committee constituted for the first time in India? [SSC CGL TIER-II 02/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 1950
The Estimates Committee was constituted in 1950 on the recommendation of John Mathai. It has a strength of 30 members where all the members are from the Lok Sabha. Chairman : Member of Lok Sabha. It examines estimates included in the budget.

subject: Polity
question_text: Members of Public Accounts Committee are elected for a period of: [SSC CHSL 25/05/2022]
solution:
(A) one year
The Public Accounts Committee is a financial standing committee where its members have a tenure of 1 year. It was formed in 1921 under the GOI Act,1919. Strength : 22 members under which 15 are from the Lok Sabha and 7 from the Rajya Sabha. It examines the audit reports of CAG.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following parts of the Indian Constitution deals with Amendment of the Constitution? [SSC GD 27/02/2024]
solution:
(B) Part XX
Constitutional Amendment is dealt under Article 368 of Part XX. The constitution can be amended by three ways : Special Majority, Special Majority with ratification of at least half of the states and Simple Majority (not dealt with as a Constitutional Amendment under Article 368). The CA Bill can be introduced in either House of the Parliament but not in the State Legislature. Both the Minister and the Private Member can introduce such a bill.

subject: Polity
question_text: The 42nd Amendment of the Indian Constitution (1976) states that India is a/an ______ nation. [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) Secular
The 42nd CA, 1976 also called the mini constitution, added the three terms 'secular', 'integrity' and 'socialist' in the Preamble. Then PM - Indira Gandhi.

subject: Polity
question_text: When was the National Emergency declared for the first time under Article 352? [SSC CPO 11/11/2022]
solution:
(D) 1962-68
The first National Emergency (Article 352) was declared in 1962 due to the India-China war. Then President - Dr. Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan. The National Emergency can be revoked anytime by the President or by a resolution passed with simple majority by the Lok Sabha. Provisions of National Emergency are taken from the GOI Act, 1935.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Commission recommended 27% reservation for OBC candidates in all levels of government services? [SSC MTS 07/10/2021]
solution:
(B) Mandal Commission
The Mandal Commission, officially known as the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes Commission, was established in 1979 under the chairmanship of B.P. Mandal. It recommended 27% reservation for Other Backward Classes (OBCs) in government jobs and educational institutions. This recommendation was implemented in the early 1990s by the V.P. Singh government. The first Backward Classes Commission was constituted under the chairmanship of Kaka Kalelkar. It presented its report in 1955. The Indira Sawhney vs Union of India also known as the Mandal Verdict challenged the implementation of the recommendations after which the Supreme Court imposed a ceiling of 50% on total reservations and also introduced the concept of a 'creamy layer' to exclude the well off communities. Sarkaria Commission - centre state relations, Balwant Commission - recommended introduction of Panchayati Raj System, Kothari commission - examined the education system.

subject: Polity
question_text: In 1990, the Dinesh Goswami Committee was formed to make recommendations on: [SSC MTS 20/10/2021]
solution:
(C) electoral reforms
The Dinesh Goswami Committee was formed in 1990 to recommend measures for improving the electoral system in India. Article 324 provides for the Election Commission of India (ECI) to oversee elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and offices of the President and Vice-President. Article 326 - Elections to the House of the People and State Legislative Assemblies to be based on adult suffrage.

subject: Polity
question_text: How many types of the amendment are mentioned by the Indian Constitution as per Article 368? [SSC CPO 05/10/2023]
solution:
(B) Two
As per Article 368 of the Indian Constitution, there are two types of constitutional amendments: (1) By a special majority of Parliament - changes in Fundamental Rights or DPSP. (2) By a special majority with ratification by half of the state legislatures - used for amendments affecting federal provisions like election of President and its manner, Supreme Court and High Court, any list in 7th schedule, representation of states in Parliament, Article 368 itself. Note: There is a third type which is by simple majority (like admission or formation of new states, rules of procedure in Parliament, abolition or creation of Legislative Councils) but it is not deemed to be an amendment under Article 368.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which of the following years did the Constitution (One Hundredth Amendment) Act come into force? [SSC CHSL 04/07/2024]
solution:
(B) 2015
The Constitution (One Hundredth Amendment) Act, 2015 came into force on 1st August, 2015. It dealt with the exchange of certain territories between India and Bangladesh, to implement the Land Boundary Agreement (LBA) signed in 1974 and its 2011 Protocol. Under this, India received 51 Bangladeshi enclaves, and Bangladesh received 111 Indian enclaves.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following is the most controversial Article in the Indian Constitution, which provides for President's rule in any state? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(B) Article 356
Article 356 contains provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in states, also known as President's Rule. For further explanation, refer to question 35 and 41.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Constitution Amendment Act added provisions related to Municipalities in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 74th Amendment Act 1992
The 74th CA, 1992 added Part IX-A to the Constitution, giving constitutional status to Municipalities. It also introduced the Twelfth Schedule, which lists 18 functional items that define the responsibilities of urban local bodies, such as urban planning, water supply, sanitation, and slum improvement. The 97th CA added Part IX-B and Article 19(1)(c) giving constitutional status to Cooperative societies. 69th CA granted special status to Delhi inserting Article 239AA and 239AB. The 71st CA added Konkani, Nepali, and Manipuri to the eighth schedule of the Constitution.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which amendment in the Indian constitution is related to extension of jurisdiction of High Courts to Union territories? [SSC CHSL 16/03/2023]
solution:
(B) 7th Amendment
The 7th CA extended the jurisdiction of High Courts to UTs, provided for the reorganization of states on a linguistic basis and allowed the appointment of the same governor for 2 or more states. As of now, there are 25 High Courts in India some having jurisdiction over more than one state or UT like Kerala HC - Kerala and Lakshadweep, Bombay HC - Maharashtra, Goa, Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu, Calcutta HC - West Bengal, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following principles was added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 11/08/2023]

options.a: The State shall provide free and compulsory education
options.b: To organise agriculture and animal husbandry on modern and scientific lines
options.c: To protect and improve the environment and to safeguard forests and wildlife
options.d: Reservation of SC's and ST's in services

solution:
(C) To protect and improve the environment and to safeguard forests and wildlife
The 42nd CA, 1976 also called the 'mini constitution' added 4 DPSPs: Article 39A - Equal Justice and Free Legal Aid, Article 39(f) - To ensure a safe, dignified, and supportive environment for the growth and development of children and youth, Article 43A - Participation of Workers in Management, Article 48A - Protection and Improvement of Environment and Wildlife.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Constitutional Amendment Acts led to formation of the Goods and Services Tax Council? [SSC CGL 25/09/2024]
solution:
(B) 101st Constitutional Amendment Act
The 101st CA, 2016 introduced the Goods and Services Tax (GST) in India. It added Article 279A, which established the GST Council. It is chaired by the Finance Minister of India. First Chairman - Arun Jaitley. This amendment empowered both the Centre and States to legislate on GST, promoting the idea of "One Nation, One Tax." 100th Amendment Act (2015) : land boundary agreement between India and Bangladesh. 103rd Amendment Act (2019) : Provided 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) in education and government jobs. 102nd Amendment Act (2018) : Granted constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes (Article 338B).

subject: Polity
question_text: The Seventy-First Amendment to the Indian Constitution included Konkani, Manipuri, and Nepali languages in the Schedule. [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(A) Eighth
The eighth schedule deals with official languages. It contains 22 languages. Amendments related to it are: (i) 21st CA : added Sindhi, (ii) 71st CA : added Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali, (iii) 92nd CA : added Bodo, Dogri, Maithili and Santhali. The 96th CA changed the official name of the Oriya language to Odia in the 8th schedule.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements are correct regarding the Supreme Court judgement on validity of 103rd Constitutional Amendment Act? [SSC CGL TIER-2 07/03/2023]
A. The Supreme Court of India's Constitutional Bench for this case consisted of 5 judges.
B. The Constitutional Bench upholds the 10% of EWS reservation with 3-2 majority.
C. As per the Supreme Court Judgement, the 103rd Constitutional Amendment is valid although it is violating the Basic Structure of the Indian Constitution.
solution:
(D) A and B only
The majority held that 103rd CA is constitutional as economic criteria can be a valid basis for reservation. Also, excluding SCs/STs/OBCs from EWS reservation does not violate equality because they already benefit from other quotas. However, the 76th CA, 1994 included the Tamil Nadu Reservation Act of 1994 (which provides for 69% reservation of seats in educational institutions and posts in state services far exceeding the 50% ceiling imposed by the Supreme Court) in the Ninth Schedule to protect it from judicial review.

subject: Polity
question_text: Under which Amendment to the Constitution Act was the reservation for OBCs in educational institutions made? [SSC CHSL 03/08/2022]
solution:
(C) 93rd Amendment
The 93rd CA inserted Article 15(5) in the Constitution. It enables the State to make special provisions for the advancement of socially and educationally backward classes (OBCs), including reservation in educational institutions, including private unaided institutions, except minority institutions.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Amendment to the Constitution Act led to establishment of the National Judicial Appointments Commission in India? [SSC CHSL 04/08/2022]
solution:
(B) 99th Amendment
The 99th CA, 2014 provided for the establishment of the National Judicial Appointments Commission to replace the collegium system for the appointment of judges to the higher judiciary. However, the Supreme Court later struck down the NJAC as unconstitutional in 2015 and restored the collegium system which is headed by the incumbent CJI and comprises the four other senior most judges of the court. 95th Amendment (2009): Extended the reservation of seats for SCs, STs and Anglo-Indians in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies for another ten years (up to 2020). The 96th CA (2011): Substituted "Oriya" with "Odia" in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution. 97th Amendment (2012): Gave constitutional status to co-operative societies adding Part IXB.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Constitutional Amendments incorporated Dadra and Nagar Haveli in the Union of India as a Union Territory? [SSC MTS 26/07/2022]
solution:
(D) 10th
The 10th CA, 1961 incorporated Dadra and Nagar Haveli into the Union of India as a Union Territory. 19th Amendment - Shifted election petition powers to High Court. 14th Amendment - Added Puducherry as a Union Territory. 12th Amendment - Added Goa, Daman & Diu to India. 56th Amendment - conferred statehood on Goa.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Amendments of the Constitution of India declared that the Parliament has the power to abridge or take away any of the Fundamental Rights under Article 368 and such an Act, will NOT be a law under the meaning of Article 13? [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(C) Twenty-fourth Amendment
In Golaknath vs. State of Punjab (1967), the Supreme Court ruled that Parliament could not amend Fundamental Rights, as constitutional amendments were considered "law" under Article 13. To override this, the 24th Amendment Act, 1971 was enacted, explicitly stating that Parliament has the power to amend any part of the Constitution, including Fundamental Rights, and such amendments would not be considered 'law' under Article 13. Later, in Kesavananda Bharati case, the Supreme Court upheld the 24th Amendment but introduced the Basic Structure Doctrine, limiting Parliament's amending powers.

subject: Polity
question_text: The Eighty-Ninth Amendment Act, 2003 of the Indian Constitution established which of the following Commission? [SSC CHSL 17/03/2023]
solution:
(D) National Commission for Scheduled Tribes
The 89th CA bifurcated the National Commission for SCs and STs into two separate bodies and established the National Commission for STs under Article 338A and National Commission for SCs under Article 338. The composition of NCSC and NCST is (Chairman + Vice Chairman + 3 other members). Competition Commission of India (CCI) : Established under the Competition Act, 2002, not by a constitutional amendment. National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC): (Article 338B) Became a constitutional body through the 102nd CA, 2018. National Commission for Women (NCW): Established in 1992 by an Act of Parliament, not through a constitutional amendment.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which among the following constitutional amendments of the Indian Constitution deals with the formation of Sikkim as a state? [SSC CHSL 20/03/2023]
solution:
(A) 36th
The 36th CA made Sikkim a full fledged state of India while the 35th CA granted Sikkim the status of an associate state. Article 371F grants special status to Sikkim.

subject: Polity
who_among_the_following_presidents_of_in: Who among the following Presidents of India gave assent to the 100th Amendment of the Constitution of India? [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(A) Pranab Mukherjee
The 100th Amendment Act, 2015 dealt with the land boundary agreement between India and Bangladesh. It was signed into law by President Pranab Mukherjee on 28th May 2015. Tenures of the President were - APJ Abdul Kalam : 2002 - 2007, Pratibha Devisingh Patil : 2007 - 2012, Pranab Mukherjee : 2012 - 2017, Ram Nath Kovind : 2017 - 2022.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Amendments of the Constitution of India gave the status of state to Goa? [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(B) 56th Amendment
The 56th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1987 granted statehood to Goa, making it the 25th state of India establishing a Legislative Assembly for it. 52nd Amendment (1985) : Introduced the Anti-Defection Law by adding the Tenth Schedule. Mizoram and Arunachal Pradesh too were created in 1987.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Amendment Acts of the Constitution of India abolished the privy purses and privileges of former rulers of princely states? [SSC CGL 20/04/2022]
solution:
(B) 26th Amendment Act 1971
The 26th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1971 abolished privy purses and privileges of the former rulers of princely states. 28th CA, 1972: Abolished special privileges and service conditions of members of the Indian Civil Services under Article 314. The 27th CA, 1971: Reorganized the North-Eastern areas, providing for Union Territories of Mizoram and Arunachal Pradesh.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which year did the Government of India pass the Disabilities Act? [SSC MTS 18/10/2021]
solution:
(A) 1995
Person with disability act was passed in 1995, it was replaced by right of persons with disabilities in 2016.

subject: Polity
question_text: 73rd Amendment Act 1992 makes provisions for a 3-tier system of Panchayati Raj for all the states having population of above: [SSC PHASE XII 26/06/2024]
solution:
(C) 20 lakh
The 3 tiers are - Gram Panchayat / Village level, Panchayat Samiti / block level / intermediate level, Zila Parishad / District level. It was recommended by the Balwant Rai Mehta Committee. Lord Ripon is regarded as the father of Local Self Government. This 3 tier system is not mandatory in every state. A state with population less than 20 lakh runs without the intermediate level. Part IX under Article 243-243O deals with the Panchayats. The 73rd CA added the 11th schedule which contains 29 subjects and enforced the Panchayats on 24th April, 1993.

subject: Polity
question_text: An amendment to the Hindu Succession Act, 1956 was passed in the year _______. [SSC CGL 12/04/2022]
solution:
(B) 2005
The Hindu Succession Act, 1956 governs the inheritance and succession of property among Hindus. It applies to Hindus, Buddhists, Jains and Sikhs. The amendment passed in 2005 granted equal rights to daughters in ancestral property, just like sons signifying gender equality.

subject: Polity
question_text: The 60th Amendment to the Constitution of India increased the ceiling of profession tax from Rs. 250 p.a. to _______ p.a. [SSC CGL 13/04/2022]
solution:
(D) Rs. 2,500
The 60th CA, 1988 increased the ceiling of profession tax from Rs. 250 per annum to Rs. 2,500 per annum. This tax is levied by state governments on professions, trades, callings, and employments.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following sets of articles is related with the feature of Emergency Provisions in the Indian Constitution? [SSC CHSL 09/08/2023]
solution:
(A) 352 to 360
Emergency provisions are contained in Part XVIII from Article 352 to 360. (1) National Emergency (Article 352) - The resolution to impose emergency shall be passed by a special majority by both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha within 1 month from the date of issue. If approved by the President, it continues for 6 months and can further be extended by the same procedure for an indefinite period. (2) President's Rule (Article 356) - It empowers the President to suspend a state government's rule if it fails to act in accordance with the constitutional provisions or comply with any direction from the centre (Article 365). It needs to be approved by the Parliament by simple majority within 2 months of the date of issue. If approved, it continues for 6 months and can be extended for a maximum period of 3 years. (3) Article 360 - Financial emergency. It has never been imposed in India.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution CANNOT be suspended even during an emergency declared on the grounds of war or external aggression? [SSC CGL 19/09/2024]
solution:
(A) Articles 20 and 21
Provisions for suspension of Fundamental Rights are taken from the Weimar Constitution of Germany. Grounds for National Emergency: War, external aggression and armed rebellion. Earlier, Internal disturbance was the ground to impose emergency. However, it was changed to 'armed rebellion' by the 44th CA, 1978. Articles 20 and 21 can never be suspended. Also, rights under Article 19 cannot be suspended when National Emergency is imposed on grounds of armed rebellion.

subject: Polity
question_text: On the night of _______ 1975, Indira Gandhi recommended the imposition of Emergency to President Fakhruddin Ali Ahmed. [SSC CPO 23/11/2020]
solution:
(C) 25th June
On June 25, 1975, Prime Minister Indira Gandhi imposed a National Emergency citing "internal disturbance". In July 2024, this date was officially declared as the 'Samvidhan Hatya Diwas'. National Emergency in India has been imposed thrice - 1962 (China war), 1971 (Pakistan War), 1975 (Internal disturbance).

subject: Polity
question_text: During a National Emergency, the term of Lok Sabha can be extended for: [SSC PHASE XII 20/06/2024]
solution:
(C) one year at a time
During a National Emergency in India, the term of the Lok Sabha can be extended by Parliament indefinitely but only for one year at a time, and not extending beyond six months after the emergency has ceased to operate. In 38th CA, National Emergency was taken out of the scope of judicial review which was later overruled in the Minerva Mill Case.

subject: Polity
question_text: In case the President is satisfied with the Governor's report, he may declare President's Rule under Article 356 but such proclamation is initially for a period of _______. [SSC CPO 28/06/2024]
solution:
(B) six months
In the President's Rule (Article 356), if the President is satisfied (usually based on the Governor's report) that the state's constitutional machinery has failed, he can impose President's Rule for an initial period of six months, which can be extended every six months up to a maximum of three years, subject to Parliamentary approval. After this, the CoMs is dismissed and the Legislative Assembly is dissolved. However, there is no effect on Fundamental Rights of the citizens during the President's rule.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which Article of the Indian constitution mentions that it is the duty of the Union government to protect States against external aggression and internal disturbance? [SSC CHSL 09/03/2023]
solution:
(C) 355
Article 355 - It shall be the duty of the Union to protect every State against external aggression and internal disturbance and to ensure that the government of every State is carried on in accordance with the provisions of this Constitution. Other options : Article 353 - Effects of Proclamation of Emergency, Article 356 - President's Rule in States, Article 358 - Suspension of Fundamental Rights under Article 19 during Emergency, Article 359 - Suspension of fundamental rights during emergencies.

subject: Polity
question_text: In 1959, the Central Government of India undertook the governance in Kerala under _______ of the Constitution of India. [SSC CHSL 12/04/2021]
solution:
(A) Article 356
Article 356 (President's Rule) is invoked when there is a failure of constitutional machinery in a state. It needs the approval from Parliament by simple majority within 2 months from the date of issue of the resolution. If approved by the President, the Legislative Assembly of the state gets dissolved. The emergency is initially valid for 6 months, extendable up to 3 years. It can be revoked any time by the President without needing Parliamentary approval.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about Public Accounts Committee is correct? [CAPF 2021]

options.a: The Committee consists of not more than 15 members from the Lok Sabha and 10 members from the Rajya Sabha.
options.b: The Finance Minister is an ex officio member of the Committee.
options.c: A member of the Committee is given a term for one year.
options.d: The term of the office of the members of the Committee from the Rajya Sabha is extended even when they retire from the Rajya Sabha.

solution:
(C) A member of the Committee is given a term for one year.
The members have a term of 1 year. The Chairman is a member of the Lok Sabha appointed by the Speaker and traditionally, the position is given to a senior member of the opposition party. For further explanation, refer to question 7 of Level I.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the Committee on Public Undertakings is/are correct?
1. There are more members from the Rajya Sabha than the Lok Sabha in the Committee.
2. The Chairperson of the Committee is appointed by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha.
Select the correct answer using the code given below. [CAPF 2022]
solution:
(B) 2 only
There are 22 members out of which 15 are from the Lok Sabha and 7 are from the Rajya Sabha. The Chairperson is a member of the Lok Sabha appointed by the Speaker. For further explanation, refer to question 4 of level I.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements about the Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha are correct?
1. Initially it was an ad-hoc Committee.
2. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint relating to unethical conduct of a member of the Lok Sabha.
3. This Committee cannot take up any matter which is sub-judice.
Select the answer using the code given below: [IAS 2024]
solution:
(C) 1 and 3 only
The Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha was first constituted in 2000 as an ad-hoc committee. It became a permanent committee in 2015. Each House of Parliament has its own separate Ethics Committee. Also, any person can make a complaint against a Lok Sabha member, not just MPs and it does not take up matters that are sub-judice.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not a circumstance for proclamation of Emergency by the President of India under Article 352 of the Constitution of India? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) Internal disturbance
Originally, the term "internal disturbance" was also included. However, it was replaced by "armed rebellion" through the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1978, to prevent misuse of emergency powers.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding constitutional amendment is/are correct?
1. The procedure for amendment to the Constitution is provided in Article 368.
2. A Bill to amend the Constitution can be introduced in either House of the Parliament.
3. The special procedure in Article 368 vests constituent powers upon the ordinary legislation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(D) 1 and 2 only
The Constitutional Amendment Bill can be introduced either by a minister or by a private member and does not require prior permission from the President. It can be introduced in either House of the Parliament (LS/RS) but not in the State Legislature. Each House must pass the Bill separately by a special majority. In case of disagreement, there is no provision for joint sitting. The President must give his assent to the Bill and cannot exercise his veto power. KC Wheare said that the Constitutional Amendment of India strikes a good balance between rigidity and flexibility.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following amendments in the Constitution of India made a Proclamation of Emergency immune from judicial review? [CDS II 2021]
solution:
(C) 42nd Amendment
The 42nd CA, 1976 ("Mini-Constitution") was passed during the Emergency period under Indira Gandhi's government. It made the Proclamation of Emergency under Article 352 immune from judicial review. This was later reversed by the 44th CA. Now, 25th June would be observed as 'Samvidhan Hatya Divas' as on 25 June, 1975 , the then Indira Gandhi government imposed a national emergency that lasted for 21 months on the grounds of Internal disturbance.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about a Bill for Amendment of the Constitution of India is not correct? [CDS I 2022]

options.a: It is governed by Article 368(2) of the Constitution of India.
options.b: Joint sitting can be resorted to for passing a Bill amending the Constitution of India.
options.c: The State Legislatures cannot initiate any Bill or proposal for amendment of the Constitution of India.
options.d: The previous sanction of the President of India is not required for introducing any Bill in the Parliament for amendment of the Constitution of India.

solution:
(B) Joint sitting can be resorted to for passing a Bill amending the Constitution of India.
For explanation, refer to question 46.

subject: Polity
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Committee on Estimates is an ad hoc committee of the Parliament.
2. Committee on Railway Convention is a standing committee of the Parliament.
Select the correct answer using the code given below: [CDS I 2022]
solution:
(D) Neither 1 nor 2
The Estimates Committee is a financial standing committee and is considered a standing committee which is permanent in nature and is reconstituted annually while the Railway Convention Committee is set up from time to time to review matters related to Railways (ad-hoc).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following is not mentioned as a form of Emergency in the Constitution of India? [CDS I 2022]

options.b: State Emergency in terms of Proclamation of President's Rule in a State

solution:
(D) Health Emergency
The Constitution of India mentions three types of emergencies: (1) National Emergency - Under Article 352 (war, external aggression, or armed rebellion), (2) State Emergency (President's Rule) - Under Article 356, (3) Financial Emergency - Under Article 360.

subject: Polity
question_text: In which one of the following States has the President's Rule been imposed most number of times? [CDS II 2022]
solution:
(C) Manipur
State Emergency : 1st time : Punjab (1951) , Maximum Times: Manipur (10 times), UP (9 times).

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following statements about the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) of the Parliament is not correct? [CDS II 2022]

options.a: It examines the Finance Accounts of the Government of India.
options.b: Fifteen members of the Committee are elected by the Lok Sabha from amongst its members.
options.c: The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.
options.d: In case a member of any other Committee constituted by the Government is elected to the PAC, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha decides whether he should continue to be a member of the former Committee.

solution:
(C) The Chairperson of the Committee is elected by its members.
The Public Accounts Committee (PAC) examines the Finance Accounts and the Appropriation Accounts of the Government of India and consists of 22 members - 15 from the Lok Sabha and 7 from the Rajya Sabha. The Chairperson of the PAC is appointed by the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, not elected by the committee members. Also, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha has the authority to decide matters mentioned in option D under parliamentary procedure.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following committees does not have members from the Rajya Sabha? [CDS I 2023]

options.d: The Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes

solution:
(C) The Estimates Committee
The Estimates Committee was formed on recommendation of John Mathai in 1950. It consists of 30 members, all of which are from the Lok Sabha. Rajya Sabha has no representation.

subject: Polity
question_text: Which one of the following amendments to the Constitution of India has introduced reservations in education and in public employment for people from the Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) of society? [CDS II 2023]
solution:
(D) The Constitution (103rd Amendment) Act
The 103rd CA, 2019: Introduced 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) in: Educational institutions (including private unaided ones), public employment. 101st Amendment - Introduced Goods and Services Tax (GST), 102nd Amendment - Gave constitutional status to National Commission for Backward Classes (NCBC).
